In 1635 he issued the revised Buke Shohatto, formally codifying the sankin-kōtai system that required daimyo to reside alternately in Edo while leaving their families as permanent hostages, effectively constraining the political and economic autonomy of domain lords. By 1639 he completed the sakoku policy, severing nearly all foreign contact except for the Dutch trading post at Dejima in Nagasaki and limited Chinese trade. After crushing the Shimabara Rebellion of 1637–1638, he banned Christianity entirely and established the temple-registration (terauke) system to strengthen social control. The bakufu sustained its finances through income from tenryō (shogunal lands), gold and silver mines, and control of major cities, while monitoring the imperial court in Kyoto through the Kyoto Deputy. By 1648 the Tokugawa governing framework was essentially complete, inaugurating nearly two centuries of peace in Japan.

role.zh: 江户幕府第三代将军，锁国体制的确立者
role.en: Third Tokugawa shogun who established the sakoku system

role.zh: 幕府首席老中，镇压岛原之乱的指挥者
role.en: Chief senior councillor and commander of the suppression of the Shimabara Rebellion

ideology.zh: 藩国自主，领地治权独立，对幕府集权的不满与潜在抗衡
ideology.en: Domain autonomy, independent lordship, resentment of and latent resistance to bakufu centralization

headquarters.zh: �的�的鹿儿岛（萨摩）/ 萩（长州）/ 高知（土佐）

relationship.zh: 外样大名是关原合战中站在西军一方或中立的诸藩后裔，被排除在幕府核心权力之外。虽然他们领有广大领地和丰厚石高，但无法出任老中等幕政要职。参勤交代与领地监察对其构成巨大经济负担和政治约束，内心积累了对德川霸权的深层不满。
relationship.en: Tozama daimyo were descendants of domains that had sided with the Western Army or remained neutral at the Battle of Sekigahara, and were excluded from the bakufu's core power structure. Despite holding extensive territories and high kokudaka, they could not serve in senior posts such as rōjū. Sankin-kōtai and domain surveillance imposed heavy economic burdens and political constraints, breeding deep underlying resentment of Tokugawa hegemony.

description.zh: 外样大名是江户幕府政治格局中最重要的潜在异质力量。以萨摩藩岛津氏、长州藩毛利氏、土佐藩山内氏、加贺藩前田氏等为代表，他们虽表面服从幕府权威，但始终保持较强的藩政独立性。萨摩藩实际控制琉球王国并从事对外贸易，拥有独特的武士训练传统和地方文化认同；长州藩在本州西端保持着强大的军事潜力。这些藩国在各自领地内实行相当自主的行政、财政和司法制度，培养了浓厚的地方忠诚意识。1648年时这些藩国尚无公开反抗幕府的举动，但两百年后正是萨摩、长州、土佐等外样藩联合推翻了幕府，完成了明治维新。
description.en: Tozama daimyo represented the most significant latent heterodox force in the Edo political order. Led by houses such as the Shimazu of Satsuma, the Mōri of Chōshū, the Yamauchi of Tosa, and the Maeda of Kaga, they outwardly submitted to bakufu authority while maintaining considerable domain autonomy. Satsuma effectively controlled the Ryukyu Kingdom and engaged in overseas trade, possessing a distinctive warrior training tradition and strong regional cultural identity; Chōshū at the western tip of Honshū maintained formidable military potential. These domains operated substantially independent administrative, fiscal, and judicial systems within their territories, fostering deep local loyalties. In 1648 these domains showed no overt defiance of the bakufu, yet two centuries later it would be precisely the tozama domains of Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa that would overthrow the shogunate and carry out the Meiji Restoration.

role.zh: 日本最大外样藩的藩主，控制琉球贸易
role.en: Lord of Japan's largest tozama domain, controlling the Ryukyu trade

role.zh: 本州西端大藩的藩主，关原西军后裔
role.en: Lord of the major domain at Honshū's western tip, heir to the Western Army legacy

ideology.zh: 皇室正统与文化权威，儒学与和歌传统，象征性至尊地位的维护
ideology.en: Imperial legitimacy and cultural authority, Confucian and waka literary traditions, maintenance of symbolic supremacy

relationship.zh: 天皇与京都公家贵族名义上是日本最高权威的来源，将军的统治正当性在形式上仍需天皇敕任。但在实际政治中，幕府通过�的禁中并公家诸法度严格限制朝廷活动，天皇的权力被局限于礼仪、学术与文化领域，政治上几乎完全受制于幕府。
relationship.en: The emperor and Kyoto court nobility were nominally the source of Japan's highest authority, and the shogun's legitimacy still formally depended on imperial appointment. In practice, however, the bakufu severely restricted court activities through the Kinchū narabini Kuge Shohatto, confining the emperor's role to ceremony, scholarship, and culture, leaving the throne almost entirely subordinate to the bakufu politically.

description.zh: 京都朝廷在江户时代是一个政治权力极度萎缩但文化象征意义极高的存在。后光明天皇（1633—1654）在位期间，朝廷的政治活动被严格限制在幕府划定的范围内——禁中并公家诸法度规定天皇应以学问为业，禁止干预政务。朝廷的年度经费由幕府拨给，公家贵族的婚姻和官位须经幕府同意。然而天皇仍保有几项不可替代的功能：授予将军以征夷大将军称号，主持重大祭祀与年号制定，以及作为和歌、书道、学术的最高庇护者维系日本文化传统。这种「政治无权但文化至尊」的格局一直延续到幕末。正是这份被搁置的正统权威，在两百年后成为倒幕运动「尊王攘夷」旗号的思想根基。
description.en: The Kyoto court in the Edo period was an entity of drastically diminished political power yet immense cultural symbolism. During the reign of Emperor Go-Kōmyō (1633–1654), court political activity was strictly confined within limits set by the bakufu—the Kinchū narabini Kuge Shohatto stipulated that the emperor should devote himself to scholarship and refrain from political intervention. The court's annual budget was allocated by the bakufu, and noble marriages and court appointments required shogunal approval. Yet the emperor retained several irreplaceable functions: conferring the title of Sei-i Taishōgun upon the shogun, presiding over major rituals and era-name promulgation, and serving as supreme patron of waka poetry, calligraphy, and scholarship to sustain Japan's cultural heritage. This pattern of 'political powerlessness but cultural supremacy' persisted until the bakumatsu era. It was precisely this shelved legitimate authority that would become the ideological foundation for the 'revere the emperor, expel the barbarians' rallying cry of the overthrow movement two centuries later.

role.zh: 日本名义最高元首，京都朝廷的象征核心
role.en: Nominal supreme head of Japan and symbolic center of the Kyoto court

role.zh: 公家贵族首席，朝廷日常事务的名义主持者
role.en: Chief of court nobility and nominal presiding figure in daily court affairs

governmentForm.zh: 以儒家官僚制为核心的君主制，国王与文官士大夫共治
governmentForm.en: A Confucian bureaucratic monarchy in which the king ruled in concert with a civilian scholar-official elite

socialStructure.zh: 社会以身分秩序为基础，由两班士族居上，其下为中人、良民平民、贱民与奴婢；宗族、乡约与科举共同维系地方与中央秩序
socialStructure.en: Society was structured by status hierarchy, with the yangban elite at the top, followed by technical middle groups, commoners, and low-status groups including slaves; lineages, local compacts, and the examination system linked local society to the state

rulingClass.zh: 以两班文官集团和中央高官家族为核心的士大夫阶层
rulingClass.en: The scholar-official elite, especially the yangban civil bureaucracy and leading court lineages

succession.zh: 王位原则上传嫡长子，由李氏王室世袭，但须经朝廷确认并受宗法礼制约束
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the House of Yi, ideally passing to the legitimate eldest son, though court confirmation and dynastic ritual norms shaped succession

structure.zh: 1648年的朝鲜仍沿用《经国大典》确立的中央集权体制。国王居于最高统治地位，下设议政府、六曹、三司及诸专门机构。政治运行深受西人与南人等党争影响，但总体官僚框架稳定，地方由八道观察使、府尹、牧使、郡守、县监等管理。
structure.en: In 1648 Joseon still operated under the centralized framework codified in the Gyeongguk daejeon. The king stood at the apex, supported by the State Council, the Six Ministries, the Three Offices, and specialized agencies. Politics was heavily affected by factional competition, especially among Westerners and Southerners, but the overall bureaucratic structure remained stable. Locally, the eight provinces were governed by governors, magistrates, and county officials.

function.zh: 统筹国家政务，协调各曹，向国王进言并审议重大行政事务
function.en: Coordinated major state affairs, supervised ministry-level administration, advised the king, and deliberated important policy matters

function.zh: 掌官员任免、考课、品阶与文官人事管理
function.en: Managed appointments, evaluations, ranks, and personnel affairs of officials

function.zh: 掌户籍、田赋、财政收支、仓储与部分贡纳事务
function.en: Handled household registers, land tax, fiscal revenue and expenditure, granaries, and parts of tribute administration

function.zh: 掌国家礼仪、祭祀、外交文书、学校与科举
function.en: Oversaw state ritual, sacrifices, diplomatic protocol, education, and the civil examinations

function.zh: 掌军籍、兵役、军官任命、部分防务调度与武备事务
function.en: Managed military registers, service obligations, officer appointments, and parts of defense administration and armaments

function.zh: 掌司法审判、刑狱、法令执行与案件覆核
function.en: Oversaw judicial proceedings, penal matters, implementation of laws, and review of cases

function.zh: 掌土木工程、官营手工业、道路桥梁、山林与部分水利
function.en: Managed public works, state craft production, roads and bridges, forests, and some water-control works

function.zh: 监察官员风纪，弹劾贪污失职，维护官僚伦理秩序
function.en: Monitored official conduct, impeached corruption and misconduct, and upheld bureaucratic morality

function.zh: 对国王与政府政策进行谏诤，纠正政治失当
function.en: Remonstrated with the king and government, seeking to correct political errors

function.zh: 掌经筵、文翰、史学讨论与王室顾问事务
function.en: Handled royal lectures, drafting, classical scholarship, and advisory functions for the throne

function.zh: 传达王命，管理奏章与王室日常政务沟通
function.en: Transmitted royal orders and managed memorials and day-to-day communication of the court

function.zh: 记录朝政、编修实录与保存国家史料
function.en: Recorded government affairs, compiled annals, and preserved official historical records

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以八道为骨架，下辖府、牧、郡、县。中央派遣观察使巡察各道，地方守令承担征税、司法、治安、仓储与赈济。乡村层面依托里、坊、面等基层组织及地方乡吏运作，但地方士族与宗族势力对实际治理影响很大。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through the eight provinces, which were subdivided into prefectures, counties, and districts. Provincial governors represented the center, while local magistrates handled taxation, justice, order, granaries, and relief. At the village level, administration relied on basic territorial units and clerks, though local yangban lineages exercised considerable practical influence.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以成文法、王命和礼教并用，核心依据为《经国大典》及其后续补编，并参照《大明律》。司法重视纲常秩序、家族伦理与官僚等级，刑罚包括笞、杖、徒、流、死等，但审理中常受身分差异与政治派系影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined codified law, royal edicts, and Confucian moral norms. Its core rested on the Gyeongguk daejeon and later supplements, with strong influence from the Great Ming Code. Justice emphasized social hierarchy, family ethics, and bureaucratic order; punishments ranged from flogging and beating to penal servitude, exile, and execution, though judgments were often shaped by status and factional politics.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以田赋、贡物、徭役和军役义务为基础。战后财政压力使国家更加依赖大同法在部分地区的推行，以米布等统一征收替代繁杂土贡。1648年大同法尚未全国普及，但已在京畿、江原、忠清、全罗等地区产生显著影响，税制正从实物杂贡向较统一的赋役体系转变。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based on land tax, local tribute, corvée, and military service obligations. Postwar fiscal pressure increased reliance on the Daedong reform in some regions, replacing diverse local tribute items with more standardized payments in rice or cloth. In 1648 the reform had not yet reached the entire kingdom, but it was already significant in Gyeonggi, Gangwon, Chungcheong, and Jeolla, marking a gradual shift from fragmented tribute obligations to a more unified tax regime.

religion.zh: 国家正统为儒教礼制与祖先祭祀，民间则兼有佛教、巫俗与风水信仰。朝廷自称尊崇程朱理学，对佛教保持限制态度，但寺院在山区社会和战后祈福活动中仍具影响。
religion.en: Official orthodoxy centered on Confucian ritual order and ancestral worship, while popular religion also included Buddhism, shamanic practices, and geomancy. The court upheld Neo-Confucianism and kept Buddhism under restriction, yet monasteries still retained influence in mountain communities and postwar devotional life.

philosophy.zh: 主流思想为朱子学，强调名分、礼制、忠孝与修身治国。1648年前后学术讨论也涉及心性论、义理政治与华夷观，尤其在明清鼎革后，朝鲜士林愈发强化"尊周思明"的文化自我定位。
philosophy.en: The dominant philosophy was Zhu Xi Neo-Confucianism, stressing hierarchy, ritual, loyalty, filial piety, and moral self-cultivation as the basis of government. Around 1648 scholarly debate also engaged questions of mind and principle, righteous politics, and the civilized-barbarian distinction; after the Ming-Qing transition, Korean literati increasingly defined Joseon as a guardian of orthodox civilization and Ming cultural legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写主要使用汉文，王朝行政、奏章与学术著述皆以文言为主；训民正音即谚文已广泛存在于民间、女性书写和部分实用文本中，但在上层官僚文化中的地位仍次于汉文。
writingSystem.en: Classical Chinese remained the principal official writing system for administration, memorials, and elite scholarship. Hunminjeongeum, or vernacular Korean script, was widely present in popular use, women's writing, and practical texts, but its status in upper bureaucratic culture was still secondary to literary Chinese.

culturalAchievements.zh: 壬辰与丙子战后，朝鲜继续保持高度发达的史学、儒学经学、木版印刷、地图编纂、白瓷工艺与宗族文集传统。宫廷与士林重视实录、日记、家乘和地方志编修，陶瓷与书画在战后恢复中重建品质。医学、农书与礼书的整理亦体现出重建国家秩序的文化努力。
culturalAchievements.en: After the Imjin and Manchu wars, Joseon maintained highly developed traditions in historiography, Confucian scholarship, woodblock printing, cartography, white porcelain, and lineage compilation. Court and literati circles valued the compilation of annals, diaries, genealogies, and local gazetteers, while ceramics and painting gradually recovered quality in the postwar period. The editing of medical, agricultural, and ritual works also reflected broader efforts to rebuild state order.

languageFamily.zh: 朝鲜语，通常归类为朝鲜语系或语言孤立体
languageFamily.en: Korean, commonly treated as the Koreanic language family or a language isolate

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 朝鲜经济以农业为绝对基础，1648年仍处于战后恢复阶段。总体产出在东亚中属中等规模，远逊于清与日本主要地区，但以人口规模、稻作与旱作并行体系、官仓和地方市场网络支撑了一个稳定王国。GDP估算反映的是以银两折算的总产出，并非近代国民收入统计。
gdpDescription.en: The Joseon economy was overwhelmingly agrarian and in 1648 was still in postwar recovery. Overall output was moderate by East Asian standards, far smaller than the Qing realm and the wealthiest regions of Japan, yet a stable kingdom was sustained by population size, mixed wet-rice and dry-field farming, official granaries, and regional market networks. The GDP estimate is a silver-denominated approximation of total output rather than a modern national-income measure.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、稻米与杂粮种植、棉布与麻布纺织、陶瓷烧造、盐业、渔业、造纸、木版印刷、冶铁与地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, rice and mixed grain cultivation, cotton and hemp textiles, ceramics, salt production, fisheries, papermaking, woodblock printing, ironworking, and local handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 主要流通与贸易品包括米、豆、棉布、麻布、人参、纸张、书籍、陶瓷、海产、木材、铁器及少量药材；对外贸易中尤以人参与皮张、金属、书籍和工艺品较受重视
tradeGoods.en: Major goods in circulation and trade included rice, beans, cotton cloth, hemp cloth, ginseng, paper, books, ceramics, marine products, timber, ironware, and some medicinal materials; in foreign trade, ginseng, hides, metals, books, and crafted goods were especially valued

name.zh: 布币、米谷与杂用铜钱并行
name.en: Cloth money, grain payments, and limited copper cash

unitName.en: bolts of cloth and seok of grain

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。两班家族拥有土地、奴婢、书籍和宗族网络，是财富与地位核心；普通农户多接近自给，常受赋役、歉收和债务压力；战后流民和失地者数量仍不可忽视。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Yangban lineages held land, slaves, books, and lineage networks and formed the core of wealth and status; ordinary peasant households often hovered near subsistence and were vulnerable to taxes, poor harvests, and debt; postwar displaced and land-poor populations remained significant.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 国家控制与地方市场并存的农业经济。土地与赋役制度由国家登记管理，但大量实际分配受地方士族、佃作关系和市场交换影响。官方对外贸易高度受限，主要通过对清朝贡体系、对日通信与釜山倭馆贸易进行管理。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined state supervision with local market exchange in an agrarian framework. Land and tax obligations were registered by the state, but actual distribution was shaped heavily by local elites, tenancy, and market transactions. Foreign trade was tightly regulated and conducted mainly through the tributary relationship with Qing China, diplomatic-commercial contacts with Japan, and trade through the Waegwan at Busan.

source.zh: 贡纳折收与大同法相关收入
官营手工业、盐业与杂项收入
source.en: State handicrafts, salt, and miscellaneous income

source.zh: 军役折纳与户籍相关征收
source.en: Commuted military obligations and household-related collections
Transit dues, market taxes, and other fees

category.en: Court, ritual, and royal household expenses
Public works, postal stations, and administrative miscellany

treasuryDescription.zh: 朝鲜国库并非单纯银库，而是米谷、布帛、铜钱、军需、仓储储备与账面折银并存。1648年财政总体恢复到可维持稳定运作的水平，但仍容易受歉收、疫病、边防压力和党争导致的征收效率低下所冲击。
treasuryDescription.en: The Joseon treasury was not a purely silver reserve but a combination of grain, cloth, copper cash, military supplies, granary stocks, and silver-equivalent accounting. By 1648 public finance had recovered enough for stable operation, but it remained vulnerable to bad harvests, disease, frontier pressure, and reduced collection efficiency caused by factional politics.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在战后恢复、维持军备、稳定仓储与推广较统一的赋役折纳。朝廷倾向于节制宫廷开支、整顿田籍和扩大大同法覆盖，但改革推进受地方既得利益和官僚派系掣肘。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on postwar recovery, maintenance of defense, stabilization of granaries, and wider use of more standardized commuted taxation. The court tended toward restraining court expenses, repairing land registers, and extending the Daedong reform, but reform was constrained by local vested interests and bureaucratic factionalism.

name.zh: 中央禁军与京城守备军

description.zh: 负责王宫、汉城与核心行政区域防务，包括训练都监等战后整编形成的精锐火器部队与宿卫兵力
description.en: Responsible for the defense of the palace, Hanseong, and the core administrative zone, including elite firearm troops and guard units reorganized after the wars

description.zh: 由各道、府、郡、县军额构成，承担城防、治安与紧急征发任务，是战时动员主力
description.en: Made up of registered provincial forces in prefectures and counties, handling fortress defense, local security, and emergency mobilization; they formed the core wartime manpower pool

description.zh: 主要部署于北方与交通要冲，用于警戒边境、传递军报和快速反应，但规模与质量不及北方游牧强国
description.en: Primarily deployed in the north and at strategic routes for frontier watch, dispatch carrying, and rapid response, though smaller and less capable than the cavalry of northern steppe powers

description.zh: 负责沿海巡防、港湾警戒和交通护送。壬辰战争后水军传统仍存，但1648年已较战争高峰期缩减
description.en: Handled coastal patrol, harbor defense, and escort duties. The naval tradition survived from the Imjin War, though by 1648 it was reduced from wartime peak levels

description.zh: 装备火绳枪、佛郎机炮及各类火器，承担守城、营阵火力和训练任务，是战后军事改革的重要成果
description.en: Equipped with matchlocks, light cannon, and other firearms, these troops provided fortress firepower, battlefield support, and training, representing an important result of postwar military reform

commanderInChief.zh: 仁祖国王为名义最高统帅，兵曹、训练都监与诸将领分掌军政军令
commanderInChief.en: King Injo was the nominal commander-in-chief, while the Ministry of War, the Military Training Agency, and senior commanders shared military administration and command

technology.zh: 朝鲜军队处于火器逐渐制度化的阶段，普遍使用火绳枪、弓箭、长枪、刀牌和各式城防火炮。海防与守城技术较成熟，军械制造受明制影响深，但后勤、骑兵与野战协同仍较弱。
technology.en: Joseon's military stood at a stage where firearms were becoming institutionalized. Matchlocks, bows, spears, swords and shields, and various fortress artillery were commonly used. Coastal and fortress defense techniques were relatively mature, and armaments were strongly influenced by Ming models, but logistics, cavalry capability, and open-field coordination remained weaker.

threats.zh: 首要威胁来自满清的宗主压力与北方边境安全，其次是对倭乱重演的长期戒备。虽然1648年对日关系较壬辰战争时期稳定，但沿海防务仍被重视；国内则需防范饥荒、流民与局部叛乱引发的治安风险。
threats.en: The primary threat came from Qing suzerain pressure and the security of the northern frontier, followed by the long-term fear of renewed Japanese aggression. Although relations with Japan were more stable than during the Imjin War, coastal defense remained important; domestically, famine, displaced people, and localized unrest posed additional security risks.

populationDescription.zh: 1648年朝鲜人口约一千万，反映的是战后逐步恢复中的人口规模。分布以农业村落为主，人口密集区集中在汉城周边、忠清、全罗和庆尚等主要农耕地带，北方山区与边地较为稀疏。
populationDescription.en: In 1648 Joseon's population was about ten million, representing a kingdom still recovering demographically after war. Most people lived in agricultural villages, with denser settlement around Hanseong and in the major farming regions of Chungcheong, Jeolla, and Gyeongsang, while the northern mountains and frontier zones were more thinly populated.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体可分为王室与宗亲、两班士族、中人、良民农工商、贱民以及数量可观的奴婢。两班垄断政治文化资本，中人承担技术与行政辅助职能，绝大多数人口为纳税服役的农民。城市手工业者和商人规模有限但在战后市场恢复中愈发重要。
socialClasses.en: Social classes may be broadly divided into the royal house and royal kin, the yangban elite, the technical middle groups, commoner farmers-artisans-merchants, low-status groups, and a substantial slave population. The yangban monopolized political and cultural capital, the middle groups served technical and clerical functions, and most of the population consisted of tax-paying and labor-serving peasants. Urban artisans and merchants remained limited in scale but were growing in importance as markets recovered after war.

allies.zh: 朝鲜在1648年缺乏真正意义上的平等军事盟友。形式上对清称臣纳贡，同时在文化情感上同情南明与残余明室。与日本德川幕府维持经通信使与釜山倭馆贸易支撑的有限和平关系。
allies.en: Joseon had no true equal military allies in 1648. Formally it acknowledged the Qing through tributary submission, while culturally and emotionally many elites sympathized with the Southern Ming and remnant Ming loyalists. Relations with Tokugawa Japan were maintained in a limited but peaceful form through diplomatic missions and trade at the Busan Waegwan.

enemies.zh: 公开敌国并不突出，但朝廷和士林普遍对满清怀有深刻戒心与屈辱记忆；对日本也仍保有壬辰战争以来的战略不信任。
enemies.en: There was no openly active enemy in a formal sense, but the court and literati retained deep mistrust of the Qing shaped by memories of humiliation, and they also preserved strategic distrust toward Japan after the Imjin War.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以求存、守礼与有限接触为主。朝鲜在现实上接受清朝主导的地区秩序，按期朝贡以避免再战；同时谨慎维持对日外交与贸易，以保障沿海稳定和获取所需物资。整体外交风格重视礼仪秩序、边境安定与文化自守。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was oriented toward survival, ritual propriety, and limited engagement. In practical terms Joseon accepted the Qing-led regional order and sent tribute on schedule to avoid renewed war; at the same time it carefully maintained diplomacy and trade with Japan to preserve coastal stability and obtain needed goods. Overall, its diplomacy emphasized ritual order, frontier stability, and cultural self-preservation.

era.zh: 近世早期

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的木版印刷传统、金属活字延续使用、天文历法接受中土新知、火绳枪与火炮制度化、白瓷与窑业技术恢复、改进农书传播及较完善的官修档案与实录编纂体系
keyInnovations.en: A mature woodblock printing tradition, continued use of metal movable type, reception of updated astronomical and calendrical knowledge from the continent, institutionalized matchlock and artillery use, recovery of white porcelain and kiln technology, diffusion of improved agricultural manuals, and a highly developed system of official archives and annals compilation

strengths.zh: 朝鲜1648年的优势在于稳定的儒家官僚体制、较高的文治水平、强韧的地方社会组织、成熟的农业基础以及战后逐步恢复的财政与军备。王朝具有较强的文化认同与制度连续性，即便经历倭乱与清兵入侵后仍能维持统治合法性。
strengths.en: Joseon's strengths in 1648 lay in its stable Confucian bureaucratic order, high level of civil administration, resilient local social organization, mature agrarian base, and gradually recovering finances and defense after war. The dynasty possessed strong cultural identity and institutional continuity, allowing it to preserve legitimacy even after the Japanese invasions and Manchu incursions.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括党争严重、社会身分固化、税役负担不均、地方豪强与土地兼并、对清政治依附下的战略被动，以及军事野战能力相对有限。经济虽稳定但商业化和货币化程度仍受约束，难以快速扩充国家财政能力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included intense factional strife, rigid social hierarchy, uneven tax burdens, local elite domination and land concentration, strategic dependence under Qing political pressure, and relatively limited field military capability. The economy was stable but only modestly commercialized and monetized, making it difficult for the state to expand fiscal capacity rapidly.

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体稳定。只要无大规模外患与连续灾荒，朝鲜能够继续在清主导的东北亚秩序中维持内部安定并推进缓慢改革。但从中长期看，若党争加剧、财政改革停滞、社会流动受阻，则王朝的结构性脆弱性将不断累积。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly stable. So long as there were no major foreign invasions or consecutive disasters, Joseon could maintain internal order within the Qing-dominated Northeast Asian order and continue gradual reform. In the medium to long term, however, structural vulnerabilities would accumulate if factional conflict deepened, fiscal reform stalled, and social mobility remained constrained.

description.zh: 朝鲜在满洲压力与战后重建中维持儒家官僚王朝秩序。
description.en: Joseon maintained its Confucian bureaucratic monarchy amid Manchu pressure and postwar recovery.

governmentForm.zh: 以波斯化宫廷文化为特征的伊斯兰君主制德干苏丹国
governmentForm.en: Islamic Deccan sultanate monarchy characterized by a Persianate court culture

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宫廷贵族、军功封臣、乌理玛与苏菲宗教精英、城市商人与工匠、乡村地主与村社农民构成；国家同时统治穆斯林与占多数的印度教人口，地方社会保留较强的种姓与村社结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of court nobles, military service elites, ulama and Sufi religious figures, urban merchants and artisans, and rural landholders and village cultivators; the state ruled both Muslim communities and a Hindu majority, while local society retained strong caste and village structures.

rulingClass.zh: 阿迪勒·沙希王室、德干派与阿法基派贵族、军事指挥官、持地封臣及高级文官
rulingClass.en: The Adil Shahi royal house, Deccani and Afaqi nobles, military commanders, landed service elites, and senior bureaucrats

succession.zh: 君主世袭，通常由王朝男性成员继承，但实际继承常受宫廷派系、军队与重臣支持所左右
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy, usually passing among male dynastic members, though actual succession was often shaped by court factions, the army, and powerful ministers

structure.zh: 中央政权以苏丹为核心，通过首席大臣、财政官、军务官、司法与宗教官僚及省级总督运作；国家结合宫廷直属财政与地方封授制管理领土。
structure.en: The central government was centered on the Sultan and operated through a chief minister, fiscal officers, military administrators, judicial and religious officials, and provincial governors; the state combined crown-controlled revenues with territorially assigned service grants.

name.zh: 王室枢密与首席大臣府
name.en: Royal Chancery and Office of the Chief Minister

function.zh: 起草诏令、协调大臣、管理王室文书与外交往来
function.en: Drafted royal orders, coordinated ministers, and managed royal correspondence and diplomacy

function.zh: 核定土地收入、征收赋税、审计省库并管理王室地产
function.en: Assessed land revenue, collected taxes, audited provincial treasuries, and managed crown lands

function.zh: 登记军官与骑兵配额、发放军饷、督办火器与要塞防务
function.en: Registered officers and cavalry quotas, disbursed military pay, and supervised firearms and fortress defense

function.zh: 管理卡齐法庭、伊斯兰捐产、宗教学者任命及部分城市纠纷裁判
function.en: Oversaw qazi courts, Islamic endowments, appointments of religious scholars, and adjudication of some urban disputes

name.zh: 宫廷营造与公共工程署
name.en: Court Construction and Public Works Department

function.zh: 负责宫殿、清真寺、陵墓、城墙、水渠与蓄水工程建设
function.en: Handled the construction of palaces, mosques, tombs, walls, canals, and water-storage works

function.zh: 联络地方总督、堡垒长官与城镇税务人员，维持行政控制
function.en: Coordinated with provincial governors, fort commanders, and town revenue officials to maintain administrative control

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省、区、堡垒与村社多层结构运作。省级由总督、军政长官或受封贵族掌管，下设税务官、书记与驻军长官；乡村多依赖传统村长、地方地主与会计人员协助征收与治安。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through multiple layers of provinces, districts, forts, and village communities. Provinces were managed by governors, military-administrative officers, or assigned nobles, beneath whom served revenue officials, clerks, and garrison commanders; villages relied heavily on traditional headmen, local landholders, and accountants for collection and order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为伊斯兰法、苏丹敕令与地方习惯法并行。穆斯林事务多由卡齐依沙里亚审理，土地、村社与跨宗教事务则常依行政裁断与既有地方惯例处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic law, sultanic decrees, and local customary law. Muslim personal and religious matters were commonly judged by qazis under sharia, while land, village, and inter-communal disputes were often resolved through administrative decision and established local practice.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政以土地税为主体，辅以市场税、关卡税、工商业税、港口税与贡赋。部分土地以军役或官职封授形式分配，其受益者负责向中央输送收入、兵员或两者兼具。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal system relied primarily on land revenue, supplemented by market taxes, transit duties, commercial levies, port dues, and tribute. Some territories were assigned as service grants tied to military or official obligations, with holders expected to provide revenue, troops, or both to the center.

religion.zh: 国家宗教为伊斯兰教，宫廷深受什叶与苏菲传统影响，但境内多数居民为印度教徒，宗教生活呈现多元并存；王室赞助清真寺、苏菲圣陵，同时也容纳印度教文人、乐师与工匠。
religion.en: The state religion was Islam, and the court was deeply influenced by Shi'i and Sufi traditions, though most inhabitants were Hindus; religious life was therefore plural. The dynasty patronized mosques and Sufi shrines while also accommodating Hindu literati, musicians, and artisans.

philosophy.zh: 宫廷思想以波斯化伊斯兰政治伦理、神秘主义与德干地区跨宗教文化交流为主，兼受阿拉伯学术传统与南亚地方知识体系影响。
philosophy.en: Court thought was shaped by Persianate Islamic political ethics, mysticism, and the cross-religious cultural exchanges of the Deccan, while also drawing on Arabic scholarly traditions and South Asian local knowledge systems.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与宫廷主要使用波斯文书写阿拉伯字母；阿拉伯文用于宗教学术，达赫尼乌尔都语、卡纳达语与马拉地语亦广泛见于口语、诗歌和地方文书。
writingSystem.en: Administration and court culture primarily used Persian written in the Arabic script; Arabic served religious scholarship, while Dakhni Urdu, Kannada, and Marathi were also widely used in speech, poetry, and local records.

culturalAchievements.zh: 比贾布尔在建筑、音乐与文学方面成就显著。穆罕默德·阿迪勒·沙阿时期修建了著名的"高尔·古姆巴兹"陵墓，其巨型穹顶代表德干建筑高峰；宫廷亦赞助达赫尼文学、波斯诗歌、细密画、金属工艺与宫廷音乐。
culturalAchievements.en: Bijapur achieved distinction in architecture, music, and literature. During the reign of Muhammad Adil Shah, the famous Gol Gumbaz mausoleum was constructed, and its massive dome represented a peak of Deccan architecture; the court also patronized Dakhni literature, Persian poetry, painting, metalwork, and court music.

languageFamily.zh: 宫廷语言属印欧语系伊朗语支与印度-雅利安语支并用环境，地方社会同时广泛使用达罗毗荼语系语言
languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment combined Indo-European Iranian and Indo-Aryan court languages with extensive use of Dravidian languages in wider regional society

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础雄厚，建立在黑土高原农业、棉纺织业、马匹与军需贸易、手工业制造及连接阿拉伯海港口的区域商业网络之上。虽不及莫卧儿帝国总量，但在德干地区仍属最富庶强国之一。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was substantial, resting on black-soil plateau agriculture, cotton textiles, horse and military supply trade, artisanal manufacturing, and regional commercial networks linked to Arabian Sea ports. Though smaller than the Mughal economy, it remained one of the wealthiest major powers in the Deccan.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、棉花与纺织、靛蓝与染色、金属加工、军械制造、石材建筑、马匹贸易、糖与油料加工
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, cotton and textiles, indigo and dyeing, metalworking, arms manufacture, stone construction, horse trade, and sugar and oil processing

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、原棉、靛蓝、谷物、糖、油料、皮革、金属器、军械、宝石、马匹、香料转运品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, raw cotton, indigo, grain, sugar, oilseeds, leather, metal goods, arms, gemstones, horses, and re-exported spices

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人、放贷者、持地军官与宫廷服务家族拥有较高财富，乡村家庭财富差异大，多以耕地、牲畜、粮储和首饰形式保存；干旱年份会显著侵蚀普通农户积蓄。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants, moneylenders, landed officers, and court service families held relatively high wealth, while rural household wealth varied greatly and was often stored in land use rights, livestock, grain reserves, and jewelry; drought years could sharply erode peasant savings.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的农业税收经济与活跃的区域市场经济并存。王室依靠土地财政和军事封授维系统治，同时允许商人、港口中介、手工业者与跨海贸易网络在相对开放环境中运作。
economicSystem.en: A state-dominant agrarian revenue economy coexisted with an active regional market economy. The dynasty relied on land revenue and military service grants to sustain rule, while merchants, port brokers, artisans, and overseas trading networks operated in a comparatively open commercial environment.

source.en: Customs, market dues, and commercial taxes
Tribute and remittances from subordinate territories

source.zh: 王室地产、矿税与杂项收入
source.en: Crown lands, mining dues, and miscellaneous income

category.zh: 宗教捐助与学术文化赞助
外交、赏赐与紧急储备支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况稳健，能够支持宫廷建筑、边境防务与短期军事动员，但若与莫卧儿帝国展开长期大规模战争，仍将承受显著财政压力。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was in sound condition and could support court construction, frontier defense, and short-term mobilization, but a prolonged large-scale war against the Mughal Empire would still impose severe fiscal strain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持稳定征收、保障贵族与军队俸给、修筑都城与纪念性建筑、并在不破坏商业活力的前提下扩大货币收入为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to preserve stable revenue extraction, maintain stipends for nobles and troops, build up the capital and monumental architecture, and expand monetized income without undermining commercial vitality.

description.zh: 骑兵为德干战争核心，由苏丹直属精锐与受封贵族骑队组成，机动作战能力强，适合突袭、追击与野战。
description.en: Cavalry formed the core of Deccan warfare, consisting of royal elite horsemen and contingents maintained by nobles, with strong mobility suited to raids, pursuit, and open battle.

description.zh: 步兵包括火绳枪兵、长矛兵与守城部队，在围城、防御与火器协同中作用突出。
description.en: Infantry included matchlockmen, spearmen, and garrison troops, and was especially important in siege warfare, defense, and firearms support.

description.zh: 拥有重炮、野战炮与攻城器材，工兵负责筑垒、爆破、壕沟与要塞维护；德干诸国一向重视火炮技术。
description.en: This branch included heavy guns, field artillery, and siege equipment, while engineers handled entrenchment, mining, trenches, and fort maintenance; Deccan states had long valued artillery expertise.

name.zh: 要塞守军与地方征召兵

description.zh: 分布于边境堡垒与地方据点，战时可扩充为区域防御力量，但训练和装备水平不如常备军。
description.en: These forces were distributed among frontier forts and local strongholds and could be expanded in wartime into regional defense forces, though they were generally less well trained and equipped than the standing army.

commanderInChief.zh: 穆罕默德·阿迪勒·沙阿为名义最高统帅，实务由宫廷大臣、总司令与诸贵族将领分掌
commanderInChief.en: Muhammad Adil Shah served as nominal commander-in-chief, while practical command was shared among senior ministers, chief commanders, and noble generals

technology.zh: 军队广泛使用火绳枪、青铜与铁制火炮、攻城工事和坚固石砌堡垒体系；骑兵仍是主力，但火器与围城技术在17世纪中叶已十分关键。
technology.en: The military made broad use of matchlocks, bronze and iron artillery, siege works, and strong stone fortification systems; cavalry remained central, but firearms and siegecraft were crucial by the mid-seventeenth century.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自北方不断扩张的莫卧儿帝国；同时还需防范德干其他苏丹国的竞争、地方封臣离心以及西部海岸贸易与港口控制的争夺。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from the expanding Mughal Empire in the north; Bijapur also had to guard against rivalry with other Deccan sultanates, centrifugal tendencies among local service elites, and competition over trade and port influence on the western coast.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，主体为农村农业人口，集中分布在德干高原耕作区、灌溉地带及城堡城市周边；宗教与语言构成多元，穆斯林人口虽占少数，但在军政和城市生活中影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million, overwhelmingly rural and concentrated in cultivated tracts of the Deccan plateau, irrigated zones, and the hinterlands of fortified towns; the population was religiously and linguistically diverse, and although Muslims were a minority, they were highly influential in military, administrative, and urban life.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与高等贵族居于顶层，其下为军事封臣、文官、宗教学者与苏菲集团；城市中有商人、放贷者、工匠与劳工；乡村则由地主、村社官员、佃农、自耕农与无地劳动者构成，并受地方种姓秩序深刻影响。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal house and high nobility, beneath them military service elites, bureaucrats, religious scholars, and Sufi establishments; towns contained merchants, financiers, artisans, and laborers; the countryside consisted of landholders, village officials, tenants, peasant proprietors, and landless laborers, all deeply shaped by local caste hierarchies.

allies.zh: 无固定长期盟友；通常根据德干均势与反莫卧儿需要，与戈尔康达等邻近政权保持机会主义合作，并与阿拉伯海商人与波斯世界维持联系。
allies.en: There were no permanent long-term allies; Bijapur generally pursued opportunistic cooperation with neighboring powers such as Golconda in accordance with Deccan balance politics and anti-Mughal needs, while maintaining ties with Arabian Sea merchants and the Persianate world.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为莫卧儿帝国；与艾哈迈德纳加尔遗留势力、戈尔康达及地方边境领主之间亦存在反复冲突与竞争。
enemies.en: The principal enemy was the Mughal Empire; recurrent conflict and rivalry also existed with remnants of Ahmadnagar politics, Golconda, and local frontier chiefs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以维持德干独立、抵御莫卧儿南侵、在邻国间维持均势并保障西海岸贸易通道为核心。比贾布尔偏好以联姻、使节、贡礼、军事协定和有限战争相结合的方式处理周边关系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving Deccan independence, resisting Mughal expansion into the south, maintaining a regional balance among neighboring states, and securing access to west-coast trade routes. Bijapur preferred to manage external relations through a mix of marriage alliances, embassies, tribute-gifts, military compacts, and limited warfare.

era.zh: 早期近代

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的火绳枪与炮兵运用、德干式堡垒工程、大跨度穹顶建筑技术、改良蓄水与城镇供水设施、波斯化文书行政体系
keyInnovations.en: Mature use of matchlocks and artillery, Deccan fortification engineering, large-span domed architectural techniques, improved water storage and urban water supply systems, and a Persianate documentary-administrative system

strengths.zh: 比贾布尔拥有稳固王朝统治、强大的骑兵与炮兵传统、富裕的农业税基、繁荣的城市文化，以及能够整合多族群与多宗教人才的宫廷体系。
strengths.en: Bijapur possessed stable dynastic rule, strong cavalry and artillery traditions, a wealthy agrarian revenue base, vibrant urban culture, and a court system capable of integrating talent from multiple ethnic and religious backgrounds.

weaknesses.zh: 国家易受贵族派系、地方封臣自主性、财政对农业收成的依赖以及面对莫卧儿长期消耗战时的资源劣势所制约。
weaknesses.en: The state was constrained by noble factionalism, the autonomy of local service elites, fiscal dependence on agricultural yields, and a relative resource disadvantage in any prolonged war against the Mughal Empire.

outlook.zh: 1648年的比贾布尔总体稳定，仍是德干最重要强国之一；但从中长期看，若莫卧儿继续向南推进，而德干诸国无法形成持久联盟，其独立性将面临越来越大的压力。
outlook.en: In 1648 Bijapur was broadly stable and remained one of the leading powers of the Deccan; however, over the medium to long term, if Mughal expansion to the south continued and the Deccan states failed to form a durable coalition, its independence would face increasing pressure.

description.zh: 比贾布尔在德干保持重要区域势力，并与莫卧儿长期竞争。
description.en: Bijapur remained a major Deccan power and a long-term rival of the Mughals.

governmentForm.zh: 德干波斯化伊斯兰世袭苏丹制君主国，结合宫廷官僚、军政贵族与地方包税体系
governmentForm.en: A Persianate Islamic hereditary sultanate combining court bureaucracy, military nobility, and local revenue-farming administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会由苏丹与宫廷精英居上，包括德干穆斯林旧族、外来阿法其与伊朗—突厥系官员、印度教文书与税务阶层、城市商人、手工业者、农村地主与农民，以及奴仆与依附群体。国家在宗教与语言上高度多元，穆斯林统治阶层与占多数的印度教臣民长期共存。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the sultan and court elite, including established Deccani Muslims, immigrant Afaqi and Iranian-Turkic officials, Hindu scribal and revenue service groups, urban merchants, artisans, rural landholders and cultivators, as well as servile and dependent groups. The state was highly diverse in religion and language, with a Muslim ruling stratum governing a largely Hindu population.

rulingClass.zh: 苏丹、王族、宫廷大臣、总督、军功贵族、波斯语官僚、德干穆斯林军政家族，以及参与财政与地方治理的印度教纳亚克与文书家族
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the sultan, royal kin, court ministers, governors, military nobles, Persianate bureaucrats, Deccani Muslim service families, and Hindu nayaks and scribal houses involved in finance and local administration

succession.zh: 王朝世袭，原则上由库特卜沙希家族男性成员继承，但实际继位常受宫廷派系、后宫、贵族与军队支持左右
succession.en: Succession was dynastic and hereditary within the Qutb Shahi house, in principle through male members, but in practice was often shaped by court factions, the harem, nobles, and military support

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹为核心，下设首席大臣、财政、军务、司法、宫廷与港务等机构；地方上由省级总督、堡垒长官、税务承包者和村社首领共同运作。行政语言以波斯语为主，同时广泛使用达卡尼乌尔都与泰卢固语。
structure.en: The central government revolved around the sultan, with major offices for the chief minister, finance, military affairs, justice, court administration, and port supervision; in the provinces, governors, fort commanders, revenue contractors, and village headmen operated together. Persian was the principal language of administration, while Dakhni Urdu and Telugu were also widely used.

function.zh: 统筹朝政、协调各部、起草诏令并监督地方总督
function.en: Coordinated state affairs, supervised major departments, drafted royal orders, and oversaw provincial governors

function.zh: 负责地税评估、包税合同、矿业税收、关税与国库收支
function.en: Managed land revenue assessment, tax-farming contracts, mining revenue, customs duties, and treasury accounts

function.zh: 管理军队编制、军饷、火器、要塞与指挥官任命
function.en: Handled army organization, military pay, firearms, forts, and officer appointments

function.zh: 负责伊斯兰法官任命、宗教地产、慈善基金及部分民刑案件
function.en: Oversaw appointments of Islamic judges, religious endowments, charitable foundations, and portions of civil and criminal justice

function.zh: 管理王室开支、后宫、典礼、赏赐、工坊与宫殿供应
function.en: Managed royal expenditures, the harem, ceremonies, gifts, workshops, and palace provisioning

function.zh: 监管马苏利帕坦等港口贸易、航运税、商馆往来与海防事务
function.en: Supervised trade at ports such as Masulipatnam, collected shipping dues, dealt with foreign factories, and handled maritime security

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以苏巴或省区、帕尔加纳与村社层级构成。各地由总督、堡垒司令、税务承包人、地方印度教长官和村长共同治理，国家对核心地区控制较强，对边缘地带多依赖军事据点与间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through provinces, parganas, and villages. Governors, fort commanders, revenue farmers, local Hindu officials, and village headmen governed jointly; the state exercised stronger control in core areas and relied more on forts and indirect rule in peripheral zones.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具伊斯兰教法、苏丹敕令、行政惯例与地方习惯法。穆斯林事务多由卡齐裁决，印度教社群的婚姻、继承及村社纠纷常依传统规范处理；重大政治与财政案件则由朝廷直接干预。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic law, royal decrees, administrative practice, and local customary law. Muslim affairs were commonly judged by qadis, while marriage, inheritance, and village disputes among Hindu communities were often handled according to traditional norms; major political and fiscal cases drew direct court intervention.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家财政以农业地税为主，辅以港口关税、矿区与钻石贸易税、市场税、盐税、通行税及附庸贡赋。核心地区实行较系统的土地征收，其他地区则常以包税和军政承包方式征收。
taxationSystem.en: State taxation rested primarily on agricultural land revenue, supplemented by port customs, taxes on mines and the diamond trade, market duties, salt taxes, transit tolls, and tribute from subordinate chiefs. Core regions saw more systematic land assessment, while other areas often relied on tax farming and military-fiscal contracting.

religion.zh: 官方为伊斯兰教，以什叶派倾向的波斯化宫廷文化尤为明显，但国家整体奉行相对务实宽容的宗教政策。境内多数居民为印度教徒，亦有逊尼派穆斯林、苏菲团体、耆那教徒及少量基督徒商人社群。
religion.en: The official religion was Islam, with a distinctly Shi'i-leaning Persianate court culture, though the state as a whole followed a relatively pragmatic and tolerant religious policy. Most inhabitants were Hindu, alongside Sunni Muslims, Sufi groups, Jains, and small Christian merchant communities.

philosophy.zh: 宫廷思想深受波斯伊斯兰政治伦理、苏菲神秘主义和德干地方文学传统影响，同时在实际治理上吸纳印度教官僚文化与地方习惯。
philosophy.en: Courtly thought was shaped by Persian Islamic political ethics, Sufi mysticism, and Deccani literary traditions, while practical governance also absorbed Hindu bureaucratic culture and local custom.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系包括波斯—阿拉伯字母用于波斯语与达卡尼语文书，泰卢固文字用于地方语言文献与社会生活记录。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing systems were the Perso-Arabic script for Persian and Dakhni documents, and the Telugu script for local-language texts and many records of social life.

culturalAchievements.zh: 戈尔孔达在建筑、园林、达卡尼文学、波斯诗歌和宫廷音乐方面成就显著。海得拉巴与其周边拥有查尔米纳尔后的城市文化传统、清真寺、陵墓与宫苑建筑群；宫廷赞助多语文学，形成德干地区独特的伊斯兰—印度混合审美。
culturalAchievements.en: Golconda was notable for achievements in architecture, gardens, Dakhni literature, Persian poetry, and court music. Hyderabad and its surroundings sustained the urban culture that had grown after the Charminar era, with mosques, tombs, and palace-garden complexes; the court patronized multilingual literature and fostered a distinctive Deccani Indo-Islamic aesthetic.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与宫廷语言主要属伊朗语支的波斯语及印欧语系的达卡尼乌尔都；民间多数语言为德拉威语系泰卢固语，另有卡纳达语、马拉地语等
languageFamily.en: Official and court languages centered on Persian of the Iranian branch and Dakhni Urdu of the Indo-European family; the majority vernacular was Telugu of the Dravidian family, with Kannada, Marathi, and others also present

gdpDescription.zh: 1648年的戈尔孔达是印度次大陆最富庶的区域政权之一，经济建立在高产农业、棉纺织业、钻石开采与加工、以及科罗曼德海岸外贸之上。其财富虽不及莫卧儿帝国总体规模，但按地区密度和国际贸易活跃度而言极为突出。
gdpDescription.en: In 1648 Golconda was among the wealthiest regional powers of the Indian subcontinent, with an economy based on productive agriculture, cotton textiles, diamond mining and finishing, and overseas trade along the Coromandel coast. Though smaller in total scale than the Mughal Empire, it was exceptionally prosperous in wealth density and international commercial activity.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米与杂粮农业、棉花种植、棉布织造与印染、钻石采掘与贸易、马匹与军需采购、盐业、金属工艺、造船配套供应、城镇手工业与港口服务业
mainIndustries.en: Rice and grain agriculture, cotton cultivation, cotton weaving and dyeing, diamond extraction and trade, horse and military supply procurement, salt production, metalworking, ship-supply industries, urban crafts, and port services

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以棉布、印花布、靛蓝、钻石、米粮、盐、铁器和手工制品为主；进口包括战马、铜锡等金属、奢侈品、香料、珍珠及西亚与东南亚货物
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on cotton cloth, painted textiles, indigo, diamonds, grain, salt, iron goods, and manufactures; imports included warhorses, metals such as copper and tin, luxury goods, spices, pearls, and merchandise from West and Southeast Asia

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人、港口经纪人与钻石商积累了可观财富，部分乡村地主和税务承包人也较富裕；但普通农户财富有限，易受歉收、征税波动与战事影响。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants, port brokers, and diamond traders accumulated substantial wealth, and some rural landholders and revenue contractors were also prosperous; ordinary peasant households, however, held limited assets and remained vulnerable to bad harvests, tax pressure, and warfare.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制属于农业税收国家下的商业化混合经济：王权控制财政、矿区和港务关键节点，但市场交易、包税、商人信贷和跨洋贸易网络高度活跃，荷兰、英国、波斯、阿拉伯与印度商人共同参与。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a commercialized mixed system under an agrarian revenue state: the crown controlled key fiscal, mining, and port nodes, but market exchange, tax farming, merchant credit, and overseas trade networks were highly active, involving Dutch, English, Persian, Arab, and Indian merchants.

source.en: Port customs and maritime trade duties

source.zh: 钻石矿区与矿业相关税收

source.en: Market taxes, transit tolls, and salt duties

source.zh: 附庸贡赋、罚没与杂项收入

category.zh: 公共工程、宗教建筑与捐赠
category.en: Public works, religious buildings, and endowments

category.zh: 海军巡防、港口与外交支出
category.en: Maritime patrols, ports, and diplomatic expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库在1648年前后仍较充盈，受益于钻石贸易、港口税收和相对繁荣的农业腹地，但大量财富也分散于贵族、地方承包人和商人手中，中央财政的动员能力并非无限。
treasuryDescription.en: Around 1648 the treasury remained well supplied, benefiting from diamonds, customs revenue, and a relatively prosperous agrarian hinterland, though much wealth was also dispersed among nobles, local contractors, and merchants, so central fiscal mobilization was not unlimited.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策注重维持军费、巩固港口与矿区收入，并通过包税、特许与宫廷赞助平衡各派势力。整体偏重短中期现金流与政治稳定，而非彻底的制度统一。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized sustaining military expenditure, securing port and mining income, and balancing factions through tax farming, privileges, and court patronage. It prioritized medium-term cash flow and political stability over complete institutional uniformity.

description.zh: 由贵族骑兵、持薪军人与受封军官组成，是快速机动与野战核心，部分依赖进口战马。
description.en: Composed of noble cavalry, salaried troopers, and mansab-like service retainers; it formed the core of field mobility and depended in part on imported warhorses.

description.zh: 包括持火绳枪步兵、持矛盾步兵与守城部队，适合城防、营地守卫和与炮兵协同作战。
description.en: Included matchlock infantry, spear-and-shield foot, and garrison troops, suited to fortress defense, camp security, and cooperation with artillery.

description.zh: 负责攻守城炮、火药库、铸炮、城堡维修及围城工程，德干要塞体系使其尤为重要。
description.en: Handled siege and defensive artillery, powder magazines, gun casting, fort maintenance, and engineering works; they were especially important in the Deccan fort system.

name.zh: 地方附庸与港口巡防队

description.zh: 由地方纳亚克、附庸首领与沿海守备组成，战时补充兵力并保护交通线与港口。
description.en: Drawn from local nayaks, subordinate chiefs, and coastal guards, these forces supplemented wartime manpower and protected routes and ports.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿卜杜拉·库特卜·沙阿苏丹，实际军务多由宫廷大臣与高级将领分掌
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Abdullah Qutb Shah, though practical military control was often shared with senior ministers and leading commanders

technology.zh: 军备属于17世纪德干火药时代水平，广泛使用火绳枪、青铜与铁制火炮、坚固山堡与石城防御体系。与莫卧儿及欧洲商人接触使其能获取火器、火药原料和铸造技术，但海军并非其绝对强项。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of the seventeenth-century Deccan gunpowder age, with widespread use of matchlocks, bronze and iron artillery, and strong hill-fort and masonry fortification systems. Contact with the Mughals and European merchants gave access to firearms, powder materials, and casting techniques, though naval power was not its supreme strength.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方扩张中的莫卧儿帝国，以及德干诸苏丹国之间时而合作、时而竞争的复杂局势；地方豪强离心、沿海商贸冲突和城堡争夺亦构成持续压力。
threats.en: The main threat came from the expanding Mughal Empire to the north, along with the unstable mix of rivalry and cooperation among the Deccan sultanates; centrifugal local magnates, coastal trade disputes, and struggles over forts also created constant pressure.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约三百五十万，主要分布于特伦甘纳与安得拉内陆农业区、城堡带及通向科罗曼德海岸的贸易走廊。人口结构以乡村农民为主，城市与港口居民虽占比较低，但经济权重极高。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 3.5 million, concentrated in the agrarian zones of Telangana and the Andhra interior, the fort belts, and the trade corridors linking the interior to the Coromandel coast. Most people were rural cultivators, while urban and port populations, though smaller in share, carried very high economic weight.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体包括王室与大贵族、军功贵族与官僚、宗教人士、城市商人与金融中介、工匠和织工、村社地主与中农、小农与佃农、劳工及奴仆。宗教、语言与职业身份彼此交错，流动性在城市商业部门相对更高。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included the royal house and high nobility, military nobles and bureaucrats, religious figures, urban merchants and financiers, artisans and weavers, village landholders and middling peasants, smallholders and tenants, laborers, and servants or slaves. Religious, linguistic, and occupational identities overlapped, and mobility was relatively greater in the urban commercial sector.

allies.zh: 在1648年前后并无绝对稳定盟友，通常与比贾布尔等德干邻国维持权宜性合作，以牵制莫卧儿；同时与波斯湾、阿拉伯海和欧洲商人保持务实关系。
allies.en: Around 1648 it had no permanently fixed ally, but often maintained pragmatic cooperation with neighboring Deccan states such as Bijapur to balance the Mughals; it also cultivated practical relations with merchants from the Persian Gulf, the Arabian Sea, and Europe.

enemies.zh: 首要战略对手是莫卧儿帝国；此外与部分德干邻国在边界、要塞和附庸控制上存在摩擦。
enemies.en: Its primary strategic adversary was the Mughal Empire; it also experienced friction with some Deccan neighbors over borders, forts, and control of subordinate chiefs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以生存与繁荣并重：对北方强敌采取缓冲、谈判、纳贡或结盟等灵活手段；对海上贸易则重在保护港口收入、吸引外国商人并避免任何单一外来势力垄断。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy balanced survival with prosperity: toward the northern great power it used flexible measures such as buffering, negotiation, tribute, or alliance; in maritime affairs it aimed to protect customs income, attract foreign merchants, and prevent any single outside power from monopolizing trade.

era.zh: 近代早期火药与海贸时代
era.en: Early Modern gunpowder and maritime trade era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与能力包括火绳枪与攻守城炮、石造堡垒体系、钻石采掘和分级交易技术、棉布印染与纺织工艺、跨区域商人信贷网络，以及适应季风海贸的港口物流组织。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and capabilities included matchlocks and siege artillery, masonry fortification systems, diamond extraction and grading for trade, cotton textile dyeing and weaving techniques, long-distance merchant credit networks, and port logistics adapted to monsoon maritime commerce.

strengths.zh: 戈尔孔达的最大优势在于财政富裕、钻石与纺织品贸易、高度商业化的港口经济、坚固要塞体系，以及能够整合波斯化宫廷文化与本地行政资源的灵活政治结构。
strengths.en: Golconda's greatest strengths were its rich finances, diamond and textile trade, highly commercialized port economy, strong fortification network, and a flexible political structure able to combine Persianate court culture with local administrative resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于王国规模有限、对贵族与包税体系依赖较深、内部派系复杂、地方控制不均衡，并长期承受莫卧儿帝国这一更大军事财政机器的压力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in its limited territorial scale, heavy dependence on nobles and tax-farming arrangements, complex internal factionalism, uneven local control, and the sustained pressure of the far larger Mughal military-fiscal machine.

outlook.zh: 1648年的前景总体仍属繁荣而谨慎乐观：只要海贸与钻石收入持续、德干诸国尚能相互制衡，戈尔孔达仍可维持富强；但从长远看，若莫卧儿继续南压且内部凝聚不足，其独立性将面临严峻挑战。
outlook.en: The outlook in 1648 was still prosperous and cautiously favorable: so long as maritime trade and diamond revenues continued and the Deccan states maintained some balance among themselves, Golconda could remain wealthy and strong; in the longer run, however, continued Mughal pressure and insufficient internal cohesion posed a grave threat to its independence.

description.zh: 戈尔康达以钻石贸易和海上商业闻名，是德干重要富裕苏丹国。
description.en: Golconda, renowned for diamond trade and maritime commerce, was a wealthy and important Deccan sultanate.

governmentForm.zh: 缅甸式君主专制王国，结合宫廷官僚、地方总督与附庸土司体系
governmentForm.en: A Burmese-style absolute monarchy combining court bureaucracy, provincial governorships, and tributary Shan chieftaincies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、僧团、世袭贵族与军政官员为上层，乡村自由农民为主体，并包含战俘、债役者与宫廷服役人口。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the royal house, the Sangha, hereditary nobles, and military-administrative elites, with village free cultivators forming the majority and smaller groups of war captives, debt bondsmen, and royal service dependents.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、宫廷大臣、地方总督、掸邦土司与高级僧侣共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal family, court ministers, provincial governors, Shan sawbwas, and senior Buddhist clergy

succession.zh: 原则上为王族内继承，由国王指定或宫廷权力斗争决定，长子继承并不稳固
succession.en: Succession was in principle dynastic within the royal house, but in practice depended on royal designation and court power struggles; strict primogeniture was not secure

structure.zh: 中央政府围绕阿瓦宫廷运作，国王通过内廷、文武大臣、财政与地方官体系治理核心缅甸地区，并对边疆掸邦、缅甸北部与南部地区实施层级不一的间接控制。
structure.en: The central government operated around the court at Ava, where the king ruled the core Burmese regions through palace institutions, civil and military ministers, fiscal officers, and provincial administrators, while exercising varying degrees of indirect control over the Shan states and peripheral northern and southern territories.

function.zh: 管理王室诏令、宫廷礼仪、侍从、机密事务与王命传达
function.en: Handled royal edicts, court ceremony, attendants, confidential affairs, and transmission of the king's commands

function.zh: 征收土地税、人头劳役、贸易关税与附庸贡赋，管理王室仓储与贵金属收入
function.en: Collected land revenue, labor obligations, customs dues, and tributary exactions, and managed royal granaries and bullion income

function.zh: 负责军队登记、兵役征发、军需补给、象队与地方守军调度
function.en: Oversaw troop registration, conscription, military supply, elephant corps, and deployment of provincial garrisons

function.zh: 审理案件、执行习惯法与王令、监察地方官并调解村社纠纷
function.en: Heard cases, enforced customary law and royal decrees, supervised provincial officials, and mediated village disputes

function.zh: 管理对佛寺的捐施、僧团关系、宗教土地确认与重大公共功德工程
function.en: Managed donations to monasteries, relations with the Sangha, confirmation of religious landholdings, and major public merit works

function.zh: 治理各省城镇、征收赋役、维持治安并向阿瓦输送兵员与物资
function.en: Governed provincial towns, collected taxes and labor, maintained order, and supplied troops and materials to Ava

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分为王室直接控制的核心平原省份与依附性较强的边疆地区。核心区由总督、城主和村社首领层层管理；掸邦等边地则多由土司保留相当自治，只需纳贡、供兵并承认宗主权。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was divided between the crown-controlled core plains provinces and more loosely held frontier zones. In the core, governors, town chiefs, and village headmen formed a layered hierarchy; in the Shan states and other borderlands, local chiefs retained substantial autonomy so long as they paid tribute, provided troops, and acknowledged overlordship.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以缅甸习惯法、王室敕令和佛教伦理原则并行为基础。婚姻、继承、债务和土地争议常依地方惯例审理，重大案件则由王廷或省级官员裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Burmese customary law, royal decrees, and Buddhist moral principles. Marriage, inheritance, debt, and land disputes were often judged according to local custom, while major cases were decided by the royal court or provincial authorities.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、实物征收、徭役、港口与河运关税、森林与矿产收益以及附庸贡赋为主。由于战乱、免税宗教土地增加和地方离心，中央实际征收能力弱于全盛时期。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on land revenue, collections in kind, labor service, port and river customs, forest and mineral income, and tribute from dependencies. Because of warfare, expanding tax-exempt religious lands, and provincial centrifugal tendencies, effective central collection was weaker than at the dynasty's height.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教为国教核心，并与纳神信仰、地方精灵崇拜和王权功德观相结合
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism formed the core religion of the state, combined with nat spirit worship, local cults, and royal ideas of merit

philosophy.zh: 政治与伦理思想以佛教功德、达摩王理念、因果报应以及缅甸王权宇宙秩序观为中心
philosophy.en: Political and ethical thought centered on Buddhist merit, the ideal of the righteous dhamma-king, karmic causation, and Burmese cosmological views of kingship

writingSystem.zh: 使用缅文书写系统记录王令、宗教文献、编年史与行政文书；巴利文在宗教与学术中具有重要地位
writingSystem.en: The Burmese script was used for royal orders, religious texts, chronicles, and administrative records, while Pali remained important in religion and scholarship

culturalAchievements.zh: 东吁时期延续了佛塔、寺院、木石建筑、编年史撰写与巴利佛学传统。阿瓦与上缅甸地区仍是僧院教育、诗歌、律法整理和宫廷礼仪文化的重要中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The Taungoo era sustained traditions of pagoda and monastery construction, timber and masonry architecture, chronicle writing, and Pali Buddhist learning. Ava and Upper Burma remained important centers of monastic education, poetry, legal compilation, and court ceremonial culture.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言属汉藏语系藏缅语族，宫廷与核心行政语言为缅语，宗教学术广泛使用巴利语
languageFamily.en: The main spoken language belonged to the Tibeto-Burman branch of the Sino-Tibetan family; Burmese was the court and principal administrative language, while Pali was widely used in religion and scholarship

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作农业为基础，辅以内河贸易、林产、象牙、宝石和区域性转口交换。相较16世纪巅峰时期，17世纪中叶经济规模仍大，但因政治衰退、边疆不稳和征发负担加重而趋于停滞。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on rice agriculture, supplemented by river trade, forest products, ivory, gemstones, and regional exchange. Compared with the sixteenth-century peak, the mid-seventeenth-century economy remained substantial but was stagnating under political decline, unstable frontiers, and heavier extraction.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、芝麻与棉作、柚木和森林采集、内河运输、手工业织造、漆器、金属加工、盐业、宝石与象役贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, sesame and cotton farming, teak and forest extraction, river transport, textile handicrafts, lacquerware, metalworking, salt production, gemstones, and elephant-related trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口稻米、木材、漆器、棉布、宝石、象牙、蜂蜡、油料和林产；进口金属制品、火器、纺织品、奢侈品、马匹与海贸商品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, timber, lacquerware, cotton cloth, gemstones, ivory, beeswax, oils, and forest products; imports included metal goods, firearms, textiles, luxury wares, horses, and maritime trade commodities

name.en: Silver kyat and mixed media currency

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现为耕地使用权、耕牛、粮储、竹木住房与村社关系；贵族和寺院则控制大片土地、劳役人口与储藏贵金属。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in cultivation rights, draft animals, grain stores, bamboo-wood housing, and village ties; nobles and monasteries controlled larger estates, dependent labor, and stores of bullion.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为农业贡赋型王国经济，结合村社自给、王室征发、地方市场交换与有限的区域长途贸易。国家对关键港口、关卡与特许贸易保有较强干预，但无法完全整合边疆商业网络。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a tributary agrarian monarchy combining village subsistence, royal extraction, local market exchange, and limited long-distance regional trade. The state intervened strongly in key ports, toll stations, and privileged commerce, but could not fully integrate frontier trading networks.

source.zh: 徭役折算与王室劳役收益
source.en: Commuted labor dues and royal service income
Tribute from dependencies and frontier submissions
Forest, mineral, and royal monopoly income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍保有相当储备，但较王朝中兴时期明显削弱，且部分财富以宫廷珍宝、粮仓、象队和地方掌控资源形式存在，流动性有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still retained meaningful reserves, but these were clearly reduced from the dynasty's restored peak, and part of state wealth existed in court valuables, granaries, elephant stocks, and provincially controlled resources rather than fully liquid funds.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持宫廷威望与边防开支，通过加重地方征发、恢复税籍和追索附庸贡赋来补缺，但结构性问题在于免税寺产扩张、地方截留与军政低效。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to preserve court prestige and frontier defense by intensifying local exactions, restoring tax rolls, and reclaiming tributary payments, but it faced structural problems from expanding tax-exempt religious estates, provincial retention, and military-administrative inefficiency.

description.zh: 步军为军队主体，由核心平原征发兵与驻军组成，使用长矛、刀盾、弓弩及一定数量火绳枪。
description.en: Infantry formed the bulk of the army, composed of levies and garrison troops from the core plains, armed with spears, swords and shields, bows, and a limited but significant number of matchlocks.

description.zh: 骑兵数量有限，多用于侦察、追击与边疆行动，马匹部分依赖外来贸易。
description.en: Cavalry remained limited in number and was used mainly for scouting, pursuit, and frontier operations, with horses partly dependent on external trade.

description.zh: 战象仍具象征与战术价值，用于冲击、统帅乘象和威慑，但在火器时代实际作战作用已受限制。
description.en: War elephants still possessed symbolic and tactical value for shock action, command platforms, and intimidation, though their battlefield effectiveness was increasingly constrained in the gunpowder age.

description.zh: 装备青铜或铁制火炮、城防炮位和火绳枪手，主要用于守城、河防和阵地支援。
description.en: These troops operated bronze or iron cannon, fortress artillery, and matchlock units, serving chiefly in siege defense, river defense, and positional support.

description.zh: 由地方总督、掸邦土司和边区首领提供，战力参差不齐，但对维持广阔领土统治不可或缺。
description.en: Supplied by provincial governors, Shan sawbwas, and frontier chiefs, these forces varied greatly in quality but were indispensable for holding the kingdom's broad territories.

commanderInChief.zh: 宾德莱王兼最高统帅

technology.zh: 军技术处于东南亚早期近代水平，广泛使用冷兵器并辅以火绳枪、城防炮和攻城器材。火器与铸炮能力存在，但不如最强海贸国家或西亚强国那样系统化。
technology.en: Military technology stood at an early modern Southeast Asian level, relying heavily on traditional weapons supplemented by matchlocks, fortress artillery, and siege equipment. Firearms and casting capabilities existed, but were less systematic than those of the strongest maritime states or major West Asian powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自暹罗阿瑜陀耶的竞争、南部控制力下降、掸邦和边疆离心、内部王位斗争，以及偶发的阿拉干和地方叛乱压力。
threats.en: Primary threats came from rivalry with Siamese Ayutthaya, weakening control in the south, centrifugal tendencies in the Shan and frontier regions, internal succession struggles, and intermittent pressure from Arakan and local rebellions.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在伊洛瓦底江中上游平原、阿瓦周边与主要灌溉农业区。王国包含缅族主体，以及掸、孟、克钦、钦、克伦等多族群与边疆社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the middle and upper Irrawaddy plains, around Ava, and in major irrigated agricultural zones. The kingdom included a Burman majority alongside Shan, Mon, Kachin, Chin, Karen, and other frontier peoples.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致包括王族与贵族、军政官员、僧侣、土司与地方首领、自由农民、工匠与商人，以及依附劳役者、战俘与奴役人口。僧团虽非世俗阶层，却在社会声望和资源占有上极其重要。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included the royal family and nobility, military and civil officials, monks, sawbwas and local chiefs, free cultivators, artisans and merchants, and dependent laborers, captives, and slaves. The Sangha was not a secular class but held enormous social prestige and resources.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式盟国；与部分掸邦、地方土司和边境势力保持朝贡或临时合作关系
allies.en: There were no stable formal allies in the modern sense; the kingdom maintained tributary or temporary cooperative ties with some Shan states, local chiefs, and border powers

enemies.zh: 主要对手为暹罗阿瑜陀耶王国；此外阿拉干、南部离心势力与不稳附庸也构成现实威胁
enemies.en: The principal rival was the Siamese kingdom of Ayutthaya; Arakan, southern centrifugal forces, and unstable dependencies also posed real dangers

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持宗主权、确保边疆服属、避免多线大战和恢复王朝威望为目标。与更远方的大帝国几无直接战略对抗，主要精力仍集中于上缅甸整合和对暹竞争。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve overlordship, secure frontier submission, avoid major wars on multiple fronts, and restore dynastic prestige. The state had little direct strategic confrontation with distant great empires, focusing instead on Upper Burmese consolidation and rivalry with Siam.

era.zh: 近世早期

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的灌溉稻作、内河舟运组织、木石佛教建筑、缅文行政文书、铸炮与火绳枪使用、战象后勤与区域性工艺生产
keyInnovations.en: Mature irrigated rice agriculture, organized inland river transport, timber and masonry Buddhist architecture, Burmese documentary administration, cannon casting and matchlock use, elephant logistics, and regional craft production

strengths.zh: 东吁国仍拥有庞大农业腹地、深厚王权传统、佛教正统性、伊洛瓦底河交通网络以及可动员的区域兵力。阿瓦作为政治与文化中心，仍能在危机中维系国家连续性。
strengths.en: The Taungoo state still possessed a large agrarian heartland, deep traditions of kingship, Buddhist legitimacy, the Irrawaddy transport network, and regionally mobilizable military manpower. Ava remained capable of preserving political continuity in times of crisis.

weaknesses.zh: 国家正处衰退期，中央财政吃紧，地方总督和附庸土司离心，王位继承不稳，南北整合能力下降，军队虽数量可观但组织质量与持续作战能力有限。
weaknesses.en: The state was in decline: central finances were strained, governors and tributary chiefs were increasingly centrifugal, succession was unstable, north-south integration was weakening, and the army, though sizeable, had limited organizational quality and sustained operational capacity.

outlook.zh: 1648年前后的东吁王朝仍有维持统治的基础，但若无法解决财政亏空、宫廷内斗和边疆控制松动，未来数十年将面临进一步分裂与政权更替风险。
outlook.en: Around 1648, the Taungoo dynasty still retained the foundations needed to survive, but unless it resolved fiscal deficits, court infighting, and loosening frontier control, it faced a serious risk of further fragmentation and eventual dynastic replacement in the coming decades.

description.zh: 东吁王朝在17世纪中叶已由盛转衰，内部与边疆压力并存。
description.en: By the mid-17th century, the Taungoo kingdom had passed its peak and faced both internal and frontier pressures.

governmentForm.zh: 东南亚曼荼罗式君主制，王权高度集中于宫廷，并通过贵族与地方城邦网络实行统治
governmentForm.en: A Southeast Asian mandala-style monarchy, with authority concentrated at court and exercised through a network of nobles and tributary towns

socialStructure.zh: 社会以国王为中心，实行等级鲜明的萨克迪纳与徭役体系，上层为王族、贵族与高级僧侣，中层为官员、城市商人和地方首领，下层为自由农民、依附民、劳役民与奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on the king and organized through a sharply stratified sakdina and corvée system, with royals, nobles, and senior monks at the top; officials, urban merchants, and local chiefs in the middle; and free peasants, dependents, corvée laborers, and slaves below

rulingClass.zh: 王族、宫廷贵族、文武官僚以及与王室关系密切的高级佛教僧团
rulingClass.en: The royal family, court nobility, civil and military officials, and the senior Buddhist clergy closely tied to the crown

succession.zh: 王位继承并非严格长子继承制，而由王族内部竞争、宫廷共识、武力与资历共同决定；1648年时巴沙通王仍在位
succession.en: Succession was not governed by strict primogeniture but by competition within the royal house, court consensus, force, and seniority; in 1648 King Prasat Thong remained on the throne

structure.zh: 中央政务围绕国王与宫廷展开，以四柱大臣体制、王室内廷和地方总督体系共同构成。朝廷依照传统暹罗官制分配文武事务，地方则通过属城、边镇与附庸首领进行间接管理。
structure.en: Central government revolved around the king and court, combining the Four Pillars ministerial system, the royal household, and a provincial governorship structure. Civil and military matters were distributed through traditional Siamese offices, while the provinces were managed indirectly through subordinate towns, frontier posts, and tributary chiefs.

function.zh: 管理首都治安、城市秩序、宫廷周边事务、公共劳役与部分司法事务
function.en: Managed security in the capital, urban order, matters around the court, public labor obligations, and some judicial affairs

name.en: Krom Na, Agriculture and Provincial Administration

function.zh: 负责田地登记、稻税征收、徭役调配、农村人口编组与地方行政监督
function.en: Oversaw land registration, rice taxation, corvée allocation, rural population registration, and supervision of provincial administration

name.en: Krom Phra Khlang, Treasury and Foreign Trade

function.zh: 掌管国家财政、王室仓储、对外贸易、海关、外交接待以及与外国商人的关系
function.en: Handled state finance, royal warehouses, foreign trade, customs, diplomatic reception, and relations with foreign merchants

name.en: Krom Wang and Military Service Administration

function.zh: 管理宫廷护卫、兵员登记、军役征发、战时后勤以及部分王室事务
function.en: Managed palace guards, manpower registration, military levies, wartime logistics, and certain royal household affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分为内地直辖城、外地大城与边疆属邦。朝廷任命或认可总督、城主与地方首领，地方需输送贡赋、劳役与兵员；对老挝、马来与高棉边缘地区则多采附庸与间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was divided among directly ruled inner provinces, outer major towns, and frontier tributaries. The court appointed or recognized governors, town lords, and local chiefs, who supplied tribute, labor, and manpower; Lao, Malay, and Khmer fringe regions were often governed through tributary and indirect arrangements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以王令、宫廷判例、习惯法与佛教伦理共同构成，后世汇编为《三印法典》的前身传统。司法强调等级秩序、王权权威、赔偿与身体惩罚。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decrees, court precedent, customary law, and Buddhist moral norms, forming the traditions later compiled in the Three Seals Law. Justice emphasized hierarchy, royal authority, compensation, and corporal punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖稻米与地税、属邦贡赋、港口关税、王室贸易利润、专卖与劳役征发。对人口亦通过萨克迪纳与兵役登记间接控制赋役负担。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on rice and land taxes, tribute from dependent polities, port customs, profits from royal trade, monopolies, and labor levies. Population was also controlled fiscally through sakdina ranking and manpower registration.

religion.zh: 以上座部佛教为国教核心，同时融合婆罗门宫廷仪式、祖灵崇拜与地方民间信仰；穆斯林、华人、波斯人、基督徒与其他外来社群亦在都城和港口存在
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism was the core state religion, combined with Brahmanical court ritual, spirit worship, and local popular beliefs; Muslim, Chinese, Persian, Christian, and other foreign communities were also present in the capital and ports

philosophy.zh: 政治与伦理思想以佛教功德观、王者护法理念、印度化王权观念及实用性的宫廷国家craft相结合
philosophy.en: Political and ethical thought blended Buddhist merit concepts, the ideal of the king as protector of the faith, Indic models of sacral kingship, and pragmatic court statecraft

writingSystem.zh: 使用泰文书写系统记录王令、佛经、贸易文书与编年史，宫廷与外交中亦使用巴利文、高棉文、孟文及部分波斯文和葡萄牙文
writingSystem.en: The Thai writing system was used for royal decrees, Buddhist texts, trade records, and chronicles, while Pali, Khmer, Mon, and to some extent Persian and Portuguese also appeared in court and diplomatic contexts

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿瑜陀耶时期在寺院建筑、佛像铸造、宫廷文学、年代记编纂、礼仪制度和国际都市文化方面成就突出。国都拥有宏伟寺庙、宫殿群与多族裔商人社区，是东南亚重要文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The Ayutthaya period achieved distinction in temple architecture, Buddha image casting, court literature, chronicle writing, ceremonial institutions, and cosmopolitan urban culture. The capital possessed grand monasteries, palace complexes, and multiethnic merchant quarters, making it a major Southeast Asian cultural center.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与主体语言属台-卡岱语系；宫廷宗教文献深受巴利语和高棉语影响，商业环境中亦常见华语、马来语、孟语与波斯语
languageFamily.en: The official and majority language belonged to the Tai-Kadai family; court and religious texts were strongly influenced by Pali and Khmer, while Chinese, Malay, Mon, and Persian were also common in commerce

gdpDescription.zh: 1648年前后的阿瑜陀耶经济在东南亚属高度繁荣水平，以稻米生产、森林物产、海贸转口和王室商业垄断为支柱。都城是连接中国、日本、印度洋世界与欧洲商人的枢纽，经济总量在区域内位居前列。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1648 Ayutthaya possessed one of the most prosperous economies in Southeast Asia, built on rice production, forest products, entrepôt trade, and royal commercial monopolies. The capital was a hub linking China, Japan, the Indian Ocean world, and European merchants, placing its aggregate economy among the leading ones in the region.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米种植、渔业、糖业、林产采集、造船、陶器、纺织、金属工艺、王室仓储贸易与港口转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, fisheries, sugar production, forest extraction, shipbuilding, ceramics, textiles, metalworking, royal warehouse trade, and port entrepôt commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口稻米、鹿皮、苏木、锡、象牙、沉香、胡椒、糖、木材与林产；进口白银、铜、丝绸、棉布、瓷器、武器、火器、奢侈品和马匹
tradeGoods.en: Exports included rice, deerskins, sappanwood, tin, ivory, agarwood, pepper, sugar, timber, and forest products; imports included silver, copper, silk, cotton textiles, porcelain, weapons, firearms, luxury goods, and horses

householdWealth.zh: 城乡家庭财富差异明显。湄南河平原农户通常拥有稻田、牲畜、农具与家屋，但财富多受赋役与气候影响；城市商人、税农、外商中介与高级官员积累了显著财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply between countryside and city. Peasant families in the Chao Phraya plain typically held rice land, tools, livestock, and houses, but remained vulnerable to taxes and climate; urban merchants, tax farmers, foreign intermediaries, and senior officials accumulated substantial wealth.

unit.en: Ayutthayan baht silver value per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为农业基础上的宫廷再分配与商业化并存模式。国家通过赋役、港税、专卖和王室贸易深度介入市场，而华人、马来人、波斯人、日本人和欧洲商人则活跃于国际贸易网络。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined an agrarian base with court redistribution and expanding commercialization. The state intervened heavily through taxation, customs, monopolies, and royal trade, while Chinese, Malay, Persian, Japanese, and European merchants operated actively in international networks.

source.en: Land tax and monetized rural obligations
Tribute from dependencies and miscellaneous taxes

category.zh: 宗教建筑、寺院与公共工程
category.en: Religious foundations, monasteries, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库因活跃海贸和王室专卖而较为充实，储备由白银、铜钱、实物仓储、贡品和可调配劳力共同构成，但大量财富也以宫廷宝藏、寺院捐献和地方积存的形式分散存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was comparatively well supplied thanks to active maritime trade and royal monopolies. Reserves consisted of silver, copper money, grain stores, tribute goods, and mobilizable labor, though considerable wealth was also dispersed in court treasure, monastic endowments, and provincial stockpiles.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持王权、边防与贸易控制，在丰年积储并通过王室贸易扩大现金流；危机时则可加重徭役、征调贡赋或扩张专卖收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized royal authority, frontier defense, and control of trade, storing surpluses in good years and expanding cash flow through royal commerce; in times of strain, the court could intensify labor demands, tribute extraction, or monopoly revenues.

description.zh: 由都城卫队、地方征发兵与火器步兵组成，是军队主干，装备长矛、刀、盾、弓弩与火绳枪
description.en: Composed of capital guards, provincial levies, and firearm infantry, forming the core of the army and equipped with spears, blades, shields, bows, and matchlocks

description.zh: 战象在东南亚战争中兼具冲击、统帅平台与威慑作用，辅以数量有限的骑兵与乘象贵族部队
description.en: War elephants served as shock forces, command platforms, and symbols of prestige in Southeast Asian warfare, supported by a limited cavalry and mounted noble contingents

description.zh: 部署城防火炮、舰炮与野战小炮，吸收葡萄牙、穆斯林和本地工匠技术，负责守城和河道战
description.en: Deployed fortress guns, naval cannon, and smaller field pieces, drawing on Portuguese, Muslim, and local craftsmanship for siege defense and riverine warfare

description.zh: 以河战、海岸巡逻、护航和运输为主，依赖桨帆船、武装商船与战舟，保护湄南河和暹罗湾交通
description.en: Focused on river war, coastal patrol, convoying, and transport, using oared and sailing craft, armed merchant vessels, and war boats to secure the Chao Phraya and Gulf of Siam

description.zh: 由外地城镇、边疆首领和附庸地区在战时提供，战力不均但对大规模动员极其重要
description.en: Supplied in wartime by outer towns, frontier chiefs, and tributary regions; uneven in quality but essential for large-scale mobilization

commanderInChief.zh: 巴沙通王兼最高统帅，日常军务由宫廷武官与各城将领分掌
commanderInChief.en: King Prasat Thong as supreme commander, with day-to-day military affairs handled by court officers and provincial commanders

technology.zh: 军事实力属东南亚强国水平，传统战象、冷兵器和征发制与火绳枪、铸炮、棱堡化城防和海上火器逐渐结合，但尚未形成完全欧式常备军体系
technology.en: Military capability stood at the level of a major Southeast Asian power, combining traditional war elephants, edged weapons, and levy systems with matchlocks, cast cannon, more bastioned defenses, and naval firearms, though not yet a fully European-style standing army

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自缅甸东吁王朝的边境竞争、高棉与老挝方向的控制波动、马来半岛港口争夺，以及宫廷内部继承与贵族斗争可能引发的军事不稳
threats.en: Major threats came from frontier rivalry with the Taungoo Burmese state, fluctuating control over Khmer and Lao regions, competition over Malay Peninsula ports, and internal instability arising from succession and noble factionalism

populationDescription.zh: 全国人口约三百万，主体集中在湄南河流域和稻作平原。人口结构多元，包括暹罗人、孟人、高棉人、老挝人、马来人、华人、波斯人、日本人及其他侨民社群，首都尤其呈现国际都市特征。
populationDescription.en: The kingdom's population was about three million, concentrated mainly in the Chao Phraya basin and rice-growing plains. It was ethnically diverse, including Siamese, Mon, Khmer, Lao, Malay, Chinese, Persians, Japanese, and other expatriate communities, with the capital especially cosmopolitan.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层自上而下大致为王族、贵族与高官、僧侣、地方首领、商人和手工业者、自由农民、依附民、战俘与奴隶。萨克迪纳等级制度决定个人名分、役使义务与司法待遇。
socialClasses.en: The social hierarchy ran broadly from royals, nobles and high officials, monks, and local chiefs, down to merchants and artisans, free peasants, dependents, captives, and slaves. The sakdina ranking system defined status, service obligations, and legal treatment.

allies.zh: 阿瑜陀耶在1648年并无近代意义上的固定盟友体系，但与波斯商人网络、部分马来港口、荷兰东印度公司及若干区域属邦保持务实合作关系，并与中国、日本维持重要贸易联系
allies.en: Ayutthaya did not possess a fixed alliance system in the modern sense in 1648, but maintained pragmatic cooperation with Persian merchant networks, certain Malay ports, the Dutch East India Company, and various regional dependencies, while preserving important trade links with China and Japan

enemies.zh: 主要对手为缅甸东吁政权及在高棉、老挝与半岛地区与暹罗争夺影响力的地方势力；与欧洲人关系以竞争和交易并存，而非全面敌对
enemies.en: The principal rival was the Taungoo Burmese state, along with local powers contesting Siamese influence in Khmer, Lao, and peninsular regions; relations with Europeans mixed competition and commerce rather than outright enmity

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以平衡贸易利益、维持区域朝贡秩序、控制半岛港口和吸纳外来商人为核心。阿瑜陀耶善于同时与亚洲和欧洲多方往来，通过外交接待、贸易特许与势力均衡维护自主地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on balancing trade interests, maintaining a regional tributary order, controlling peninsular ports, and incorporating foreign merchant communities. Ayutthaya excelled at dealing simultaneously with Asian and European powers, using diplomatic reception, trading privileges, and balance-of-power tactics to preserve autonomy.

era.zh: 近世早期

keyInnovations.zh: 稻作水利与河运体系成熟，造船与航海技术发达，城防和铸炮技术吸收欧亚多方影响，王都文书管理、仓储物流和国际商业中介能力在东南亚处于先进水平
keyInnovations.en: Wet-rice irrigation and river transport systems were mature, shipbuilding and navigation were well developed, and fortification and cannon-casting absorbed influences from across Eurasia; the capital's record-keeping, warehouse logistics, and international commercial brokerage were advanced by Southeast Asian standards

strengths.zh: 阿瑜陀耶的优势在于富庶的稻米腹地、活跃的国际贸易、都城的多族裔商业网络、较强的王权号召力以及能够整合内陆与海上资源的战略位置。
strengths.en: Ayutthaya's strengths lay in its rich rice hinterland, vibrant international trade, multiethnic commercial networks in the capital, relatively strong royal authority, and strategic position linking inland and maritime resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括地方控制依赖附庸和人身征发、宫廷权力斗争频繁、继承机制不稳、对贸易收入波动敏感，以及在长期大战中后勤和地方忠诚度容易受损。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included dependence on tributary control and manpower levies, frequent court factional struggles, unstable succession practices, sensitivity to fluctuations in trade income, and vulnerability of logistics and provincial loyalty in prolonged wars.

outlook.zh: 1648年的阿瑜陀耶处于强盛与繁荣阶段，短期内仍将是东南亚最重要的王国之一。若能维持宫廷稳定并平衡缅甸、半岛港口与外商势力，其国力可继续增长；但一旦继承危机与边疆战争叠加，繁荣也可能迅速转为脆弱。
outlook.en: In 1648 Ayutthaya stood in a phase of strength and prosperity and was likely to remain one of Southeast Asia's most important kingdoms in the near term. If it maintained court stability and balanced Burma, the peninsular ports, and foreign merchant interests, its power could continue to grow; but if succession crises and frontier wars coincided, prosperity could quickly turn fragile.

description.zh: 阿瑜陀耶是东南亚重要贸易与外交中心，与亚洲及欧洲势力广泛往来。
description.en: Ayutthaya was a major Southeast Asian center of trade and diplomacy with broad links to Asian and European powers.

ruler.zh: 苏塔姆拉（阇耶陀诃阇·辛哈）

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制下的贵族-官僚王国，以阿洪宫廷、派克劳役制度与地方长官体系为支柱
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy with an aristocratic-bureaucratic structure, centered on the Ahom court, the paik corvée system, and provincial officers

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、世袭贵族、大官、军事与行政服役的派克人口、祭司群体、工匠、商人及被征服或归附族群构成；国家通过服役编户组织社会而非纯粹按土地贵族划分
