rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以驻莫桑比克岛的葡萄牙总督、军官、王室财政官和大商人为核心。若干获封的葡裔或本地化庄园主也在赞比西河流域形成地方性权势。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Portuguese governor on Mozambique Island, military officers, crown fiscal officials, and major merchants. Some granted prazeiro estate-holders of Portuguese or Luso-African background also formed local power centers in the Zambezi basin.

succession.zh: 总督并非世袭，而由里斯本或葡属印度总督体系任命，任期通常有限。地方庄园与商贸网络则常通过家族继承与婚姻联盟延续影响力。
succession.en: The governor was not hereditary but appointed through Lisbon or the Estado da Índia hierarchy, usually for a limited term. Local estates and trading networks often passed influence through family inheritance and marriage alliances.

structure.zh: 行政中心位于莫桑比克岛，名义上隶属葡属印度的广泛海上帝国体系。实际统治重点在沿海港口、要塞和若干河口贸易站，对内陆多依靠盟约、贡赋和武装贸易维系影响。宗教机构在教育、登记和社会控制中亦发挥重要作用。
structure.en: The administrative center was on Mozambique Island, nominally within the wider Estado da Índia. In practice, rule focused on coastal ports, forts, and a few riverine trading posts, while influence inland depended on alliances, tribute, and armed commerce. Religious institutions also played an important role in education, record-keeping, and social control.

function.zh: 处理行政命令、外交往来、任命与港口治理。
function.en: Handled executive orders, diplomacy, appointments, and port administration.

function.zh: 征收关税、贸易税、贡赋并管理王室专卖收益。
function.en: Collected customs duties, trade taxes, tribute, and managed crown monopoly revenues.

function.zh: 负责岛上要塞、防务、舰船补给和对沿海据点的军事协调。
function.en: Oversaw fortress defense, garrisons, naval provisioning, and military coordination of coastal posts.

function.zh: 管理教产、传教活动、婚姻登记及部分教育慈善事务。
function.en: Managed church properties, missionary work, marriage registration, and parts of education and charity.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠港口长官、要塞指挥官和受封庄园主执行。对非洲内陆社群多通过通事、盟约首领和贡赋关系间接管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on port captains, fortress commanders, and granted estate-holders. Inland African communities were governed indirectly through interpreters, allied chiefs, and tribute relationships.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以葡萄牙王室法令、殖民条例和教会法为基础，在葡人据点内执行较严。涉及本地社群时，往往混用葡式司法、习惯法与权宜仲裁。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Portuguese royal law, colonial ordinances, and canon law, enforced most strictly in Portuguese enclaves. In dealings with local communities, Portuguese justice was often mixed with customary law and pragmatic arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自港口关税、象牙和奴隶贸易税、许可费及属地贡赋。内陆直接税征收能力有限，更多依靠贸易节点控制获取收益。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from port customs, taxes on ivory and slave trade, licensing fees, and tribute from dependent areas. Capacity for direct inland taxation was limited, so income relied heavily on controlling trade nodes.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 莫桑比克岛形成了葡萄牙、斯瓦希里与非洲沿海文化交汇的港口社会，建筑上出现石造教堂、仓库与要塞。传教活动推动了部分地区的文字记录和宗教艺术传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Mozambique Island developed into a port society where Portuguese, Swahili, and African coastal cultures interacted, visible in stone churches, warehouses, and fortifications. Missionary activity encouraged limited literacy and the spread of religious art in some regions.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系罗曼语族（官方葡萄牙语）与尼日尔-刚果语系班图诸语并存。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European Romance Portuguese coexisted with Niger-Congo Bantu languages.

philosophy.zh: 以天主教世界观、王权忠诚和海上帝国商贸实用主义为主，并与斯瓦希里-班图地方传统相互交织。
philosophy.en: Dominated by a Catholic worldview, loyalty to monarchy, and the practical mercantile ethos of a maritime empire, interwoven with local Swahili-Bantu traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 官方主要使用葡萄牙文拉丁字母；教会记录亦使用拉丁文，本地口述传统十分重要。
writingSystem.en: Official administration used Portuguese in the Latin alphabet; church records also used Latin, while local oral traditions remained highly important.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量不大，但依赖转口贸易而非单纯农业生产。莫桑比克岛与赞比西流域的象牙、黄金、奴隶和印度洋航运构成其核心价值。
gdpDescription.en: The overall economy was modest, but disproportionately dependent on entrepôt trade rather than agriculture alone. Mozambique Island and the Zambezi-linked trade in ivory, gold, slaves, and Indian Ocean shipping created most of its value.

mainIndustries.zh: 转口贸易、象牙贸易、黄金交换、奴隶贩运、港口服务、渔业、椰子与粮食生产
mainIndustries.en: Entrepôt trade, ivory commerce, gold exchange, slave trading, port services, fishing, and coconut and grain production

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、黄金、奴隶、琥珀、木材、米、椰产品、印度棉布、珠饰与金属器
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, gold, slaves, ambergris, timber, rice, coconut products, Indian cotton textiles, beads, and metalware

name.zh: 葡萄牙雷亚尔与西班牙银元并用
name.en: Portuguese real and Spanish silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 沿海葡裔商人和中介家族财富明显高于普通渔民、农民与奴隶人口。多数家庭处于低积累状态，财富集中于贸易网络、船只、仓储和人身依附关系。
householdWealth.en: Coastal Portuguese and intermediary trading families were substantially wealthier than ordinary fishers, cultivators, and enslaved people. Most households had little accumulated wealth, which was concentrated in trade networks, vessels, storage, and bonded labor.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 属殖民港口型经济，结合王室特许贸易、私人商贸和地方贡赋网络。对外高度依赖印度洋航线与葡萄牙帝国的海上联系。
economicSystem.en: It was a colonial port economy combining crown-chartered trade, private commerce, and local tribute networks. It depended heavily on Indian Ocean routes and maritime links within the Portuguese Empire.

source.en: Taxes on ivory and slave trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏规模有限，常受航运延误、战时调拨和地方贪腐影响。现金与货物库存并存，流动性并不稳定。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in size and often affected by shipping delays, wartime diversion, and local corruption. It consisted of both cash and stored goods, so liquidity was not always stable.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持港口、驻军和贸易税源，而非大规模内陆开发。必要时通过提高关税、租税承包和临时征发弥补短缺。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining ports, garrisons, and customs revenues rather than extensive inland development. Shortfalls were addressed through higher duties, tax farming, and occasional extraordinary levies.

description.zh: 由葡萄牙士兵、混血民兵和受雇本地兵组成，守备要塞与港口。
description.en: Composed of Portuguese soldiers, mixed-race militia, and hired local troops, defending forts and ports.

description.zh: 负责近海航线护卫、缉私和港口联络，多使用轻型船只。
description.en: Guarded coastal routes, suppressed smuggling, and linked ports using mostly light craft.

description.zh: 来自赞比西庄园和地方盟友酋长的辅助武装，用于内陆护路与突袭。
description.en: Auxiliary forces from Zambezi estates and allied chiefs, used for inland escort and raiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 葡萄牙总督兼莫桑比克军事长官
commanderInChief.en: Portuguese Governor and Military Commander of Mozambique

technology.zh: 装备火绳枪、轻型火炮、堡垒炮台和小型武装帆船，技术水平高于周边多数地方势力，但兵员与补给不足。海防和据点防御优于野战能力。
technology.en: Armed with matchlocks, light artillery, fortress batteries, and small armed sailing craft, it was technologically superior to many nearby powers but constrained by manpower and supply. Coastal defense and fortified positions were stronger than field warfare capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自荷兰海上竞争、阿曼与斯瓦希里海岸势力、内陆非洲政权的反抗，以及长距离补给线脆弱。若印度洋战局恶化，沿海据点极易被孤立。
threats.en: Major threats came from Dutch maritime competition, Omani and Swahili coastal forces, resistance from inland African polities, and fragile long-distance supply lines. If the Indian Ocean strategic situation worsened, coastal outposts could be easily isolated.

populationDescription.zh: 人口绝大多数为非洲本地居民，葡萄牙人和其他欧亚裔定居者只占极小比例。人口主要分布于沿海聚落、河口据点和赞比西流域村社。
populationDescription.en: The overwhelming majority of the population was African, while Portuguese and other Eurasian settlers formed only a small minority. Population was concentrated in coastal settlements, river-mouth posts, and villages in the Zambezi basin.

socialClasses.zh: 葡萄牙官员与商人、教士与庄园主、混血中间阶层、自由渔民与农民、工匠与港口劳工、受役与奴隶人口。
socialClasses.en: Portuguese officials and merchants, clergy and estate-holders, mixed intermediary groups, free fishers and cultivators, artisans and port laborers, and dependent or enslaved populations.

allies.zh: 名义上与葡萄牙帝国其他属地、部分赞比西流域庄园主及若干沿海和内陆盟约酋长合作。
allies.en: Nominally allied with other Portuguese imperial possessions, some Zambezi estate-holders, and several coastal and inland allied chiefs.

enemies.zh: 荷兰东印度公司、敌对斯瓦希里港邦、部分阿曼支持的海岸势力，以及间歇反抗的内陆首领。
enemies.en: The Dutch East India Company, hostile Swahili port polities, some Omani-backed coastal forces, and intermittently resisting inland chiefs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保住航线、控制港口中转和维系地方盟约为先。其目标不是全面征服内陆，而是确保贸易利润与帝国海权节点安全。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized securing sea lanes, controlling port transit, and maintaining local alliances. The goal was not full inland conquest but the protection of trade profits and imperial maritime nodes.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶印度洋火器海贸时代
era.en: Mid-17th-century Indian Ocean gunpowder maritime age

keyInnovations.zh: 石砌海港要塞、远洋帆船网络、火绳枪与舰炮结合的港防体系，以及以书面账册和海关凭证支撑的跨洋贸易管理。
keyInnovations.en: Stone-built harbor fortifications, oceanic sailing networks, port-defense systems combining matchlocks and naval artillery, and transoceanic trade management supported by written ledgers and customs documentation.

strengths.zh: 地处东非与印度洋贸易关键节点，具备港口、要塞和远洋网络优势。虽然人口不多，但能借海上联系放大影响力。
strengths.en: It occupied a key node between East Africa and the Indian Ocean trade world, with advantages in ports, fortifications, and long-distance maritime links. Though not populous, it could magnify its influence through seaborne connections.

weaknesses.zh: 控制范围碎片化，内陆统治薄弱，财政依赖贸易与外来补给。驻军规模有限，难以同时应对海上强敌和地方叛乱。
weaknesses.en: Its control was fragmented, inland authority weak, and finances dependent on trade and external supply. Garrison strength was limited, making it difficult to face both maritime rivals and local uprisings simultaneously.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍可维持沿海据点和贸易收益，但若荷兰与阿曼压力上升，其区域地位将继续受挤压。长期前景取决于帝国海军支援和对赞比西流域的实际整合能力。
outlook.en: In the short term it could still maintain coastal outposts and trade revenue, but increasing Dutch and Omani pressure would continue to squeeze its regional position. Long-term prospects depended on imperial naval support and real integration of the Zambezi zone.

description.zh: 1648年的葡属东非是一个以莫桑比克岛为核心、依靠港口贸易与要塞维系的葡萄牙沿海殖民据点体系。
description.en: In 1648, Portuguese East Africa was a Portuguese coastal colonial network centered on Mozambique Island and sustained by port trade and fortified outposts.

dynasty.zh: 葡萄牙布拉干萨王朝属下几内亚海岸据点
dynasty.en: Portuguese Guinea Coast settlements under the House of Braganza

governmentForm.zh: 由葡萄牙王室授权总督或驻地官员管理的松散海岸殖民贸易据点，以堡垒、商站和盟约关系为基础。
governmentForm.en: A loosely held coastal colonial trading zone administered by Portuguese crown-appointed officials, based on forts, trading posts, and alliance networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会主要由本地曼丁卡、巴兰特、帕佩尔等族群构成，葡萄牙官员、商人和少量混血中间人集中于海岸贸易点。沿海居民与内陆社群通过奴隶、盐、布匹和粮食交换形成复杂联系。教会影响存在，但远弱于城市化较高的葡属亚洲据点。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed mainly of local Mandinka, Balanta, Papel, and related peoples, while Portuguese officials, merchants, and a small mixed intermediary group clustered in coastal trading points. Coastal residents and inland communities were tied through exchanges in slaves, salt, cloth, and foodstuffs. Church influence existed but was much weaker than in more urbanized Portuguese Asian settlements.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以葡萄牙驻地官员、堡垒指挥者、商人和获得特许的贸易承包人为主。地方上则必须依赖非洲首领与通婚形成的中介家族合作。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Portuguese local officials, fort commanders, merchants, and licensed trade contractors. At the local level, they depended heavily on African chiefs and intermediary families linked by marriage.

succession.zh: 总督或主要官员由葡萄牙王室委任，并无本地世袭制度。贸易影响力常通过商人家族和地方盟约关系延续。
succession.en: The governor or principal officials were appointed by the Portuguese crown rather than through local hereditary succession. Commercial influence often continued through merchant families and local alliance ties.

structure.zh: 该地并非高度整合的领土殖民地，而是若干河口和海湾据点的集合。葡萄牙统治重心在贸易监管、维持驻军、争取本地首领合作，以及防止其他欧洲商人夺取市场。行政权力覆盖狭窄，离开据点后大多需依赖协商。
structure.en: This was not a highly integrated territorial colony but a collection of posts along estuaries and bays. Portuguese rule focused on trade supervision, maintaining a garrison, securing cooperation from local chiefs, and preventing rival Europeans from taking markets. Administrative reach was narrow, and beyond the posts authority depended largely on negotiation.

function.zh: 发布命令、管理文书、处理与本地首领及外商的往来。
function.en: Issued orders, maintained records, and handled dealings with local chiefs and foreign traders.

function.zh: 征收港口税、贸易许可费和货物流转税。
function.en: Collected port duties, trading license fees, and taxes on goods in transit.

function.zh: 负责商站防御、治安和小型海岸巡逻。
function.en: Handled factory defense, local security, and small-scale coastal patrols.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依托商站代理人、翻译和与本地酋长的协议。葡萄牙很少直接管理村社，而是通过贸易利益和礼物外交维持影响。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on trading-post agents, interpreters, and agreements with local chiefs. The Portuguese rarely governed villages directly, instead maintaining influence through trade benefits and gift diplomacy.

legalSystem.zh: 在葡萄牙据点内实行王室法令、商法惯例和教会登记制度。对本地居民案件常以习惯法和临时仲裁处理，实际司法高度弹性。
legalSystem.en: Within Portuguese posts, royal ordinances, mercantile custom, and church registration applied. Cases involving local residents were often handled by customary law and ad hoc arbitration, making actual justice highly flexible.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、奴隶和货物交易税、许可费为主。由于缺乏深入内陆的征税机构，王室财政高度依赖沿海交换。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested mainly on port customs, taxes on slave and goods trade, and licensing fees. Because inland fiscal institutions were lacking, crown revenue depended heavily on coastal exchange.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 几内亚海岸形成了跨文化贸易中介群体，促进了语言、服饰和物质文化的混合。港口礼仪、商业账册和通婚网络构成其最重要的文化特征。
culturalAchievements.en: The Guinea coast fostered cross-cultural intermediary communities that blended language, dress, and material culture. Port etiquette, commercial ledgers, and intermarriage networks were among its most important cultural features.

languageFamily.zh: 官方为印欧语系罗曼语族葡萄牙语，本地多属尼日尔-刚果语系曼德语支与大西洋语支语言。
languageFamily.en: Officially Portuguese of the Indo-European Romance branch; local languages belonged largely to Niger-Congo Mande and Atlantic branches.

philosophy.zh: 官方意识形态为天主教王权与商业实用主义，本地社会则保持西非多元宗教与族群传统。
philosophy.en: Official ideology combined Catholic monarchy with mercantile pragmatism, while local society retained diverse West African religious and ethnic traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 官方记录使用葡萄牙文拉丁字母，教会文书偶用拉丁文；多数本地政治与社会记忆依靠口述传统。
writingSystem.en: Official records used Portuguese in the Latin alphabet, with occasional Latin in church documentation; most local political and social memory remained oral.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，主要以沿海贸易和周边农业供给为支撑。其价值不在大规模生产，而在连接西非内陆与大西洋商路的中介功能。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small and sustained mainly by coastal trade and surrounding agricultural supply. Its value lay not in large-scale production but in mediating between the West African interior and Atlantic trade routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 奴隶贸易、中介贸易、稻米与杂粮生产、渔业、盐业、木材与船只补给
mainIndustries.en: Slave trading, intermediary commerce, rice and grain cultivation, fishing, salt production, timber, and shipping supply

tradeGoods.zh: 奴隶、象牙少量、蜂蜡、皮革、稻米、盐、布匹、酒类、金属器与珠饰
tradeGoods.en: Slaves, small quantities of ivory, beeswax, hides, rice, salt, cloth, alcohol, metalware, and beads

name.zh: 雷亚尔与实物交换并行

householdWealth.zh: 财富高度集中于贸易中介、奴隶商和与葡萄牙往来密切的家族。普通农渔家庭财富有限，多以土地使用权、粮食储备和亲族关系体现。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was highly concentrated among trade intermediaries, slave dealers, and families closely connected to the Portuguese. Ordinary farming and fishing households had little accumulated wealth, expressed mainly in land-use rights, food reserves, and kinship ties.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 属海岸商站型殖民经济，以有限行政、贸易许可和地方首领合作为运作核心。实物交换和银币流通并存，市场制度较为碎片化。
economicSystem.en: It was a coastal factory-based colonial economy centered on limited administration, trade licensing, and cooperation with local chiefs. Barter and silver coin circulation coexisted, and market institutions were fragmented.

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏规模偏小，常依赖年度航运与商税入账。由于贸易季节性明显，现金储备常不足，只能靠货物库存与信用支撑运营。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was small and depended on annual shipping and customs receipts. Because trade was strongly seasonal, cash reserves were often thin and operations were supported by stored goods and credit.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在压缩驻防成本并最大化贸易税收。为稳住与本地首领的关系，官府常以减税、礼品或特许换取市场准入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on minimizing garrison costs while maximizing trade revenue. To preserve relations with local chiefs, officials often exchanged tax reductions, gifts, or privileges for market access.

description.zh: 负责堡垒和仓储区防御，武器以火绳枪和少量火炮为主。
description.en: Defended forts and warehouse areas, armed mainly with matchlocks and a few guns.

description.zh: 乘小型船只巡查河口和近海，兼顾通信与缉私。
description.en: Used small craft to patrol estuaries and nearby waters, handling communications and anti-smuggling duties.

description.zh: 由合作首领提供，战时用于警戒、护送和有限作战。
description.en: Provided by cooperating chiefs and used in wartime for watch duty, escort, and limited combat.

commanderInChief.zh: 葡属几内亚总督兼堡垒司令
commanderInChief.en: Governor of Portuguese Guinea and Fort Commander

technology.zh: 军事技术以火绳枪、轻炮、木石混合堡垒和小型沿海帆船为主，适合守点不适合远征。战斗力受热带环境、疾病和补给不足严重限制。
technology.en: Military technology centered on matchlocks, light artillery, mixed wood-and-stone fortifications, and small coastal sailing craft, suitable for static defense rather than expeditionary warfare. Effectiveness was heavily limited by tropical disease, climate, and supply shortages.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括英、法、荷等欧洲商人竞争，本地首领反复变动的同盟关系，以及疾病导致的兵员损耗。其据点若失去海运支持，防务将迅速恶化。
threats.en: Main threats included competition from English, French, and Dutch traders, shifting alliances among local chiefs, and manpower losses from disease. If maritime support failed, the defense of its posts would deteriorate quickly.

populationDescription.zh: 人口绝大多数为西非本地社群，葡萄牙人与混血商人仅占小部分。城市化程度很低，人口多分散在河口村落、农田与集市周边。
populationDescription.en: The overwhelming majority of the population belonged to West African local communities, while Portuguese and mixed merchants were only a small minority. Urbanization was very low, with people dispersed among estuary villages, farmlands, and market sites.

socialClasses.zh: 葡萄牙官员与商人、混血中介家族、本地首领与武士、自由农渔民、市场商贩、奴隶与受役人口。
socialClasses.en: Portuguese officials and merchants, mixed intermediary families, local chiefs and warriors, free farmers and fishers, market traders, and enslaved or dependent populations.

allies.zh: 依赖葡萄牙帝国支持，并与若干沿海和河口首领维持交易同盟。
allies.en: It relied on support from the Portuguese Empire and maintained trading alliances with several coastal and estuarine chiefs.

enemies.zh: 敌对力量包括竞争性欧洲商人、拒绝葡萄牙贸易条件的本地政体，以及海盗与走私集团。
enemies.en: Hostile forces included rival European traders, local polities rejecting Portuguese trade terms, and pirate or smuggling groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保住奴隶和沿海贸易份额、维系本地合作关系、避免高成本征服为核心。其外交实质上是商业谈判与武力威慑并用。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving the slave and coastal trade share, sustaining local partnerships, and avoiding costly conquest. In practice, its diplomacy combined commercial bargaining with limited coercive force.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶大西洋商站与火器贸易时代
era.en: Mid-17th-century Atlantic factory and gunpowder trade age

keyInnovations.zh: 河口商站网络、账册化港口贸易管理、小型火器守备体系，以及利用跨文化中介维持远距离商业联系的制度。
keyInnovations.en: Estuarine factory networks, ledger-based port trade management, small-scale gunpowder garrison systems, and institutions using cross-cultural intermediaries to sustain long-distance commerce.

strengths.zh: 虽规模有限，但能利用海岸贸易节点和与本地首领的关系获得收益。行政成本较低，适合维持小型但灵活的殖民据点。
strengths.en: Though small in scale, it profited from coastal trade nodes and relationships with local chiefs. Administrative costs were relatively low, making it suitable for maintaining a small but flexible colonial foothold.

weaknesses.zh: 领土控制极弱，财政依赖单一贸易，军事力量不足以深入内陆。疾病、高死亡率和欧洲竞争使其长期稳定性脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Territorial control was extremely weak, finances depended on narrow trade channels, and military strength was insufficient for inland expansion. Disease, high mortality, and European competition made long-term stability fragile.

outlook.zh: 只要大西洋贸易继续繁荣，该地仍能维持基本收益；但其命运主要取决于欧洲海上竞争和本地盟约是否持续。若贸易格局变化，其据点可能迅速边缘化。
outlook.en: As long as Atlantic trade remained profitable, the territory could maintain basic revenues; however, its fate depended largely on European maritime competition and the durability of local alliances. If trade patterns shifted, its posts could quickly become marginal.

description.zh: 1648年的葡属几内亚是一个沿西非海岸分布、以商站和奴隶贸易为核心的葡萄牙小型殖民据点体系。
description.en: In 1648, Portuguese Guinea was a small Portuguese colonial network of coastal trading posts on West Africa centered on commerce and the slave trade.

dynasty.zh: 明清易代时期的新安县香港沿海村社
dynasty.en: Coastal village communities of Hong Kong in Xin'an County during the Ming-Qing transition

governmentForm.zh: 由村中长老、宗族首领和保甲基层组织共同管理的地方村社自治结构，名义上隶属中国县级行政体系。
governmentForm.en: A local village self-governing structure managed by elders, lineage heads, and baojia-style grassroots organization, nominally under the Chinese county administration.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以渔民、盐户、樵采者和少量农户为主，组织基础是宗族、乡里和渔业合作关系。人口规模很小，聚落分散，生活高度依赖海湾、滩涂和山地资源。1648年正值明清鼎革余波，沿海社会对战乱和海禁政策十分敏感。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed mainly of fishers, salt workers, woodcutters, and a small number of cultivators, organized through lineage, neighborhood, and fishing cooperation. The population was very small, settlements dispersed, and livelihoods depended heavily on bays, tidal flats, and hill resources. In 1648, the area was still affected by the Ming-Qing transition and was highly sensitive to warfare and coastal restrictions.

rulingClass.zh: 并不存在独立王公贵族，地方权威主要掌握在宗族长老、富裕渔户和与县衙有联系的乡绅式人物手中。若遇动荡，海上武装头目也可能暂时左右局势。
rulingClass.en: There was no independent princely nobility; local authority rested mainly with lineage elders, prosperous fishing households, and gentry-like figures connected to the county yamen. In times of unrest, maritime armed leaders could also temporarily shape events.

succession.zh: 村社领袖通常不采取正式世袭君主制，而由宗族资望、财力和年龄决定影响力。家族内部长支与族老会议往往决定实际继承和领导权分配。
succession.en: Village leadership did not follow a formal hereditary monarchy but depended on lineage prestige, wealth, and seniority. In practice, major family branches and councils of elders decided succession and leadership distribution.

structure.zh: 香港地区在1648年并非独立政权，而是广东新安县下属的若干渔村、墟市和海湾聚落。日常事务由村老和族长处理，涉及赋役、治安与诉讼时则需向县级官府报告。由于地处海隅，实际治理弹性较大，官方控制并不总是深入。
structure.en: In 1648 Hong Kong was not an independent state but a set of fishing villages, small market points, and coastal settlements within Xin'an County, Guangdong. Daily affairs were handled by village elders and lineage heads, while taxation, security, and lawsuits ultimately went to county authorities. Because of its remote maritime setting, actual governance was flexible and official control was not always deep.

name.en: Village Covenant and Council of Elders

function.zh: 协调村务、祭祀、纠纷调解与公共劳役。
function.en: Coordinated village affairs, ritual observances, dispute mediation, and communal labor.

function.zh: 负责夜间警戒、海盗预警和与上级官府联络。
function.en: Handled night watch, pirate warnings, and communication with higher authorities.

localAdmin.zh: 基层管理以宗族与保甲为主，里甲、乡约和族规在实际生活中比远方官府更常见。官府主要在征粮、治安和海禁执行上发挥作用。
localAdmin.en: Grassroots administration relied on lineage structures and baojia arrangements; village compacts and clan rules were more visible in daily life than distant state authority. The government intervened mainly in grain tax collection, security, and enforcement of maritime restrictions.

legalSystem.zh: 名义上适用明末至清初中国成文律例及县衙司法程序。实际纠纷多先由族老、乡约调解，重大案件再上报县官处理。
legalSystem.en: Nominally the area followed late Ming to early Qing Chinese written law and county judicial procedure. In practice, most disputes were first mediated by elders and village compacts, with serious cases reported to the county magistrate.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以田赋、渔盐相关摊派和杂役为主，规模不大。沿海战乱与海禁政策会影响税收稳定性，有时出现逃散或减收。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation consisted mainly of land tax, levies related to fishing and salt activity, and miscellaneous labor dues, all on a small scale. Coastal warfare and maritime restriction policies affected revenue stability, sometimes causing flight or reduced collection.

religion.zh: 中国民间宗教
religion.en: Chinese folk religion

culturalAchievements.zh: 虽然尚无大型城市文化，但沿海村社已形成祭海、祠堂祭祖和渔业节令等稳定习俗。简朴的庙宇、祠堂和墟市网络构成其地方文化核心。
culturalAchievements.en: Although it lacked major urban culture, coastal villages had already developed stable customs such as sea rites, ancestral hall worship, and fishing seasonal observances. Modest temples, lineage halls, and market networks formed the core of local culture.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系汉语族，以粤语前身及周边粤东、客家接触方言为主。
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Sinitic branch, with early Cantonese forms and contact dialects from eastern Guangdong and Hakka-speaking areas.

philosophy.zh: 文化思想以华南民间信仰、祖先崇拜、儒家伦理和海神信仰并存为特征。
philosophy.en: Its cultural outlook combined South China folk religion, ancestor veneration, Confucian ethics, and reverence for sea deities.

writingSystem.zh: 使用汉字，文书多为文言格式，民间口语则接近粤语及周边方言。
writingSystem.en: Chinese characters were used, with documents generally written in literary Chinese, while spoken language resembled Cantonese and neighboring dialects.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模很小，主要依赖渔业、采盐、烧炭、樵采和有限的沿海小贸易。其价值在于海湾避风、渔获和珠江口周边交通联系，而非成熟城市市场。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was very small, relying mainly on fishing, salt making, charcoal burning, woodcutting, and limited coastal petty trade. Its value lay in sheltered anchorages, fish catch, and links around the Pearl River estuary rather than in an urban market economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、晒盐、薪炭、樵采、少量稻作与海上小贩贸易
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, salt production, fuel and charcoal, woodcutting, small-scale rice cultivation, and petty maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 鲜鱼、咸鱼、盐、蚝贝、木柴、木炭、稻米少量、陶器与日用杂货
tradeGoods.en: Fresh fish, salted fish, salt, oysters and shellfish, firewood, charcoal, small amounts of rice, pottery, and daily goods

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富有限，以渔船、网具、盐田份额和祖产房舍为主。少数富裕渔户或墟市商贩拥有较多船只和储货能力，但总体没有明显的大地主阶层。
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed little wealth, mainly in the form of fishing boats, nets, shares in salt pans, and inherited dwellings. A few prosperous fishing families or market traders owned more boats and storage, but there was no pronounced large landlord class overall.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 属于华南沿海自然经济与小市场交换并存的村社经济，受县级赋税和海禁政策影响。商业化程度有限，但与珠江口渔盐网络保持联系。
economicSystem.en: It was a South China coastal village economy combining subsistence production with small-market exchange, shaped by county taxation and maritime restriction policies. Commercialization was limited but linked to fishing and salt networks in the Pearl River estuary.

treasuryDescription.zh: 地方可支配储备很少，多数资源分散在宗族、庙产和私人储粮中。遇台风、海盗或禁海政策时，财政缓冲能力较弱。
treasuryDescription.en: Disposable local reserves were very limited, with most resources dispersed among lineages, temple property, and private grain stores. Fiscal resilience was weak in the face of typhoons, piracy, or maritime prohibition.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持基本治安、赋役征收和公共修缮为主，尽量避免过度加派。若遇战乱或荒歉，通常依靠宗族互助、减缓征收或临时摊派应对。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining basic security, collecting dues, and funding public repairs while trying to avoid excessive burdens. In times of conflict or scarcity, communities relied on lineage mutual aid, delayed collection, or temporary levies.

description.zh: 由渔民和壮丁组成，负责村落警戒、海盗预警和简易守御。
description.en: Made up of fishers and able-bodied men, responsible for village watch, pirate warning, and simple defense.

description.zh: 利用渔船改装的小艇巡查近岸水域，传递消息并防范劫掠。
description.en: Used adapted fishing boats to watch nearby waters, relay messages, and deter raiding.

commanderInChief.zh: 村社长老与保甲首人，受新安县官府节制
commanderInChief.en: Village elders and baojia heads under the authority of Xin'an County officials

technology.zh: 武备十分简陋，以刀矛、弓、少量火器和民用船只为主。其军事能力仅足以应对小规模海盗袭扰或村际冲突，无法进行正规作战。
technology.en: Armament was very rudimentary, consisting of blades, spears, bows, a few firearms, and civilian boats. Military capability was sufficient only for small-scale pirate defense or village conflicts, not for regular warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自海盗、沿海战乱余波、海禁引发的人口迁移和台风等自然灾害。若珠江口局势紧张，这些小聚落很难独立自保。
threats.en: Main threats came from piracy, the aftereffects of coastal warfare, migration caused by maritime bans, and natural disasters such as typhoons. If tensions rose in the Pearl River estuary, these small settlements could not defend themselves independently.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少且分散，多为沿海渔村居民与少量山地农户。居民流动性较高，部分人随渔汛迁徙，部分因战乱与海禁而迁出迁入。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and dispersed, consisting mostly of coastal fishing-village residents and a small number of upland cultivators. Mobility was relatively high, with some moving seasonally with fishing cycles and others relocating because of warfare and maritime restrictions.

socialClasses.zh: 宗族长老与较富渔户、普通渔民与农户、盐户与樵采者、小商贩、佃户与雇工。
socialClasses.en: Lineage elders and relatively prosperous fishing households, ordinary fishers and cultivators, salt workers and woodcutters, petty traders, and tenants or hired laborers.

allies.zh: 无独立外交，主要依附新安县及更高层广东地方政权，并与邻近村社维持互助关系。
allies.en: It had no independent diplomacy, relying chiefly on Xin'an County and higher Guangdong authorities while maintaining mutual aid with neighboring villages.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家级敌人，但常受海盗、流寇和战乱波及。
enemies.en: It had no fixed state-level enemies, but was often threatened by pirates, bandits, and the spillover of warfare.

foreignPolicy.zh: 严格说并无独立对外政策，地方优先目标是避乱、保渔、守村并与上级官府保持顺从。对外来船只通常采取谨慎交易与防范并行的态度。
foreignPolicy.en: Strictly speaking it had no independent foreign policy; local priorities were to avoid disorder, preserve fishing livelihoods, defend the villages, and remain compliant with higher authorities. Toward outside vessels, communities generally combined cautious trade with vigilance.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶华南沿海村社与帆船渔业时代
era.en: Mid-17th-century South China coastal village and sailing-fishery era

keyInnovations.zh: 小型帆船渔业、晒盐技术、宗族组织下的水利与公共修缮，以及在有限范围内使用火器进行海防警戒。
keyInnovations.en: Small sailing-fishery techniques, salt-pan production, lineage-based management of waterworks and public repair, and limited use of firearms for coastal watch.

strengths.zh: 地形多湾多岛，适合渔业、生计自给和小规模避风贸易。宗族与村社组织紧密，具备一定互助和灾后恢复能力。
strengths.en: Its many bays and islands suited fishing, subsistence livelihoods, and small sheltered trade. Lineage and village institutions were cohesive, offering some mutual aid and post-disaster resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 人口和资源都极其有限，缺乏城镇市场、正规军和独立财政。对战乱、海禁、风灾和海盗都十分脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Population and resources were extremely limited, with no urban market, regular army, or independent fiscal base. It was highly vulnerable to warfare, maritime bans, storms, and piracy.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍将维持渔村聚落形态，前景取决于广东沿海秩序是否恢复。若区域贸易重新活跃，其港湾价值可能上升，但1648年尚远未形成重要城市。
outlook.en: In the short term it would remain a cluster of fishing settlements, with prospects depending on the restoration of order along the Guangdong coast. If regional trade revived, the value of its harbors might rise, but in 1648 it was still far from becoming a major city.

description.zh: 1648年的香港只是广东新安县辖下人口稀少的沿海渔村群落，以渔业、盐业和宗族自治维持生计。
description.en: In 1648, Hong Kong was merely a sparsely populated group of coastal fishing villages within Xin'an County, sustained by fishing, salt making, and lineage-based self-government.

dynasty.zh: 丁纳瓦卡王朝后期之康提王室
dynasty.en: Late Kandyan royal line following the Dinawaka tradition

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心、依托贵族与寺院网络运作的僧伽罗佛教山地君主制。
governmentForm.en: A Sinhalese Buddhist highland monarchy centered on the king and sustained by noble and temple networks.

socialStructure.zh: 康提社会以国王、宫廷贵族、地方酋长、寺院僧侣与农民村社为主要层级。土地占有、服役义务与种姓性职业分工紧密相连，农村社会在稻作、贡赋与劳役制度下组织。佛教寺院既是宗教中心，也是教育、文化与地方权威的重要支柱。
socialStructure.en: Kandyan society was structured around the king, court nobles, provincial chiefs, monastic clergy, and peasant village communities. Landholding, service obligations, and caste-based occupational roles were closely linked, and rural society was organized through rice cultivation, tribute, and labor dues. Buddhist monasteries served not only as religious centers but also as pillars of education, culture, and local authority.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、阿迪加尔等高官、地方迪萨瓦与显贵家族构成。高地贵族通过对土地、劳役和军役的控制维系统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, high officers such as the Adigars, provincial dissavas, and prominent noble families. Highland nobles maintained power through control of land, labor obligations, and military service.

succession.zh: 王位继承原则上由王族内部承袭，但实际常受宫廷派系、姻亲关系与军事支持左右。1648年前后，拉贾辛哈二世已稳固其统治，但继承安排仍带有宫廷政治的不确定性。
succession.en: Succession was in principle hereditary within the royal house, but in practice it was often shaped by court factions, marriage ties, and military backing. By around 1648, Rajasinha II had consolidated his rule, yet succession politics still retained an element of uncertainty.

structure.zh: 康提王国的政务由国王直接裁决，中央依靠少数重臣与宫廷官僚执行。全国划分为若干省区，由迪萨瓦等地方长官管理，并通过村社与服役体系向中央输送税赋和人力。寺院网络在王权合法性与地方治理中具有辅助作用。
structure.en: The affairs of the Kingdom of Kandy were decided directly by the king, with central administration carried out by a small group of chief ministers and court officials. The realm was divided into provinces managed by dissavas and other local officers, who transmitted taxes and manpower to the center through village and service structures. Monastic networks also supported royal legitimacy and local governance.

function.zh: 负责王室事务、诏令发布、宫廷礼仪与对外文书。
function.en: Handled royal household affairs, proclamations, court ceremonial, and external correspondence.

function.zh: 协调各省迪萨瓦、征收贡赋、登记劳役并监督地方秩序。
function.en: Coordinated provincial dissavas, collected tribute, recorded labor obligations, and supervised local order.

function.zh: 管理军役征发、边防动员、兵器储备及皇家象队。
function.en: Managed military levies, frontier mobilization, arms stores, and the royal elephant corps.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省区和村社为基础，迪萨瓦、科拉勒与村长层层负责。地方官兼具财政、司法与军事征发职能。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on provinces and village communities, with dissavas, korales, and village headmen forming a layered hierarchy. Local officers combined fiscal, judicial, and military mobilization functions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以王命、习惯法和佛教伦理为基础，并因地区与社群而有所差异。案件多由地方长官先行裁断，重大事务上诉至宫廷。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on royal decree, customary law, and Buddhist moral norms, with variations by region and community. Cases were usually judged first by local officers, while major matters could be appealed to the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以稻米、肉桂、象牙等实物贡纳和劳役为主，货币税收比例较低。王室还依靠对土地、山林资源和贸易通道的控制获取收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on payments in kind such as rice, cinnamon, and ivory, along with labor service, while cash taxation remained limited. The crown also drew income from control over land, forest resources, and trade routes.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 康提延续并保护僧伽罗佛教礼仪、寺院学术与宫廷艺术传统。1648年前后，王国在抵抗沿海欧洲势力的同时，仍维持宗教建筑、壁画与佛教文献抄写活动。
culturalAchievements.en: Kandy preserved Sinhalese Buddhist ritual, monastic learning, and courtly artistic traditions. Around 1648, while resisting European coastal powers, the kingdom still maintained religious construction, mural art, and manuscript copying of Buddhist texts.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语族僧伽罗语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Indo-Aryan Sinhala

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教王权观与僧伽罗地方传统并行
philosophy.en: Theravada Buddhist kingship blended with Sinhalese local tradition

writingSystem.zh: 僧伽罗文，宗教与学术场合并用巴利文
writingSystem.en: Sinhala script, with Pali also used in religious and scholarly contexts

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 康提经济以山地稻作、林产、象只贸易与转运贸易为基础。由于沿海长期受葡萄牙与荷兰势力争夺，王国经济具备较强的内陆自给性，但对外贸易收益波动较大。
gdpDescription.en: The Kandyan economy was based on upland rice cultivation, forest products, elephant trade, and transit commerce. Because the coasts were contested by Portuguese and Dutch forces, the kingdom developed a relatively self-sufficient inland economy, though foreign trade income was volatile.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、林产采集、象只捕获、手工业、区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, forest extraction, elephant capture, handicrafts, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、肉桂、胡椒、槟榔、象只、蜂蜡、木材、象牙
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cinnamon, pepper, areca nut, elephants, beeswax, timber, and ivory

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富主要体现为稻田使用权、牲畜、储粮与村社关系，而非大量铸币积蓄。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in access to paddy land, livestock, grain stores, and village ties rather than large accumulations of coin.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王权控制的贡赋经济、村社农业和有限市场交换相结合的前近代混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy combining crown-controlled tribute, village agriculture, and limited market exchange.

source.en: Forest products, elephants, and royal monopolies

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备多以贵金属、实物贡品、象牙及可动员粮秣形式保存，现金流并不充裕。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were held in precious metals, tribute goods, ivory, and mobilizable grain supplies, with limited liquid cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 在战争压力下，王室倾向扩大实物征收、加强专营并动用临时贡纳以维持军政支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Under wartime pressure, the crown tended to expand in-kind levies, reinforce monopolies, and demand extraordinary tribute to sustain military and administrative spending.

description.zh: 以长矛、刀剑、弓箭及部分火绳枪为装备，依托山地防御作战。
description.en: Equipped with spears, swords, bows, and some matchlocks, they fought primarily in mountain defensive warfare.

description.zh: 由地方贵族按服役义务征发，战时承担守隘、运输与支援任务。
description.en: Raised by local nobles through service obligations, these forces guarded passes and provided transport and wartime support.

description.zh: 主要用于仪仗、运输、威慑及有限战场用途，兼有王宫护卫职能。
description.en: Used mainly for ceremony, transport, intimidation, and limited battlefield roles, while also serving palace security functions.

commanderInChief.zh: 拉贾辛哈二世国王

technology.zh: 军备处于南亚山地王国中等水平，熟悉火绳枪与堡垒防御，但火器制造和火炮数量受限。其优势在于地形利用、伏击和后勤分散。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a middling level for a South Asian highland kingdom, with knowledge of matchlocks and fortified defense but limited artillery production and gun numbers. Its advantages lay in terrain use, ambush, and dispersed logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自荷兰东印度公司与残余葡萄牙势力在沿海的扩张，以及地方贵族忠诚度的波动。
threats.en: The main threats came from the expansion of the Dutch East India Company and residual Portuguese power along the coast, as well as fluctuations in the loyalty of local nobles.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于中部高地与农业山谷，乡村居民占绝大多数。族群以僧伽罗人为主，并有泰米尔人、摩尔商人与其他小型社群。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the central highlands and agricultural valleys, with the overwhelming majority living in rural communities. Sinhalese formed the majority, alongside Tamils, Moor merchants, and smaller groups.

name.zh: 萨巴勒格穆沃地区聚落

socialClasses.zh: 国王与王族、宫廷贵族、地方长官、僧侣、工匠与商人、农民与服役群体。
socialClasses.en: King and royal kin, court nobility, provincial chiefs, clergy, artisans and merchants, peasants, and service groups.

allies.zh: 与荷兰在反葡萄牙问题上曾有合作，但关系充满猜疑与利益冲突。
allies.en: Kandy had cooperated with the Dutch against the Portuguese, but the relationship was marked by mistrust and conflicting interests.

enemies.zh: 葡萄牙殖民势力，以及逐渐控制沿海的荷兰东印度公司。
enemies.en: Portuguese colonial forces, and increasingly the Dutch East India Company as it consolidated the coasts.

foreignPolicy.zh: 王国采取平衡沿海欧洲势力、争取贸易利益并维护山地独立的现实主义外交。
foreignPolicy.en: The kingdom pursued a pragmatic foreign policy aimed at balancing European coastal powers, securing trade advantages, and preserving highland independence.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶南亚高地王国技术水平
era.en: Mid-17th-century South Asian highland kingdom technology

keyInnovations.zh: 山地堡垒防御、象役组织、宫廷与寺院文书体系、有限火器吸收。
keyInnovations.en: Mountain fortification, organized elephant service, court and monastic record systems, and limited adoption of firearms.

strengths.zh: 地形险要、佛教王权合法性强、地方动员能力较高，并能利用欧洲列强竞争。
strengths.en: Strong defensive terrain, durable Buddhist royal legitimacy, considerable local mobilization capacity, and an ability to exploit rivalry among European powers.

weaknesses.zh: 对沿海贸易控制不足，火器与财政基础有限，地方贵族离心倾向时常影响中央权威。
weaknesses.en: Weak control over coastal trade, limited firearms and fiscal capacity, and recurrent centrifugal tendencies among provincial nobles.

outlook.zh: 短期内康提仍可凭借山地防御维持独立，但若无法控制沿海贸易与稳定对外关系，长期压力将持续增加。
outlook.en: In the short term, Kandy could preserve its independence through mountain defense, but long-term pressure would grow if it failed to influence coastal trade and stabilize foreign relations.

description.zh: 1648年的康提王国是一个依靠山地防御、佛教王权与地方贵族网络维持独立的僧伽罗内陆王国。
description.en: In 1648, the Kingdom of Kandy was a Sinhalese inland realm that preserved its independence through mountain defense, Buddhist kingship, and networks of local nobles.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个掸族土司政权组成、彼此松散联系的分封式山地诸邦体系。
governmentForm.en: A loosely connected confederated highland order composed of multiple Shan saopha principalities.

socialStructure.zh: 掸邦社会以召法土司家族、佛教僧团、地方首领与稻作村社为核心。平原盆地与河谷聚落承担农业生产，山地则分布多种族群与交换网络。身份秩序受血缘、地方效忠与佛教施主关系共同塑造。
socialStructure.en: Shan society centered on saopha ruling families, Buddhist monastic communities, local headmen, and rice-growing village settlements. Agricultural production was concentrated in basin plains and river valleys, while the surrounding uplands contained diverse peoples and exchange networks. Social order was shaped by kinship, local allegiance, and Buddhist patronage ties.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层为各邦召法、其亲族、随从武士与书记官。地方权力常依赖家族婚姻、私属武装和对盆地农田的控制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted of the saophas of each state, their kin, armed retainers, and scribal officials. Local power often rested on marriage alliances, private armed followings, and control of basin farmlands.

succession.zh: 继承通常在土司家族内部进行，多为父子或旁系承袭，但也常因争位和外部强邻干预而变动。不同邦国的继承规则并不完全一致。
succession.en: Succession usually remained within the ruling lineage, often passing from father to son or to collateral branches, but it could be disrupted by succession disputes and intervention from stronger neighbors. Rules varied from state to state.

structure.zh: 掸邦并非统一中央集权国家，而是若干大小邦国在共同文化和佛教传统下形成的松散体系。各邦召法对本地拥有较高自主权，同时在对外事务上受到缅甸、兰纳、老挝或中国边疆力量的影响。政务规模较小，以宫廷随从、书记与地方头人为主。
structure.en: The Shan States were not a fully centralized kingdom but a loose system of principalities linked by common culture and Buddhist tradition. Each saopha exercised substantial autonomy locally, while foreign affairs were influenced by Burma, Lan Na, Lao polities, or frontier Chinese forces. Administration was small in scale and relied on court retainers, scribes, and local headmen.

function.zh: 处理王族事务、贡礼往来、土地授予与宫廷命令。
function.en: Handled princely household affairs, tribute exchanges, land grants, and court orders.

function.zh: 征收稻谷、管理仓储、组织劳役与记录村社贡纳。
function.en: Collected rice tribute, managed granaries, organized labor obligations, and recorded village dues.

function.zh: 负责山口防务、武装随从调度和对外使节护送。
function.en: Managed mountain-pass defense, deployment of armed retainers, and escort of envoys.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托盆地村社、头人与土司直属亲信。边远山地常采取间接统治，以贡纳和盟誓维系关系。
localAdmin.en: Local rule depended on basin villages, headmen, and the saopha's direct retainers. Remote uplands were often governed indirectly through tribute and oath-based allegiance.

legalSystem.zh: 司法以习惯法、土司法令和佛教道德规范为基础。多数纠纷在村社或土司法庭解决，重案则由统治家族裁断。
legalSystem.en: Justice was based on customary law, princely decree, and Buddhist moral norms. Most disputes were settled at the village level or in the saopha's court, while serious cases were decided by the ruling family.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以稻谷、林产、家畜、手工业品和劳役为主，货币征收有限。土司亦通过过路税和边贸抽分获得收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxes were gathered mainly in rice, forest products, livestock, handicrafts, and labor service, with limited cash collection. Saophas also derived income from tolls and shares of frontier trade.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 掸邦在寺院教育、佛塔营建、编年记录与地方文学方面延续上座部佛教文化圈传统。尽管政治分散，各邦仍共享礼仪、书写和功德施舍文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The Shan States sustained Theravada cultural traditions through monastic education, pagoda building, chronicle writing, and local literature. Despite political fragmentation, the states shared rituals, writing practices, and a culture of merit-making patronage.

languageFamily.zh: 侗台语系台语支掸语
languageFamily.en: Kra-Dai, Tai branch, Shan language

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教与掸族地方王权传统结合
philosophy.en: Theravada Buddhism combined with Shan local traditions of rulership

writingSystem.zh: 掸文，宗教文献常见巴利文与缅文影响
writingSystem.en: Shan script, with Pali and Burmese influence in religious texts

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 掸邦经济以盆地稻作、山地林产、畜牧与边境转运贸易为主。由于诸邦分立且交通受地形制约，整体市场整合度较低，但跨山通道赋予其区域中介价值。
gdpDescription.en: The Shan economy rested on basin rice cultivation, upland forest products, livestock, and frontier transit trade. Because the states were divided and movement was constrained by terrain, overall market integration was low, but mountain routes gave them regional intermediary value.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、畜牧、林产采集、织造、边境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, livestock raising, forest extraction, weaving, and frontier trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、棉布、漆器、盐、马匹、银、林木、茶叶
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cotton cloth, lacquerware, salt, horses, silver, timber, and tea

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为稻田、水牛、织物、金银饰品与谷仓储备，地区间差异较大。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in paddy fields, buffaloes, textiles, silver ornaments, and granary reserves, with considerable regional variation.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 分散的土司贡赋经济与地方市场、边贸交换并存的山地农业经济。
economicSystem.en: A decentralized highland agrarian economy combining saopha tribute structures, local markets, and frontier exchange.

source.en: Frontier customs and road toll shares

treasuryDescription.zh: 各邦储备分散于土司府库与寺院捐献体系中，粮仓和银饰品常兼具储值功能。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were dispersed among princely treasuries and monastic donation networks, with granaries and silver ornaments also serving as stores of value.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体保守，重视维持地方平衡与粮储，避免过重征敛引发属民外逃。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, emphasizing local balance and grain reserves while avoiding excessive extraction that might drive dependents away.

description.zh: 由土司直属武士与护卫组成，是各邦最可靠的常备武装。
description.en: Composed of the saopha's own warriors and guards, these were the most dependable standing troops of each state.

description.zh: 战时由盆地村社与附属部族提供，装备较简陋，以守御和短期出征为主。
description.en: Supplied in wartime by basin villages and dependent tribes, these forces were lightly equipped and used mainly for defense and short campaigns.

description.zh: 以马匹和驮畜支持巡逻、传令与山地机动，规模有限。
description.en: Using horses and pack animals for patrols, communications, and mountain mobility, these units were small in scale.

commanderInChief.zh: 各邦召法分别统军
commanderInChief.en: Each state's Saopha commanded his own forces

technology.zh: 军备以冷兵器和少量火绳枪为主，攻城与重炮能力较弱。掸邦更擅长山地机动、要道控制与地方武装动员。
technology.en: Armament relied mainly on edged weapons and small numbers of matchlocks, with weak siege and heavy artillery capability. The Shan states were more adept at mountain mobility, pass control, and mobilizing local warbands.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自缅甸王朝势力扩张、邻近泰老政权压力以及各邦之间的内斗。
threats.en: Main threats came from the expansion of Burmese royal power, pressure from neighboring Tai and Lao polities, and rivalries among the Shan states themselves.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于谷地盆地与山间聚落，城市化程度较低。掸族为主体，但同时包括佤、拉祜、克钦、傣仂及其他山地族群。
populationDescription.en: The population was scattered across valley basins and mountain settlements, with low urbanization. Shan peoples formed the core population, alongside Wa, Lahu, Kachin, Tai Lü, and other upland groups.

socialClasses.zh: 召法王族、地方贵族与武士、僧侣、自由农民、工匠商贩、附属山地部民与奴婢。
socialClasses.en: Saopha royalty, local nobles and warriors, monks, free peasants, artisans and traders, dependent upland peoples, and slaves or bonded servants.

allies.zh: 各邦多依时局与邻近政权结盟，常与兰纳、老挝或其他掸邦维持临时合作。
allies.en: Alliances were fluid and situational, with temporary cooperation often formed with Lan Na, Lao states, or other Shan polities.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括试图加强宗主权的缅甸势力，以及争夺边地资源的邻邦。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included Burmese forces seeking tighter overlordship and neighboring states competing for frontier resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 掸邦总体采取灵活附庸与平衡外交，通过进贡、联姻和地方结盟在强邻之间保存自治。
foreignPolicy.en: The Shan states generally followed a flexible policy of tributary balancing, using tribute, marriage ties, and local alliances to preserve autonomy between stronger neighbors.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶东南亚山地土司政权技术水平
era.en: Mid-17th-century Southeast Asian highland princely technology

keyInnovations.zh: 盆地灌溉稻作、山道贸易组织、寺院书写文化、有限火器传入。
keyInnovations.en: Basin irrigation rice agriculture, mountain-route trade organization, monastic literate culture, and limited firearm diffusion.

strengths.zh: 地形复杂、地方适应力强、边贸中介地位重要，且政治结构具备弹性。
strengths.en: Complex terrain, strong local adaptability, valuable intermediary position in frontier trade, and flexible political structures.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、常备军有限、财政基础薄弱，面对中央集权强国时较难统一应对。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, limited standing forces, and a weak fiscal base made unified resistance to centralized powers difficult.

outlook.zh: 若周边强国扩张加剧，掸邦自治空间将受压缩；但在短期内，其分散结构仍有助于延续地方生存。
outlook.en: If neighboring great powers intensified their expansion, Shan autonomy would shrink; in the short term, however, their dispersed structure still aided local survival.

description.zh: 1648年的掸邦是由多个奉行上座部佛教的山地土司政权组成的松散诸邦体系。
description.en: In 1648, the Shan States were a loose constellation of Theravada Buddhist highland principalities ruled by saophas.

dynasty.zh: 无固定王朝，以加达等级体系轮任领导
dynasty.en: No fixed dynasty; leadership rotated through the Gadaa age-grade system

governmentForm.zh: 以加达制度为核心、由年龄等级与氏族会议共同治理的部族联邦式社会。
governmentForm.en: A tribal federative society governed through the Gadaa system of age-grades and clan assemblies.

socialStructure.zh: 奥罗莫社会以氏族、支系与年龄等级组织为基本单元，政治、军事与宗教责任彼此交织。成年男性通过加达制度进入不同公共职责阶段，群体决策常经大会讨论。牧畜与农牧混合生计塑造了较强的流动性与地方自治。
socialStructure.en: Oromo society was organized around clans, sub-lineages, and age-grade institutions in which political, military, and religious responsibilities overlapped. Adult males moved through public roles under the Gadaa system, and collective decisions were often made in assemblies. Pastoral and agro-pastoral lifeways fostered considerable mobility and local autonomy.

rulingClass.zh: 奥罗莫不存在严格世袭贵族阶层，政治权威主要由阿巴·加达、氏族长老与宗教权威共享。影响力取决于声望、年龄等级资历、调解能力与战时领导力。
rulingClass.en: The Oromo did not possess a rigid hereditary aristocracy; political authority was shared among the Abba Gada, clan elders, and religious authorities. Influence depended on prestige, age-grade standing, mediating ability, and wartime leadership.

succession.zh: 最高政治领导并非王朝世袭，而依加达周期轮替。权力交接具有制度化特征，但不同地区的实践存在差异。
succession.en: Top political leadership was not dynastically hereditary but rotated according to the Gadaa cycle. Transfer of authority was institutionalized, though practices varied by region.

structure.zh: 奥罗莫政治结构以部族会议、长老协商和加达等级轮值领导为中心，没有单一持久中央官僚机构。阿巴·加达在任期内主持重大决策、仲裁争端并组织战时动员。各氏族和地区共同体保有高度自主性。
structure.en: Oromo political organization centered on tribal assemblies, councils of elders, and rotating leadership under the Gadaa system, without a single permanent centralized bureaucracy. The Abba Gada presided over major decisions, mediated disputes, and coordinated wartime mobilization during his term. Clans and regional communities retained a high degree of autonomy.

name.en: Gadaa Assembly and Council of Elders

function.zh: 负责集体决策、仲裁纠纷、确认领导轮替与规范部族秩序。
function.en: Handled collective decision-making, dispute mediation, confirmation of leadership rotation, and regulation of social order.

function.zh: 组织战时集结、牧群保护、迁徙协调与传递号令。
function.en: Organized wartime musters, herd protection, migration coordination, and transmission of commands.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由氏族长老、加达领袖与聚落首领共同承担。权力更多建立在协商、习惯与共同体认可之上，而非固定文官体系。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out jointly by clan elders, Gadaa leaders, and settlement headmen. Authority rested more on negotiation, custom, and communal recognition than on a fixed civil bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、长老裁决和加达规范为基础，强调赔偿、和解与群体平衡。宗教誓约与公共集会在执行中具有重要作用。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, elder adjudication, and Gadaa norms, with emphasis on compensation, reconciliation, and communal balance. Religious oaths and public assemblies played important roles in enforcement.

taxationSystem.zh: 奥罗莫并无成熟常设税制，公共资源主要来自牲畜贡献、战利品分配、仪式性馈赠与临时动员。对外贸易或过境抽分仅在部分地区存在。
taxationSystem.en: The Oromo lacked a mature permanent tax system; public resources came mainly from livestock contributions, division of war booty, ritual gifts, and temporary mobilization. Trade or transit dues existed only in some areas.

religion.zh: 瓦克费凡纳宗教
religion.en: Waaqeffanna religion

culturalAchievements.zh: 奥罗莫文化在口述史诗、氏族记忆、法律格言与加达仪式方面极为丰富。1648年前后，奥罗莫扩张与迁徙推动了东非高原上语言、习俗与政治组织的广泛传播。
culturalAchievements.en: Oromo culture was rich in oral epics, clan memory, legal maxims, and Gadaa ritual. Around 1648, Oromo migrations and expansion helped spread their language, customs, and political institutions across the Horn of Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 亚非语系库施特语族奥罗莫语
languageFamily.en: Afro-Asiatic, Cushitic, Oromo language

philosophy.zh: 加达公共伦理与瓦卡信仰相结合的共同体秩序观
philosophy.en: A communal ethic combining Gadaa civic order with belief in Waaqa

writingSystem.zh: 无广泛通用本族文字，主要依靠口述传统
writingSystem.en: No widely used indigenous script; culture was transmitted primarily through oral tradition

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 奥罗莫经济以牧畜、农牧混合生产、地方交换和战时掠获为基础。其财富结构偏重牛群与群体资源，而非城市商业或高度货币化财政。
gdpDescription.en: The Oromo economy was based on pastoralism, agro-pastoral production, local exchange, and wartime acquisition. Its wealth structure centered on cattle and communal resources rather than urban commerce or highly monetized finance.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧畜、农牧混合农业、皮革加工、蜂蜜生产、地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, agro-pastoral farming, leatherworking, honey production, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 牛、羊、皮革、乳制品、谷物、蜂蜜、咖啡豆、奴隶
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, sheep, hides, dairy products, grain, honey, coffee beans, and slaves

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为牛群规模、亲属网络、武装能力和对牧场与水源的稳定获取。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured chiefly by herd size, kinship networks, fighting capacity, and secure access to pasture and water.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族共有规范、牲畜财富、互助交换与有限市场接触构成的部族农牧经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal agro-pastoral economy built on clan norms, livestock wealth, reciprocal exchange, and limited market contact.

source.en: Shares of booty and captured goods
Local exchange dues and transit shares

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备多以牲畜、谷物、蜂蜜和可分配战利品形式存在，而非集中式金库。
treasuryDescription.en: Public reserves existed largely in the form of livestock, grain, honey, and distributable booty rather than a centralized treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作强调按需动员和共同体分担，尽量避免形成固定高压征收，以维护氏族团结与流动性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized ad hoc mobilization and shared communal burden, avoiding rigid heavy extraction in order to preserve clan cohesion and mobility.

description.zh: 以长矛、盾牌、短剑为主要装备，依靠年龄等级组织快速集结。
description.en: Armed mainly with spears, shields, and short blades, they could be assembled rapidly through age-grade organization.

description.zh: 部分地区使用骑乘力量进行侦察、追击和远程掠袭，机动性较强。
description.en: In some regions, mounted forces were used for scouting, pursuit, and long-range raiding, offering strong mobility.

description.zh: 负责保卫营地、牲畜群和水源，战时可并入主力。
description.en: These defended camps, herds, and water sources, and could join the main force in wartime.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿巴·加达

technology.zh: 技术水平以东非内陆部族社会标准而言较强，擅长机动集结、长矛战和牧区生存，但缺乏系统火器与攻城能力。军事优势依赖人数、速度、地理熟悉度和社会动员制度。
technology.en: By the standards of inland East African tribal societies, Oromo military capability was strong, excelling in mobile mustering, spear warfare, and pastoral-zone endurance, but lacking systematic firearms and siege capability. Their advantages rested on numbers, speed, familiarity with terrain, and social mobilization institutions.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与埃塞俄比亚高原政权、邻近部族和地方资源竞争相关的持续冲突，以及干旱对牧群生计的冲击。
threats.en: The main threats came from ongoing conflict with Ethiopian highland polities, neighboring peoples, and competition over local resources, as well as drought shocks affecting pastoral livelihoods.

populationDescription.zh: 奥罗莫人口分布广泛，以流动性较强的牧民和半定居农牧民为主。固定城市极少，人口多集中于季节性营地、聚落与牧场带。
populationDescription.en: The Oromo were widely distributed, consisting mainly of mobile pastoralists and semi-settled agro-pastoralists. Permanent cities were rare, and most people lived in seasonal camps, settlements, and pasture zones.

socialClasses.zh: 阿巴·加达与长老、成年战士、普通牧民与农牧民、依附者与俘获奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Abba Gada and elders, adult warriors, ordinary pastoralists and agro-pastoralists, dependents, and captured slaves.

allies.zh: 联盟多基于氏族和地区需要形成，部分奥罗莫群体彼此协作，也会与邻近社群达成临时互助。
allies.en: Alliances were usually formed according to clan and regional need; some Oromo groups cooperated with one another and also made temporary arrangements with neighboring communities.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括埃塞俄比亚高原诸政权、敌对邻族以及争夺牧场与水源的地方群体。
enemies.en: Principal rivals included Ethiopian highland polities, hostile neighboring peoples, and local groups competing over pasture and water.

foreignPolicy.zh: 奥罗莫并无统一持续的国家外交，而以氏族联盟、迁徙扩张、通婚与冲突调解构成其对外关系方式。
foreignPolicy.en: The Oromo lacked a single permanent state foreign policy; instead, external relations were shaped by clan alliances, migratory expansion, marriage ties, and conflict mediation.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶东非农牧部族社会技术水平
era.en: Mid-17th-century East African agro-pastoral tribal technology

keyInnovations.zh: 加达制度下的社会动员、牧畜资源管理、口述法传统、适应多生态带迁徙组织。
keyInnovations.en: Social mobilization through the Gadaa system, pastoral resource management, oral legal tradition, and migration organization across multiple ecological zones.

strengths.zh: 社会动员力强、机动性高、氏族网络广泛，适应东非高原与草原过渡地带的环境能力突出。
strengths.en: Strong social mobilization, high mobility, broad clan networks, and notable adaptability to the transitional environments of the Horn of Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏稳定中央政权、城市财政和系统火器，难以长期经营攻城与固定行政控制。
weaknesses.en: Lack of a stable central state, urban fiscal base, and systematic firearms made long-term siege warfare and fixed administrative control difficult.

outlook.zh: 1648年前后，奥罗莫仍处于扩张与重组阶段，凭借人口与制度优势可能继续扩大影响，但内部区域差异也会逐渐加深。
outlook.en: Around 1648, the Oromo remained in a phase of expansion and reorganization; their demographic and institutional strengths could continue to widen their influence, though internal regional differentiation would also deepen.

description.zh: 1648年的奥罗莫是一个依靠加达制度、氏族协商与农牧机动性扩展势力的东非部族社会。
description.en: In 1648, the Oromo were an East African tribal society expanding through the Gadaa system, clan consultation, and agro-pastoral mobility.

governmentForm.zh: 以昌加米尔为核心的神权化君主帝国，王权通过姆瓦里神谕与军事征服获得合法性。
governmentForm.en: A sacral monarchy centered on the Changamire, whose authority was legitimized through Mwari religious sanction and military conquest.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、地方首领、战士与普通农牧民为主要层级，另有工匠与商人群体。地方共同体仍保有较强的宗族与酋邦传统，但逐步被帝国性的贡赋与军事体系整合。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the royal house, provincial chiefs, warriors, and common farming-pastoral communities, with artisans and traders forming smaller strata. Local communities retained strong lineage and chieftaincy traditions, but were increasingly integrated into an imperial tribute and military order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由昌加米尔王室、受封的地方酋长以及控制牛群、金矿与军队的贵族组成。宗教祭司在政治合法性上也具有重要影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Changamire royal house, subordinate regional chiefs, and nobles who controlled cattle, gold resources, and armed retainers. Religious custodians also held major influence in conferring political legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部继承，但实际继承常受贵族支持、军事实力与宗教认可共同影响。继承争议可能引发地方首领分化与武装冲突。
succession.en: Succession generally remained within the royal house, but in practice depended on noble backing, military strength, and religious approval. Disputed succession could trigger factionalism among provincial chiefs and armed conflict.

structure.zh: 帝国政权围绕都城达南贡贝与王廷运作，由昌加米尔直接统辖核心地区，并通过附属酋长治理外围。行政机构较为精简，依赖贡赋网络、王室使者与军事首领维持统治。
structure.en: The empire was organized around the court at Danangombe, with the Changamire directly ruling the core zone and subordinate chiefs administering outer territories. Administration was relatively lean, relying on tribute networks, royal envoys, and military leaders to maintain authority.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室仓储、贡品分配与王命传达。
function.en: Managed the palace, royal stores, tribute distribution, and transmission of royal commands.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、黄金与劳役，并监督王室牧群。
function.en: Collected grain, cattle, gold, and labor dues, while supervising royal herds.

function.zh: 组织王室卫队、征召附庸部众并协调边境防御与远征。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, mobilized subordinate levies, and coordinated frontier defense and campaigns.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠归附的酋长与头人，他们向王廷纳贡并提供兵员。核心区的王室任命者控制更强，边缘地区则保留较多间接统治特征。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on subordinate chiefs and headmen who paid tribute and supplied manpower. Royal appointees exercised stronger control in the core, while peripheral areas retained more indirect rule.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁决与宗教禁忌为基础。重大案件常由酋长会议或王廷裁断，赔偿、牲畜罚没与仪式性和解较为常见。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, royal judgment, and religious taboo. Major disputes were often settled by councils of chiefs or the royal court, with compensation, livestock forfeiture, and ritual reconciliation common.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋主要以实物和劳役形式征收，包括谷物、牛只、黄金、象牙与建筑或军事劳役。贡赋关系既是财政来源，也是政治臣属的象征。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily collected in kind and labor, including grain, cattle, gold, ivory, and construction or military service. Tribute served both as a fiscal base and as a symbol of political subordination.

religion.zh: 姆瓦里崇拜
religion.en: Mwari cult

culturalAchievements.zh: 罗兹维延续并发展了津巴布韦高地的石砌建筑传统，在王城与精英聚落中展现权力象征。口传史诗、赞歌与宗教仪式在维系帝国认同方面尤为重要。
culturalAchievements.en: The Rozwi sustained and developed the stone-building traditions of the Zimbabwe plateau, expressing authority through capitals and elite settlements. Oral epics, praise poetry, and ritual performance were especially important in maintaining imperial identity.

languageFamily.zh: 班图语系，主要为绍纳语群
languageFamily.en: Bantu language family, primarily the Shona cluster

philosophy.zh: 以姆瓦里信仰、祖灵崇拜与王权神授观念为核心的宗教政治文化。
philosophy.en: A religio-political culture centered on Mwari belief, ancestor veneration, and sacral kingship.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土广泛使用的成文文字，信息主要通过口述传统、记忆制度与象征物传承。
writingSystem.en: No widely used indigenous writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, memory practices, and symbolic media.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以粟类农业、牧牛、黄金开采与区域贸易为支柱。其财富水平在南部非洲内陆属较强，但整体仍以低货币化的实物经济为主。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on cereal agriculture, cattle herding, gold extraction, and regional trade. It was relatively strong for the southern African interior, though still dominated by low-monetization in-kind exchange.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、牧业、采金、冶铁、陶器、象牙贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, gold mining, ironworking, pottery, ivory trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、象牙、牛只、铁器、谷物、盐
tradeGoods.en: Gold, ivory, cattle, iron goods, grain, salt

name.zh: 实物贡赋与金属交换体系
name.en: In-kind tribute and metal exchange system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以牛群、粮仓、妻属网络与可动用劳力衡量。精英阶层则通过控制矿区、贡赋与战利品积累更高财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in cattle, grain stores, kinship networks, and command over labor. Elites accumulated greater wealth by controlling mining zones, tribute flows, and war booty.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year (estimated)
taels of silver equivalent per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以村社生产、王室贡赋与长距离交换并存的混合型前近代经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed premodern economy combining village production, royal tribute, and long-distance exchange.

category.en: Grants to chiefs and political integration

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库大部分以牛群、黄金、象牙和粮食储备形式存在，而非纯粹铸币储藏。王室财富具有很强的可分配性，用于维系忠诚与军事动员。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury existed largely in cattle, gold, ivory, and grain reserves rather than coined money. Royal wealth was highly redistributive, used to secure loyalty and military mobilization.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调扩大附庸贡赋、控制矿产与维持王室再分配能力。现金税不发达，财政稳健性高度依赖丰收与政治服从。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized expanding tributary inflows, controlling mineral resources, and preserving the redistributive capacity of the crown. Cash taxation was limited, and fiscal stability depended heavily on harvests and political obedience.

description.zh: 驻于王廷周边的精锐部队，负责护卫君主与执行关键战役。
description.en: Elite troops stationed around the court, responsible for protecting the ruler and conducting key operations.

description.zh: 由各地酋长提供的主力兵员，擅长步战与区域性作战。
description.en: The main body supplied by regional chiefs, suited to infantry combat and local campaigning.

description.zh: 人数较少，负责侦察、追击与边地巡逻。
description.en: A smaller force used for scouting, pursuit, and patrols in frontier zones.

commanderInChief.zh: 昌加米尔·曼博

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、弓箭、盾牌和少量铁制武器为主，可能接触有限火器但未形成核心战力。机动与地形熟悉度比器械复杂度更重要。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on spears, bows, shields, and iron weapons, with only limited access to firearms that were not yet decisive. Mobility and knowledge of terrain mattered more than technical sophistication.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近绍纳政权、葡萄牙势力及其商站渗透，以及附庸酋长可能发生的叛离。对贸易路线与矿区的控制是军事冲突焦点。
threats.en: Major threats came from neighboring Shona polities, Portuguese influence and trading outposts, and possible defections by tributary chiefs. Control of trade routes and mining areas was a central cause of conflict.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于津巴布韦高地的农业与牧业聚落，人口密度在肥沃高地较高。整体城市化程度有限，政治中心更接近大型王室聚落而非高度商业化城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in farming and pastoral settlements across the Zimbabwe plateau, with higher density in fertile uplands. Urbanization was limited overall, and political centers resembled large royal settlements more than highly commercial cities.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、祭司与灵媒、地方酋长、战士、工匠商人、普通农牧民与依附者。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobility, priests and spirit mediums, local chiefs, warriors, artisans and traders, common farmers-pastoralists, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与部分内陆绍纳首领及受其保护的附庸社群保持臣属或合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained tributary or cooperative relations with some inland Shona chiefs and protected subordinate communities.

enemies.zh: 葡萄牙东非势力、敌对绍纳政权及不稳的边缘酋邦。
enemies.en: Portuguese East African interests, rival Shona polities, and unstable frontier chiefdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制内陆贸易通道、压制葡萄牙影响并通过征服或纳贡扩大势力圈为主。外交与军事通常交织进行。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling inland trade corridors, containing Portuguese influence, and expanding the sphere of power through conquest or tribute. Diplomacy and warfare were closely intertwined.

era.zh: 南部非洲早期近代铁器与石构传统时代
era.en: Early modern southern African ironworking and stone-built tradition

keyInnovations.zh: 石砌精英建筑、区域性采金组织、铁器制造与以牛群为核心的后勤动员体系。
keyInnovations.en: Stone-built elite architecture, organized regional gold extraction, ironworking, and cattle-based logistical mobilization.

strengths.zh: 控制高地资源、拥有强烈的王权合法性，并能通过贡赋与战争整合广大内陆地区。
strengths.en: It controlled upland resources, enjoyed strong royal legitimacy, and could integrate broad inland territories through tribute and warfare.

weaknesses.zh: 行政制度较松散，货币化水平有限，对地方酋长忠诚与收成状况依赖很大。
weaknesses.en: Its administrative system was relatively loose, monetization was limited, and it depended heavily on the loyalty of chiefs and the quality of harvests.

outlook.zh: 1648年前后罗兹维仍具扩张与统合能力，但若外来贸易压力增加或继承失稳，帝国凝聚力可能在数代内下降。
outlook.en: Around 1648 the Rozwi still possessed real capacity for expansion and consolidation, but rising external trade pressure or unstable succession could weaken imperial cohesion within a few generations.

description.zh: 罗兹维帝国是以高地资源、王室贡赋与姆瓦里宗教权威为基础的南部非洲内陆强权。
description.en: The Rozwi Empire was a major inland southern African power built on upland resources, royal tribute, and the religious authority of the Mwari cult.

governmentForm.zh: 由选侯主导的天主教世袭君主制国家，在帝国法框架内保持高度领邦自主性。
governmentForm.en: A Catholic hereditary princely state led by an elector, retaining strong territorial autonomy within the framework of Imperial law.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宫廷贵族、教士、城市市民、乡村自由民与佃农构成，等级差异明确。三十年战争后的巴伐利亚仍保留传统等级秩序，但战争、税役与驻军使下层社会承受重压。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of court nobility, clergy, urban burghers, rural freeholders, and tenant peasants, with clear hierarchical distinctions. Post-war Bavaria preserved its traditional estate order, but war, taxation, and garrisoning placed severe burdens on lower strata.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层为维特尔斯巴赫王室、宫廷官僚、高级教士与领地贵族。选侯通过赞助、军功与行政任命加强对贵族的约束。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the Wittelsbach dynasty, court bureaucrats, high clergy, and territorial nobility. The elector strengthened control over the nobility through patronage, military service, and administrative appointments.

succession.zh: 继承遵循维特尔斯巴赫家族世袭原则，以男性宗支优先。若出现支系分封与绝嗣问题，需结合家族协定与帝国承认处理。
succession.en: Succession followed hereditary Wittelsbach principles, generally favoring male dynastic branches. Questions of partition or extinction of lines were handled through family compacts and Imperial recognition.

structure.zh: 巴伐利亚政府以慕尼黑宫廷为核心，形成较成熟的枢密、财政与战争行政体系。1648年时国家机器已因长期战争而显著扩张，并在税收、征兵与治安方面加强对地方的渗透。
structure.en: The Bavarian government was centered on the Munich court and had developed a comparatively mature privy, fiscal, and war administration. By 1648 the state apparatus had expanded markedly under the pressures of long war, increasing its reach into local taxation, recruitment, and policing.

function.zh: 处理重大政策、外交、王朝事务与高级任命。
function.en: Handled high policy, diplomacy, dynastic affairs, and senior appointments.

function.zh: 管理税收、领地收入、债务、军费拨付与盐业等重要财源。
function.en: Managed taxes, domain income, debt, military disbursements, and major revenues such as salt.

function.zh: 负责军队编制、征募、供给、驻防与要塞事务。
function.en: Oversaw force structure, recruitment, supply, garrisons, and fortress affairs.

function.zh: 监督法院、地方法官、警务与领地行政执行。
function.en: Supervised courts, local magistrates, policing, and territorial administrative enforcement.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由县区官员、领主法庭、城市议会与教会网络共同治理。中央政府在核心地区控制较强，但许多旧有特权与地方习惯仍然存在。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared among district officials, seigneurial courts, town councils, and ecclesiastical networks. Central control was strongest in the core lands, though many older privileges and local customs persisted.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合罗马法传统、巴伐利亚地方法与神圣罗马帝国法。教会法在婚姻、道德与部分司法领域仍具显著影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman-law traditions, Bavarian territorial law, and the law of the Holy Roman Empire. Canon law remained influential in marriage, morality, and some judicial matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收包括领地常税、消费税、盐业收入、关卡税及非常战时摊派。三十年战争使国家对直接征敛、借款与实物供给的依赖明显上升。
taxationSystem.en: Taxes included regular territorial levies, excises, salt income, tolls, and extraordinary wartime contributions. The Thirty Years' War greatly increased dependence on direct extraction, loans, and in-kind provisioning.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 巴伐利亚是德意志南部巴洛克艺术与反宗教改革建筑的重要中心，宫廷、修道院与教堂赞助活跃。慕尼黑在音乐、宗教艺术与礼仪文化方面具有区域影响力。
culturalAchievements.en: Bavaria was a major center of Baroque art and Counter-Reformation architecture in southern Germany, with active patronage by the court, monasteries, and churches. Munich exerted regional influence in music, religious art, and ceremonial culture.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族，以上德语巴伐利亚方言为主
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic; predominantly Upper German Bavarian dialects

philosophy.zh: 反宗教改革天主教文化、经院与巴洛克政治神学并行。
philosophy.en: Counter-Reformation Catholic culture combined with scholastic thought and Baroque political theology.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母，行政和学术常用拉丁文，日常与部分官方文书使用德语。
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet, with Latin common in administration and scholarship, and German used in daily and much official writing.

gdpDescription.zh: 尽管三十年战争造成严重破坏，巴伐利亚仍凭借农业、盐业、酿造、手工业与区域贸易保持较强经济基础。1648年的恢复尚不完全，但财政与生产能力在帝国诸邦中仍属上游。
gdpDescription.en: Despite severe devastation from the Thirty Years' War, Bavaria retained a strong economic base in agriculture, salt, brewing, crafts, and regional trade. Recovery in 1648 was incomplete, but its fiscal and productive capacity remained above average among Imperial states.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、盐业、酿酒、纺织、金属加工、林业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, salt production, brewing, textiles, metalworking, forestry

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、啤酒、谷物、木材、牲畜、布匹、金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Salt, beer, grain, timber, livestock, cloth, metal goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地、牲畜、谷物储备、城市作坊与债权。战争后乡村家庭普遍贫困化，而城市商人与盐业相关群体恢复较快。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in land, livestock, grain reserves, urban workshops, and credit claims. Rural households were widely impoverished after the war, while urban merchants and salt-related groups recovered more quickly.

economicSystem.zh: 以领主土地制、城市行会和国家监管盐业与消费税并存的混合型领邦经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed territorial economy combining seigneurial landholding, urban guild structures, and state-supervised salt and excise revenues.

treasuryDescription.zh: 战后财政较紧张，国库虽保有一定现金与可抵押收入，但须面对军费和债务压力。巴伐利亚的信用能力仍强于多数中小德意志邦国。
treasuryDescription.en: Postwar finances were strained; the treasury held some cash and assignable revenues but faced heavy military and debt burdens. Bavaria nevertheless retained stronger credit capacity than many smaller German states.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持常备军、恢复税基并通过盐业与消费税稳定现金流。选侯政府倾向于扩大中央征收权并压缩地方抗税空间。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining a standing army, restoring the tax base, and stabilizing cash flow through salt and excise revenues. The electoral government tended to expand central collection powers and reduce room for local resistance.

description.zh: 由火枪手与长枪兵传统编制演变而来，是巴伐利亚军队主力。
description.en: Derived from the mixed musketeer-pikeman formations of the era and formed the core of the Bavarian army.

description.zh: 用于侦察、冲击、掩护与追击，在德意志战场上具有重要机动价值。
description.en: Used for scouting, shock action, screening, and pursuit, with major operational value on German battlefields.

description.zh: 负责野战炮、攻城与守城任务，技术与训练水平在帝国诸邦中较高。
description.en: Handled field artillery, siege operations, and garrison defense, with relatively high technical and training standards among Imperial states.

commanderInChief.zh: 选侯马克西米利安一世

technology.zh: 采用17世纪中叶西欧标准的火枪、长枪、火炮、堡垒工事与军需体系。军纪、训练和指挥制度较成熟，但战后补充与财政能力受到限制。
technology.en: Bavaria used mid-seventeenth-century Western European muskets, pikes, artillery, fortification methods, and military supply systems. Discipline, training, and command were relatively mature, though postwar replenishment and fiscal capacity were constrained.

threats.zh: 1648年主要威胁虽因和约而下降，但仍包括帝国内宗教政治竞争、瑞典军力余波、法兰西影响扩张及战后社会失序。边境驻军与债务压力也构成持续风险。
threats.en: Though major threats diminished with the peace of 1648, Bavaria still faced confessional-political competition within the Empire, lingering Swedish military influence, expanding French power, and postwar disorder. Frontier garrisons and debt burdens remained continuing risks.

populationDescription.zh: 巴伐利亚人口在三十年战争中遭受显著损失，许多乡村地区出现减员与荒废。尽管如此，其核心地区仍维持较高农业人口与若干重要城市中心。
populationDescription.en: Bavaria suffered substantial population loss during the Thirty Years' War, with depopulation and abandonment in many rural districts. Even so, its core lands still sustained a large agrarian population and several important urban centers.

socialClasses.zh: 王朝贵族、高级教士、低级贵族、城市市民与行会成员、农民、佃农、雇工与贫民。
socialClasses.en: Dynastic nobility, high clergy, lesser nobility, urban burghers and guild members, peasants, tenants, laborers, and the poor.

allies.zh: 与哈布斯堡皇帝及多数天主教帝国诸侯维持紧密关系。
allies.en: Maintained close relations with the Habsburg emperor and most Catholic princes of the Empire.

enemies.zh: 长期敌手包括瑞典势力、法国在帝国中的影响网络以及若干新教竞争者。
enemies.en: Long-term adversaries included Swedish power, French influence networks inside the Empire, and various Protestant rivals.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交上寻求在支持皇帝与维护本邦自主之间取得平衡，并通过帝国议会、联姻和军事实力巩固选侯地位。1648年后重心逐步转向恢复内政与防范法瑞干涉。
foreignPolicy.en: Its diplomacy sought to balance support for the emperor with preservation of territorial autonomy, using Imperial institutions, marriage alliances, and military power to secure electoral status. After 1648 the emphasis shifted increasingly toward internal recovery and guarding against French and Swedish interference.

era.zh: 欧洲早期近代火器—巴洛克国家建设时期
era.en: Early modern European gunpowder and Baroque state-building era

keyInnovations.zh: 常备军制度、火枪与火炮协同、近代堡垒工事、财政文书化与盐业国家监管。
keyInnovations.en: Standing army institutions, musket-artillery coordination, modern fortress engineering, bureaucratic fiscal record-keeping, and state regulation of salt production.

strengths.zh: 王朝稳固、财政工具较多、军队训练优良且宗教认同明确，是德意志南部最强领邦之一。
strengths.en: With a stable dynasty, diverse fiscal tools, a well-trained army, and clear confessional identity, Bavaria was one of the strongest states in southern Germany.

weaknesses.zh: 战争破坏、人口损失、债务攀升与对高税负的依赖削弱了社会承受力。
weaknesses.en: War damage, population loss, rising debt, and reliance on heavy taxation weakened social resilience.

outlook.zh: 1648年后的巴伐利亚具备恢复与再集中的条件，若能控制债务并保持宫廷与地方合作，仍可继续在帝国内发挥重要影响。
outlook.en: After 1648 Bavaria had the means for recovery and renewed centralization; if it controlled debt and preserved cooperation between court and local elites, it could continue to play a major role within the Empire.

description.zh: 1648年的巴伐利亚是在三十年战争余烬中保持强大军政能力的天主教选侯国。
description.en: Bavaria in 1648 was a Catholic electoral state that retained strong military and administrative capacity amid the aftermath of the Thirty Years' War.

governmentForm.zh: 由选侯统治的世袭新教领邦，在神圣罗马帝国内享有广泛自治并以等级会议传统为支撑。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Protestant electoral principality ruled by an elector, enjoying broad autonomy within the Holy Roman Empire and supported by estate institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宫廷与官僚贵族、路德宗教士、城市市民、矿工、工匠及广大农民组成。萨克森经济较为发达，但长期战争和反复占领使城乡负担加重，社会紧张上升。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of court and bureaucratic nobility, Lutheran clergy, urban burghers, miners, artisans, and a broad peasantry. Saxony had a relatively advanced economy, but prolonged war and repeated occupations increased burdens across town and countryside and heightened social strain.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括韦廷王室、枢密官僚、地方贵族与受国家支持的路德宗教会精英。选侯政权需要与等级会议及重要城市利益协商。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the Wettin dynasty, privy bureaucrats, territorial nobility, and Lutheran ecclesiastical elites backed by the state. Electoral authority still had to negotiate with the estates and major urban interests.

succession.zh: 继承遵循韦廷家族的世袭原则，以阿尔贝系男系为核心。王朝内部虽较稳定，但分封传统与家族法仍可能影响领地完整性。
succession.en: Succession followed hereditary Wettin principles, centered on the male Albertine line. The dynasty was comparatively stable, though traditions of partition and family law could still affect territorial integrity.

structure.zh: 萨克森政府以德累斯顿宫廷为核心，由枢密院、财政署和司法体系共同运作。相比许多帝国诸侯国，萨克森行政更文书化、更依赖受教育官僚，但1648年已因战争与外交失误而显露疲态。
structure.en: The Saxon government centered on the Dresden court and operated through a privy council, fiscal administration, and judicial system. Compared with many Imperial states, Saxony was more document-driven and reliant on educated bureaucrats, though by 1648 it showed signs of strain from war and diplomatic failures.

function.zh: 统筹政策、外交、宗教事务与高级行政决策。
function.en: Coordinated policy, diplomacy, religious affairs, and high administrative decisions.

function.zh: 管理税赋、矿山收益、铸币、债务与宫廷支出。
