function.en: Collected land taxes, managed granaries, registered population, and organized labor conscription.

function.zh: 组织军队征召、武器管理、边防部署与水军调度。
function.en: Organized military levies, arms management, frontier deployment, and river-fleet coordination.

function.zh: 管理寺院登记、僧侣考试、佛教节日安排与王室功德施舍。
function.en: Managed monastery registration, monastic examinations, Buddhist festival arrangements, and royal merit-making donations.

localAdmin.zh: 全国分为若干穆昂（省），由国王任命或确认的昭穆昂治理。基层以村寨为单位，由村长管理。苏里亚旺萨加强了对地方的控制力。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into muang (provinces), each governed by chao muang appointed or confirmed by the king. The basic unit was the village, managed by headmen. Souligna Vongsa strengthened central control over the provinces.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以佛教道德、王令与习惯法为基础。苏里亚旺萨时期大力完善法典，使澜沧成为东南亚法制较为健全的国家之一。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Buddhist morality, royal edicts, and customary law. Under Souligna Vongsa, the legal code was substantially refined, making Lan Xang one of the more legally developed states in Southeast Asia.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以稻谷田赋为主，辅以劳役征调、林产贡纳、市场税和外贸抽分。各穆昂须向中央上缴定额贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was collected primarily through rice land taxes, supplemented by labor conscription, forest product tribute, market taxes, and foreign trade levies. Each muang was required to remit a fixed tribute to the center.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 苏里亚旺萨时期是澜沧文化的巅峰。佛教寺院教育普及，巴利文经典研习兴盛。著名的《罪孽花》（Sang Sinxay）等文学作品成书于此时期。王室大力赞助寺庙建造与宗教艺术创作。
culturalAchievements.en: The reign of Souligna Vongsa marked the cultural zenith of Lan Xang. Buddhist monastic education was widespread, and Pali scripture study flourished. Famous literary works like Sang Sinxay were composed during this period. The crown generously patronized temple construction and religious art.

languageFamily.zh: 侗台语系台语支老挝语
languageFamily.en: Kra-Dai, Tai branch, Lao language

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教教义与老挝王权宇宙观
philosophy.en: Theravada Buddhist doctrine and Lao royal cosmological order

writingSystem.zh: 老挝文（源自高棉文系统），宗教文献用巴利文
writingSystem.en: Lao script (derived from the Khmer system), with Pali used for religious texts

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 澜沧经济以湄公河流域的水稻种植为根本，辅以丰富的森林资源（沉香、松脂、象牙、鹿茸）出口。万象和琅勃拉邦是重要的贸易中心。苏里亚旺萨治下与荷兰东印度公司等建立了外交贸易关系。
gdpDescription.en: The Lan Xang economy was rooted in Mekong basin rice cultivation, supplemented by exports of rich forest products (agarwood, benzoin, ivory, deer antlers). Vientiane and Luang Prabang were important trade centers. Under Souligna Vongsa, diplomatic trade relations were established with the Dutch East India Company among others.

mainIndustries.zh: 水稻种植、森林产品采集（沉香、松脂、象牙）、织锦、河流运输、区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, forest product collection (agarwood, benzoin, ivory), silk weaving, river transport, regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 沉香、松脂、象牙、鹿茸、蜂蜡、兰花香料、稻米、织锦
tradeGoods.en: Agarwood, benzoin, ivory, deer antlers, beeswax, orchid spices, rice, silk textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以稻田面积、水牛数量、织锦储备与银饰为标志。上层贵族拥有大片田庄与象群。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was marked by paddy field area, buffalo counts, textile reserves, and silver ornaments. The upper nobility owned extensive estates and elephant herds.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室贡赋和稻作农业为基础、辅以森林产品出口的农业—贸易型王国经济。
economicSystem.en: An agricultural-trade kingdom economy based on royal tribute and rice farming, supplemented by forest product exports.

source.zh: 森林产品税与出口关税
source.en: Forest product tax and export duties
Market tax and river transit duties

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富包括王宫金银储备、象群、稻谷仓储与佛教寺院的功德基金，黄金时代积累丰厚。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth included palace gold and silver reserves, elephant herds, rice granaries, and Buddhist monastic merit funds, accumulated abundantly during the golden age.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 苏里亚旺萨推行稳健财政，平衡军事开支与宗教布施，保持充裕储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Souligna Vongsa pursued a sound fiscal policy, balancing military expenditure with religious patronage and maintaining ample reserves.

description.zh: 驻守万象王宫的精锐步兵与象兵，是国王最可靠的军事力量。
description.en: Elite infantry and elephant corps stationed at the Vientiane palace, the king's most reliable military force.

description.zh: 澜沧以战象闻名（国名即'百万大象之地'），象兵在战场冲击与威慑方面具有重要作用。
description.en: Lan Xang was renowned for its war elephants (the name means 'Land of a Million Elephants'), with the elephant corps playing an important role in battlefield shock and deterrence.

description.zh: 各穆昂战时提供的步兵，装备竹矛、刀剑与弓箭，以丛林与河流作战为主。
description.en: Infantry provided by each muang during wartime, armed with bamboo spears, swords, and bows, fighting primarily in jungle and river terrain.

description.zh: 湄公河及其支流的船队，负责运兵、巡逻与河流沿线防御。
description.en: Boat fleet on the Mekong and its tributaries, responsible for troop transport, patrol, and riverine defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏里亚旺萨亲统全军
commanderInChief.en: Souligna Vongsa personally commanded all forces

technology.zh: 军备以冷兵器为主（矛、刀、弓），战象为特色兵种。火器使用有限，但通过越南和欧洲商人有所接触。水军在湄公河防御中发挥关键作用。
technology.en: Armament relied mainly on edged weapons (spears, swords, bows), with war elephants as a distinctive arm. Firearm use was limited but expanding through contact with Vietnamese and European traders. The river fleet played a key role in Mekong defense.

keyWeapons.zh: 竹矛、刀剑、弓箭、战象、河船、少量火绳枪
keyWeapons.en: Bamboo spears, swords, bows, war elephants, river boats, limited matchlocks

defensiveStructures.zh: 万象城墙与护城河，各穆昂中心亦有木栅寨防，地形本身（丛林、河流）为天然屏障
defensiveStructures.en: Vientiane city walls and moat, with timber palisades at provincial muang centers; terrain itself (jungle, rivers) served as natural barriers

threats.zh: 缅甸东吁王朝势力、暹罗阿瑜陀耶王国扩张、越南北方郑主南方阮主的压力。苏里亚旺萨时期军事威胁相对可控。
threats.en: Burmese Toungoo forces, Siamese Ayutthaya expansion, and pressure from the Trinh and Nguyen lords of Vietnam. Under Souligna Vongsa, military threats were relatively manageable.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以老挝泰族为主体，分布于湄公河流域河谷与盆地。山地有苗族、瑶族、卡族等少数民族。苏里亚旺萨治下人口稳步增长。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly Lao Tai, distributed along the Mekong valley and basins. Upland areas were home to Hmong, Yao, Kha, and other minorities. Population grew steadily under Souligna Vongsa.

ethnicGroups.zh: 老挝泰族为主体，兼有苗族、瑶族、卡族（高地孟—高棉族群）及少量越南、华人商人
ethnicGroups.en: Lao Tai as the majority, with Hmong, Yao, Kha (highland Mon-Khmer groups), and small numbers of Vietnamese and Chinese merchants

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族官僚、佛教僧团、自由农民、工匠与商人、依附民与奴仆。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, noble bureaucrats, Buddhist clergy, free peasants, artisans and merchants, bonded dependents and slaves.

allies.zh: 与暹罗阿瑜陀耶维持外交关系（时有紧张），接待荷兰东印度公司等欧洲使节。
allies.en: Maintained diplomatic relations with Siamese Ayutthaya (sometimes tense), and received European envoys including the Dutch East India Company.

enemies.zh: 缅甸东吁王朝为历史敌人，越南郑主与阮主均对澜沧边境构成潜在威胁。
enemies.en: The Burmese Toungoo dynasty was a historic enemy, and both the Trinh and Nguyen lords of Vietnam posed potential threats to Lan Xang's borders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 苏里亚旺萨推行和平外交与贸易开放政策，主动接触欧洲商人，同时维持与邻国的朝贡—外交平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Souligna Vongsa pursued a policy of peaceful diplomacy and trade openness, actively engaging European merchants while maintaining a tribute-diplomatic balance with neighboring states.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶东南亚大陆王国技术水平
era.en: Mid-17th-century mainland Southeast Asian kingdom technology

keyInnovations.zh: 水稻灌溉技术、湄公河水运体系、佛教寺院教育网络、织锦工艺、有限火器引进。
keyInnovations.en: Rice irrigation technology, Mekong waterway transport system, Buddhist monastic education network, silk-weaving craftsmanship, and limited firearm adoption.

strengths.zh: 苏里亚旺萨治下政治稳定、法制健全、文化繁荣，森林产品出口带来可观收入，战象部队具有威慑力。
strengths.en: Political stability, sound legal system, and cultural prosperity under Souligna Vongsa; substantial income from forest product exports, and a deterrent war-elephant force.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模有限，城市化程度不高，军事技术落后于越南和缅甸，继承制度不稳定（苏里亚旺萨去世后即分裂为三国）。
weaknesses.en: Limited population, low urbanization, military technology lagging behind Vietnam and Burma, and an unstable succession system (the kingdom split into three after Souligna Vongsa's death).

outlook.zh: 1648年正值澜沧黄金时代的顶峰，但苏里亚旺萨去世后（1694年）王国将因继承危机分裂为万象、琅勃拉邦与占巴塞三国。
outlook.en: 1648 marked the peak of Lan Xang's golden age, but after Souligna Vongsa's death (1694), the kingdom would split into the three successor states of Vientiane, Luang Prabang, and Champassak.

description.zh: 1648年的澜沧王国在苏里亚旺萨治下迎来黄金时代，政治稳定、法制健全、佛教文化繁荣，是东南亚大陆最强盛的老挝王国。
description.en: In 1648, the Kingdom of Lan Xang under Souligna Vongsa entered its golden age — politically stable, legally refined, and culturally flourishing as the most powerful Lao kingdom on mainland Southeast Asia.

ruler.zh: 五世达赖喇嘛洛桑嘉措 / 第悉索南群培（摄政）
ruler.en: 5th Dalai Lama Lobsang Gyatso / Desi Sonam Choephel (regent)

dynasty.zh: 甘丹颇章（格鲁派政教合一政权）

governmentForm.zh: 以达赖喇嘛为最高精神与世俗权威的政教合一体制。世俗行政由第悉（摄政大臣）主持，军事后盾由和硕特蒙古固始汗提供。1642年建立，1648年时尚处于巩固阶段。
governmentForm.en: A theocratic system with the Dalai Lama as the supreme spiritual and temporal authority. Secular administration was headed by the Desi (regent), while military backing was provided by Güshi Khan of the Khoshut Mongols. Established in 1642, it was still in its consolidation phase in 1648.

socialStructure.zh: 西藏社会以寺院僧侣集团、世俗贵族、自由农牧民与农奴为主要阶层。格鲁派三大寺（哲蚌寺、色拉寺、甘丹寺）拥有大量土地与人口。农奴制为基本经济社会关系。
socialStructure.en: Tibetan society was structured around monastic clergy, secular aristocrats, free herders and farmers, and serfs. The three great Gelug monasteries (Drepung, Sera, Ganden) held vast lands and populations. Serfdom was the fundamental socioeconomic relationship.

rulingClass.zh: 达赖喇嘛与格鲁派高僧、第悉与噶厦官员、和硕特蒙古军事贵族、西藏世俗贵族共同构成统治层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum comprised the Dalai Lama and senior Gelug monks, the Desi and Kashag officials, Khoshut Mongol military nobles, and Tibetan secular aristocrats.

succession.zh: 达赖喇嘛通过转世灵童制度继承（非世袭），由高僧团寻找认定。第悉由达赖喇嘛任命。和硕特汗位在固始汗家族内传承。
succession.en: The Dalai Lama was succeeded through the reincarnation (tulku) system rather than heredity, with senior monks searching for and identifying the new incarnation. The Desi was appointed by the Dalai Lama. The Khoshut khanship was inherited within Güshi Khan's family.

structure.zh: 甘丹颇章政府由达赖喇嘛为最高权威、第悉（摄政）主持行政、噶厦（内阁）处理日常政务的三层结构组成。1648年索南群培担任第悉，正着手建设行政体系。和硕特蒙古固始汗作为军事保护者驻守，但日常行政由藏族官员和僧侣主导。
structure.en: The Ganden Phodrang government had a three-tier structure: the Dalai Lama as supreme authority, the Desi (regent) heading administration, and the Kashag (cabinet) handling daily governance. In 1648, Sonam Choephel served as Desi, actively building the administrative system. Güshi Khan of the Khoshut Mongols remained as military protector, but daily administration was led by Tibetan officials and clergy.

function.zh: 处理日常行政事务、财政管理、地方行政督导与外交通信。由四名噶伦（大臣）组成，三俗一僧。
function.en: Handled daily administrative affairs, fiscal management, provincial oversight, and diplomatic correspondence. Composed of four Kalön (ministers), three lay and one monastic.

function.zh: 管理税收征收、国库出纳、寺院田产登记与贡赋核算。
function.en: Managed tax collection, treasury disbursement, monastic land registration, and tribute accounting.

function.zh: 负责对外使节接待、与蒙古诸部和中原政权的通信、宗教外交与翻译事务。
function.en: Handled reception of foreign envoys, correspondence with Mongol polities and Chinese regimes, religious diplomacy, and translation affairs.

function.zh: 管理藏军征召、地方民兵、武器储备与边防事务。实际军力由和硕特蒙古骑兵为核心。
function.en: Managed Tibetan army recruitment, local militias, arms stockpiles, and frontier defense. Actual military core remained the Khoshut Mongol cavalry.

localAdmin.zh: 全藏分为若干宗（县级行政区），由宗本管理。寺院庄园由寺院直接管辖。地方贵族在领地上保有自治权。拉萨以外地区行政覆盖仍在建设中。
localAdmin.en: Tibet was divided into dzong (county-level districts), each managed by a dzongpön. Monastic estates were directly administered by their monasteries. Local nobles retained autonomy in their domains. Administrative coverage outside Lhasa was still being constructed.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以佛教戒律、藏族习惯法与第悉颁布的行政法令为基础。刑罚包括罚金、苦役与流放，死刑极少使用（受佛教戒杀影响）。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Buddhist monastic codes, Tibetan customary law, and administrative decrees issued by the Desi. Punishments included fines, hard labor, and exile, with capital punishment rarely used (influenced by Buddhist prohibitions against killing).

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物（青稞、酥油、羊毛、盐）和劳役为主。寺院、贵族和政府各自向所属农奴征收。货币税收有限，以银元宝与茶砖为主要流通媒介。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was collected mainly in kind (barley, butter, wool, salt) and labor service. Monasteries, nobles, and the government each extracted from their own serfs. Cash taxation was limited, with silver ingots and tea bricks as primary media of exchange.

religion.zh: 藏传佛教格鲁派（兼有宁玛、噶举、萨迦等教派）
religion.en: Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism (with Nyingma, Kagyu, and Sakya schools coexisting)

culturalAchievements.zh: 五世达赖喇嘛是西藏文化的伟大推动者。此时期开始兴建布达拉宫（1645年动工），佛教经典编纂、唐卡绘画、金铜佛造像与藏戏艺术蓬勃发展。拉萨成为藏传佛教世界的绝对中心。
culturalAchievements.en: The 5th Dalai Lama was a great patron of Tibetan culture. The construction of the Potala Palace began in this period (started 1645), and Buddhist scripture compilation, thangka painting, bronze casting, and Tibetan opera all flourished. Lhasa became the undisputed center of the Tibetan Buddhist world.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系藏缅语族藏语
languageFamily.en: Sino-Tibetan, Tibeto-Burman, Tibetan language

philosophy.zh: 格鲁派中观应成与唯识学，五世达赖亦尊重宁玛密续传统
philosophy.en: Gelug Prasangika Madhyamaka and Yogachara, with the 5th Dalai Lama also respecting Nyingma tantric traditions

writingSystem.zh: 藏文（源自古印度梵文字母系统）
writingSystem.en: Tibetan script (derived from the ancient Indian Brahmic writing system)

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 西藏经济以高原畜牧（牦牛、绵羊）与河谷青稞种植为基础，辅以盐湖采盐、羊毛贸易与宗教朝圣经济。与尼泊尔、印度、蒙古和中原的茶马贸易及宗教物品交换是重要收入来源。寺院经济在整体经济中占有极大比重。
gdpDescription.en: The Tibetan economy was based on highland pastoralism (yaks, sheep) and valley barley cultivation, supplemented by salt-lake mining, wool trade, and the religious pilgrimage economy. Tea-horse trade and religious-goods exchange with Nepal, India, Mongolia, and China were important revenue sources. The monastic economy constituted an enormous share of the total economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧（牦牛、绵羊）、青稞种植、盐湖采盐、羊毛加工、宗教器物制作、茶马贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism (yaks, sheep), barley cultivation, salt-lake mining, wool processing, religious artifact production, tea-horse trade

tradeGoods.zh: 羊毛、牦牛制品、盐、麝香、藏药、宗教法器、黄金（砂金）
tradeGoods.en: Wool, yak products, salt, musk, Tibetan medicine, religious implements, gold (placer)

name.zh: 银元宝、茶砖与实物交换并用
name.en: Silver ingots, tea bricks, and barter exchange

householdWealth.zh: 农奴家庭财富极少，主要为寺院和贵族所控制的牲畜与土地。自由牧民以牦牛群规模衡量财富。
householdWealth.en: Serf households possessed very little wealth, with livestock and land primarily controlled by monasteries and nobles. Free herders measured wealth by the size of their yak herds.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以寺院庄园制与贵族领地制为基础的农奴制经济，辅以跨喜马拉雅贸易与朝圣经济。
economicSystem.en: A serfdom-based economy centered on monastic estates and noble landholdings, supplemented by trans-Himalayan trade and pilgrimage economy.

source.zh: 田赋与牧业税（实物折算）

category.zh: 布达拉宫建设与寺院工程
category.en: Potala Palace construction and monastic projects

category.zh: 军队与和硕特蒙古驻军供养
category.en: Military and Khoshut Mongol garrison maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库与寺院财富高度交融，布达拉宫、三大寺均储有大量金银、佛像与宗教珍品。蒙古诸部与信众的功德捐赠是重要财源。
treasuryDescription.en: State and monastic wealth were highly intertwined, with the Potala Palace and three great monasteries holding vast gold, silver, statuary, and religious treasures. Merit donations from Mongol tribes and devotees were an important financial source.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持布达拉宫建设、供养僧团与和硕特蒙古驻军为三大优先，索南群培推行审慎的支出管理。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized Potala Palace construction, monastic support, and Khoshut Mongol garrison maintenance, with Sonam Choephel pursuing prudent expenditure management.

description.zh: 固始汗麾下的蒙古骑兵精锐，是甘丹颇章政权的军事核心与最强战力。
description.en: Elite Mongol cavalry under Güshi Khan, the military core and strongest fighting force of the Ganden Phodrang government.

description.zh: 从各宗征召的藏族步兵，装备刀、矛、弓箭和少量火绳枪。
description.en: Tibetan infantry levied from each dzong, armed with swords, spears, bows, and limited matchlocks.

description.zh: 三大寺中的武装僧侣，尤其哲蚌寺拥有相当的武装力量，在政治危机中发挥军事作用。
description.en: Armed monks from the three great monasteries, particularly Drepung which maintained significant armed forces, playing a military role during political crises.

description.zh: 驻守边境关卡与要道的地方守备力量，负责控制入藏通道。
description.en: Local garrison forces stationed at border checkpoints and key passes, responsible for controlling access routes into Tibet.

commanderInChief.zh: 名义上达赖喇嘛为最高权威，实际军事指挥由固始汗与第悉协调
commanderInChief.en: The Dalai Lama was nominally supreme, with actual military command coordinated between Güshi Khan and the Desi

technology.zh: 军事技术以蒙古骑兵传统为主，藏军装备较为简陋。火器使用有限。高原地形本身构成强大的天然防御屏障。
technology.en: Military technology was primarily based on Mongol cavalry traditions, with Tibetan troops lightly equipped. Firearms use was limited. The highland terrain itself formed a powerful natural defensive barrier.

keyWeapons.zh: 蒙古复合弓、马刀、藏刀、长矛、皮甲、少量火绳枪
keyWeapons.en: Mongol composite bows, sabers, Tibetan knives, spears, leather armor, limited matchlocks

defensiveStructures.zh: 高原地形为最大屏障，各宗设有宗堡（城堡），布达拉宫兼具宫殿与堡垒功能
defensiveStructures.en: Highland terrain as the greatest barrier, each dzong had a fortress (dzong), and the Potala Palace served both as palace and fortress

threats.zh: 内部反格鲁派势力（噶玛噶举、藏巴汗残余）、北方准噶尔的扩张野心、和硕特蒙古保护者与藏族政府之间的潜在权力矛盾。
threats.en: Internal anti-Gelug forces (Karma Kagyu, Tsangpa remnants), northern Dzungar expansionist ambitions, and potential power tensions between the Khoshut Mongol protectors and the Tibetan government.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以藏族为绝对主体，分布于卫藏（拉萨、日喀则地区）、安多和康区。拉萨河谷人口最为密集。和硕特蒙古人作为军事贵族驻扎于此。大量人口为寺院或贵族领地上的农奴。
populationDescription.en: The population was overwhelmingly Tibetan, distributed across Ü-Tsang (Lhasa and Shigatse regions), Amdo, and Kham. The Lhasa valley was the most densely populated area. Khoshut Mongols were stationed as a military aristocracy. A large proportion of the population were serfs on monastic or noble estates.

ethnicGroups.zh: 藏族为绝对主体（卫藏、安多、康巴三大方言区），和硕特蒙古驻军与贵族，少量尼泊尔、印度商人与工匠
ethnicGroups.en: Tibetans as the overwhelming majority (three dialect groups: Ü-Tsang, Amdo, Khampa), Khoshut Mongol garrison and nobles, small numbers of Nepali and Indian merchants and artisans

socialClasses.zh: 达赖喇嘛与高僧、和硕特蒙古军事贵族、世俗贵族、噶厦官员、普通僧侣、自由农牧民、农奴与奴仆。
socialClasses.en: The Dalai Lama and senior monks, Khoshut Mongol military nobles, secular aristocrats, Kashag officials, ordinary monks, free farmers and herders, serfs and slaves.

allies.zh: 与和硕特蒙古固始汗为军事联盟（保护者关系），与准噶尔汗国保持宗教联系，与明清之际的中原政权维持朝贡—宗教外交。
allies.en: Military alliance with Güshi Khan's Khoshut Mongols (patron-protector relationship), religious ties with the Dzungar Khanate, and tributary-religious diplomacy with Chinese regimes during the Ming-Qing transition.

enemies.zh: 内部反对势力（被征服的藏巴汗余部、噶玛噶举派残余），与拉达克存在边境冲突。
enemies.en: Internal opposition (remnants of the conquered Tsangpa Khan and Karma Kagyu), and border conflicts with Ladakh.

foreignPolicy.zh: 五世达赖推行以宗教权威为核心的外交战略：利用与蒙古诸部的檀越关系巩固政权，同时在明清交替中保持灵活立场。
foreignPolicy.en: The 5th Dalai Lama pursued a diplomacy centered on religious authority: leveraging patron-priest relationships with Mongol tribes to consolidate the regime, while maintaining a flexible stance during the Ming-Qing transition.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶青藏高原技术水平

keyInnovations.zh: 布达拉宫建筑工程、藏医学体系、唐卡绘画与金铜佛造像技术、青稞高原农业、高原畜牧与酥油加工。
keyInnovations.en: Potala Palace architectural engineering, Tibetan medical system, thangka painting and bronze-casting techniques, highland barley agriculture, and plateau pastoralism with butter processing.

strengths.zh: 达赖喇嘛的宗教权威覆盖整个藏传佛教世界，和硕特蒙古提供可靠军事保障，高原地形天然防御力强大，朝圣经济与捐赠收入持续稳定。
strengths.en: The Dalai Lama's religious authority spanned the entire Tibetan Buddhist world, Khoshut Mongols provided reliable military protection, highland terrain offered powerful natural defense, and pilgrimage economy and donation income remained steady.

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础薄弱（高原制约）、对和硕特蒙古军事依赖度高、内部教派矛盾未完全消除、行政体系尚在建设之中。
weaknesses.en: Weak economic base (constrained by the plateau), high military dependence on Khoshut Mongols, incompletely resolved sectarian tensions, and an administrative system still under construction.

outlook.zh: 五世达赖喇嘛正在建立一个前所未有的政教合一政权，布达拉宫建设象征着雄心壮志。但甘丹颇章长期前景取决于能否平衡蒙古保护者与藏族官僚之间的权力关系。
outlook.en: The 5th Dalai Lama was building an unprecedented theocratic government, with the Potala Palace construction symbolizing grand ambitions. But the Ganden Phodrang's long-term prospects depended on balancing power between Mongol protectors and the Tibetan bureaucracy.

description.zh: 1648年的甘丹颇章政权在五世达赖喇嘛领导下初步统一西藏、兴建布达拉宫，以宗教权威与蒙古军事力量为双重支柱建立政教合一体制。
description.en: In 1648, the Ganden Phodrang government under the 5th Dalai Lama had just unified Tibet, begun constructing the Potala Palace, and established a theocratic system supported by twin pillars of religious authority and Mongol military power.

governmentForm.zh: 名义独立的王国，实际处于中国（明/清）朝贡体系与日本萨摩藩双重宗主权之下。1609年萨摩入侵后，琉球在保持对华朝贡的同时须向萨摩纳贡服从。
governmentForm.en: A nominally independent kingdom, actually under dual suzerainty from China (Ming/Qing tributary system) and Japan's Satsuma domain. After the 1609 Satsuma invasion, Ryukyu maintained its tributary relationship with China while also submitting to and paying tribute to Satsuma.

socialStructure.zh: 琉球社会由王族、按司（地方贵族）、士族、平民与仆役构成。1609年后萨摩藩控制了贸易利润，但琉球士族仍保有地方行政与文化领导权。
socialStructure.en: Ryukyuan society consisted of the royal family, aji (local nobles), shizoku (gentry), commoners, and servants. After 1609, Satsuma controlled trade profits, but Ryukyuan gentry retained local administrative and cultural leadership.

rulingClass.zh: 第二尚氏王族、按司贵族、三司官（三位高级官员）、士族阶层。萨摩藩驻琉球代官在幕后拥有监督权。
rulingClass.en: The Second Shō royal family, aji nobility, Sanshikan (three senior officials), and gentry class. Satsuma's resident agent in Ryukyu held behind-the-scenes supervisory power.

succession.zh: 王位在第二尚氏家族内传承，但须获得萨摩藩默许与中国册封。
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the Second Shō family, but required tacit Satsuma approval and Chinese investiture.

structure.zh: 琉球中央政府以首里王府为核心，设三司官（三位最高行政官员）主持日常政务。下设评定所等机构处理财政、外交与司法。全国分为三府（中山、山南、山北）管辖各间切。1609年后，萨摩藩设在那霸的代官监督贸易与外交。
structure.en: The Ryukyuan central government centered on the Shuri court, with the Sanshikan (three highest administrative officials) presiding over daily affairs. Subordinate bodies including the Hyōjōsho handled finance, diplomacy, and justice. The country was divided into three regions (Chūzan, Sannan, Sanhoku) governing each magiri. After 1609, Satsuma's resident agent in Naha supervised trade and diplomacy.

function.zh: 主持国政、协调各部事务、审议法令与监督地方行政。
function.en: Presided over state affairs, coordinated departmental business, deliberated on decrees, and supervised local administration.

function.zh: 处理财政预算、税收核算、司法裁决与外贸管理。
function.en: Handled fiscal budgeting, tax accounting, judicial decisions, and foreign trade management.

function.zh: 组织对中国的朝贡使团、管理贡品采购与使团人员、处理册封仪式事务。
function.en: Organized tributary missions to China, managed tribute procurement and mission personnel, and handled investiture ceremonies.

function.zh: 管理那霸港口商船出入、货物登记、关税征收与外国商人接待。
function.en: Managed Naha harbor ship traffic, cargo registration, customs collection, and reception of foreign merchants.

localAdmin.zh: 全国分为间切（村落群），由按司或士族担任地方官。首里王府通过任命制控制地方。萨摩藩对琉球的土地与人口进行了系统调查以便控制。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into magiri (village clusters), with aji or gentry serving as local officials. The Shuri court controlled localities through appointments. Satsuma conducted systematic surveys of Ryukyuan land and population for control purposes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以琉球习惯法与王令为基础，受中国礼法与日本法制双重影响。1609年后萨摩藩对贸易与外交施加了额外法律约束。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Ryukyuan customary law and royal edicts, influenced by both Chinese ritual law and Japanese legal practice. After 1609, Satsuma imposed additional legal constraints on trade and diplomacy.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以稻米、甘蔗与人头税为主。1609年后萨摩藩抽取大量贸易利润并征收附加税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was collected mainly through rice, sugarcane, and head taxes. After 1609, Satsuma extracted substantial trade profits and imposed additional levies.

religion.zh: 琉球本土信仰（御岳崇拜、神女制度）、佛教、儒学
religion.en: Indigenous Ryukyuan religion (utaki worship, noro priestess system), Buddhism, and Confucianism

culturalAchievements.zh: 琉球文化融合了中日东南亚多元影响。首里城建筑、琉球古典音乐与舞踊、�的织物（红型染色）、陶器（�的烧）与漆器工艺享有盛名。17世纪琉球空手道的雏形亦在此时发展。
culturalAchievements.en: Ryukyuan culture blended Chinese, Japanese, and Southeast Asian influences. Shuri Castle architecture, classical Ryukyuan music and dance, bingata-dyed textiles, pottery, and lacquerware were renowned. The precursors of Ryukyuan karate also developed during this period.

languageFamily.zh: 日本语系琉球语群
languageFamily.en: Japonic, Ryukyuan language group

philosophy.zh: 儒学（受中国影响）、佛教与琉球本土灵媒信仰并存
philosophy.en: Confucianism (Chinese influence), Buddhism, and indigenous Ryukyuan spiritual-medium beliefs coexisting

writingSystem.zh: 汉字（官方文书）与平假名（日本影响），琉球语以口述为主
writingSystem.en: Chinese characters (official documents) and hiragana (Japanese influence), with Ryukyuan language primarily oral

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 琉球曾是东亚—东南亚海上贸易的重要中转站，但1609年萨摩入侵后贸易自主权受限。经济以农业（稻米、甘蔗）、渔业和受控贸易为主。中国朝贡贸易仍是最重要的商业活动。
gdpDescription.en: Ryukyu had been an important maritime trade entrepôt between East and Southeast Asia, but trade autonomy was curtailed after the 1609 Satsuma invasion. The economy was based on agriculture (rice, sugarcane), fishing, and regulated trade. Chinese tributary trade remained the most important commercial activity.

mainIndustries.zh: 朝贡转口贸易、稻米与甘蔗种植、渔业、纺织（芭蕉布）、陶瓷、漆器制作
mainIndustries.en: Tributary entrepôt trade, rice and sugarcane cultivation, fishing, textile weaving (bashōfu), ceramics, lacquerware production

tradeGoods.zh: 硫磺、泡盛酒、漆器、芭蕉布、砂糖、中国丝绸与瓷器（转运）、东南亚香料（转运）
tradeGoods.en: Sulfur, awamori liquor, lacquerware, banana-fiber cloth, sugar, Chinese silk and porcelain (transit), Southeast Asian spices (transit)

name.zh: 中国铜钱与日本银币并用
name.en: Chinese copper cash and Japanese silver coins used concurrently

householdWealth.zh: 士族家庭拥有田产、住宅与少量贸易所得；平民多为自耕农，以稻田和甘蔗地为主要财产。
householdWealth.en: Gentry households possessed land, residences, and modest trade income; commoners were mostly smallholding farmers with paddy and sugarcane fields as their main assets.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以朝贡贸易和农业为基础、受萨摩藩经济控制的双重宗主制下的小型岛国经济。
economicSystem.en: A small island economy based on tributary trade and agriculture, operating under dual suzerainty with Satsuma economic control.

category.zh: 首里城维护与公共工程
category.en: Shuri Castle maintenance and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 琉球国库规模有限，大量贸易利润被萨摩藩抽取。首里城内保有王室金银与贡品储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The Ryukyuan treasury was modest in scale, with much trade profit extracted by Satsuma. Shuri Castle held royal gold, silver, and tribute goods reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政在萨摩藩榨取与中国朝贡义务之间艰难平衡，琉球王府力图通过朝贡贸易利润维持基本运转。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy struggled to balance Satsuma extraction and Chinese tributary obligations, with the Ryukyuan court striving to maintain basic operations through tributary trade profits.

description.zh: 驻守首里城与王宫的禁卫部队，规模很小，主要承担礼仪与治安功能。
description.en: A small guard force stationed at Shuri Castle and the royal palace, primarily serving ceremonial and policing functions.

description.zh: 各间切临时征召的民兵，装备简陋，以维持治安和海岸监视为主。1609年后萨摩禁止琉球持有大量武器。
description.en: Militia temporarily levied from each magiri, poorly equipped, mainly for policing and coastal watch. After 1609, Satsuma prohibited Ryukyu from maintaining substantial arsenals.

description.zh: 小型船只组成的沿海巡逻力量，负责监视海盗与管理近海航运。
description.en: A coastal patrol force of small vessels, responsible for monitoring piracy and managing near-shore shipping.

commanderInChief.zh: 三司官之一兼管军事，但军事能力极为有限
commanderInChief.en: One of the Sanshikan oversaw military affairs, but military capacity was extremely limited

technology.zh: 1609年萨摩入侵后琉球武装被大幅裁减，武器储备受到严格限制。民间武术（后发展为空手道）在禁武政策下悄然传承。
technology.en: After the 1609 Satsuma invasion, Ryukyuan armed forces were drastically reduced and weapons stockpiles strictly limited. Civilian martial arts (later developing into karate) were quietly transmitted under the weapons prohibition.

keyWeapons.zh: 刀剑、竹矛（有限）、民间武术器具（棍棒、农具改造）
keyWeapons.en: Swords, bamboo spears (limited), civilian martial arts implements (staffs, converted farming tools)

defensiveStructures.zh: 首里城石城墙与城门，那霸港设施
defensiveStructures.en: Shuri Castle stone walls and gates, Naha harbor facilities

threats.zh: 萨摩藩的持续控制为最大政治威胁。海盗活动与明清易代期间的东海不稳定亦有影响。琉球自身几乎无军事防御能力。
threats.en: Satsuma's continued control was the greatest political threat. Piracy and East China Sea instability during the Ming-Qing transition also posed concerns. Ryukyu itself had virtually no military defense capability.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于冲绳本岛南部，其余分布于先岛诸岛与奄美群岛（1609年后奄美直接归萨摩管辖）。人口以琉球人为主，那霸有少量华人与日本商人社区。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in southern Okinawa Main Island, with the rest distributed across the Sakishima and Amami Islands (Amami came under direct Satsuma control after 1609). The population was predominantly Ryukyuan, with small Chinese and Japanese merchant communities in Naha.

ethnicGroups.zh: 琉球人为绝对主体，那霸有小型华人社区（久米村/唐营）与少量日本商人
ethnicGroups.en: Ryukyuans as the overwhelming majority, with a small Chinese community (Kumemura/Chinese settlement) in Naha and a few Japanese merchants

socialClasses.zh: 王族、按司贵族、士族（秀才阶层）、平民农渔民、奴仆。
socialClasses.en: Royal family, aji nobility, shizoku gentry, commoner farmers and fishermen, and servants.

allies.zh: 名义上为中国（明/清）藩属国，同时受日本萨摩藩控制。琉球在两大强权之间维持双重身份以求生存。
allies.en: Nominally a tributary of China (Ming/Qing), while simultaneously controlled by Japan's Satsuma domain. Ryukyu maintained a dual identity between the two great powers for survival.

enemies.zh: 无明确外敌，但萨摩藩的控制本身即为最大的主权威胁。
enemies.en: No explicit external enemies, but Satsuma's control itself was the greatest threat to sovereignty.

foreignPolicy.zh: 琉球外交的核心是维持中日之间的'两面体制'：对中国隐瞒萨摩控制以维系朝贡贸易，对日本则顺从以避免进一步侵蚀。
foreignPolicy.en: Ryukyuan diplomacy was centered on maintaining a 'dual-face system' between China and Japan: concealing Satsuma control from China to preserve tributary trade, while submitting to Japan to avoid further erosion of autonomy.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶东亚海洋小王国技术水平
era.en: Mid-17th-century East Asian maritime small-kingdom technology

keyInnovations.zh: 琉球造船术（进贡船）、泡盛酿造技术（东南亚蒸馏法）、红型染色工艺、首里城石造建筑、空手道雏形武术。
keyInnovations.en: Ryukyuan shipbuilding (tribute vessels), awamori distillation (Southeast Asian technique), bingata dyeing, Shuri Castle stone architecture, and proto-karate martial arts.

strengths.zh: 独特的中日双重朝贡地位使其保有贸易中转利益，文化融合创造了独特的琉球文明，小型岛国治理效率较高。
strengths.en: The unique dual tributary status with China and Japan preserved trade-transit benefits, cultural blending created a distinctive Ryukyuan civilization, and small-island governance was relatively efficient.

weaknesses.zh: 军事力量几乎为零，完全依赖外部保护，经济被萨摩藩严重榨取，主权名存实亡。
weaknesses.en: Military power was virtually nil, survival depended entirely on external protection, the economy was severely exploited by Satsuma, and sovereignty existed in name only.

outlook.zh: 琉球王国将在中日两强之间继续维持微妙的平衡存在，但随着日本近代化，最终将在1879年被正式并入日本。
outlook.en: The Ryukyu Kingdom would continue its delicate balancing act between China and Japan, but with Japanese modernization, it would ultimately be formally annexed by Japan in 1879.

description.zh: 1648年的琉球王国在尚质王治下维持中日双重朝贡的微妙身份，以首里城为中心保持独特的文化与有限的自治。
description.en: In 1648, the Ryukyu Kingdom under Shō Shitsu maintained its delicate dual-tributary status between China and Japan, preserving a distinctive culture and limited autonomy centered on Shuri Castle.

governmentForm.zh: 以凯拉王朝苏丹为最高统治者的伊斯兰苏丹国。苏莱曼·索隆确立了伊斯兰教为国教，建立了中央集权与地方酋长制相结合的治理体系。
governmentForm.en: An Islamic sultanate with the Keira dynasty sultan as supreme ruler. Sulayman Solong established Islam as the state religion and built a governance system combining central authority with local chieftaincy.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以苏丹王族、伊斯兰学者（富基哈）、地方酋长、自由农牧民与奴隶为主要阶层。伊斯兰教在苏莱曼·索隆时期成为统治精英的核心意识形态，但民间仍保留大量非洲传统习俗。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the sultan's royal family, Islamic scholars (fuqaha), local chiefs, free agro-pastoralists, and slaves. Islam became the core ideology of the ruling elite under Sulayman Solong, though African traditional customs persisted widely among commoners.

rulingClass.zh: 凯拉王族、富尔族贵族、伊斯兰学者与法官、各部族酋长。
rulingClass.en: The Keira royal family, Fur nobility, Islamic scholars and judges, and tribal chiefs.

succession.zh: 苏丹位在凯拉王族内传承，通常由苏丹指定或王族内部协商选出，母系血统在候选人资格中具有重要作用。
succession.en: The sultanship was inherited within the Keira lineage, usually designated by the sultan or chosen through intra-royal negotiation, with maternal lineage playing an important role in candidate eligibility.

structure.zh: 达尔富尔苏丹国以苏丹为最高权威，设有若干宫廷官员（如阿巴·迪马、阿巴·恩加拉等）处理行政、军事与司法事务。全国分为若干地方行政区，由苏丹任命的总督或本地酋长管理。苏莱曼·索隆正在建立国家体制，引入伊斯兰行政规范。
structure.en: The Darfur Sultanate had the sultan as supreme authority, with several court officials (such as Ab Dima, Ab Ngara, etc.) handling administrative, military, and judicial affairs. The country was divided into local administrative districts managed by sultan-appointed governors or local chiefs. Sulayman Solong was building state institutions and introducing Islamic administrative norms.

function.zh: 处理王室事务、接待使节与朝觐者、颁布法令与协调各省事务。
function.en: Handled royal affairs, received envoys and petitioners, promulgated decrees, and coordinated provincial affairs.

function.zh: 依据伊斯兰教法裁决纠纷、管理婚姻与继承事务、监督宗教教育。苏莱曼·索隆时期新设。
function.en: Adjudicated disputes according to Islamic law, managed marriage and inheritance affairs, and supervised religious education. Newly established under Sulayman Solong.

function.zh: 征收各部族贡纳、管理跨撒哈拉贸易关税、登记奴隶与象牙贸易。
function.en: Collected tribal tribute, managed trans-Saharan trade customs, and registered slave and ivory trade.

function.zh: 组织军队征召、管理骑兵与步兵部署、防御邻国入侵与进行扩张战争。
function.en: Organized army levies, managed cavalry and infantry deployment, defended against neighboring invasions, and conducted wars of expansion.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托部族酋长与苏丹任命的省长（沙蒂亚），酋长在本部族内享有自治权。苏莱曼·索隆逐步将伊斯兰法引入地方治理。
localAdmin.en: Local governance relied on tribal chiefs and sultan-appointed provincial governors (shartay), with chiefs retaining autonomy within their tribes. Sulayman Solong gradually introduced Islamic law into local governance.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以伊斯兰教法与达尔富尔本地习惯法并行。苏莱曼·索隆引入卡迪（法官）制度，但传统酋长司法在部落层面仍占主导。
legalSystem.en: Law was a parallel system of Islamic sharia and local Darfurian customary law. Sulayman Solong introduced the qadi (judge) system, but traditional chiefly justice still dominated at the tribal level.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物贡纳（谷物、牲畜、奴隶）为主，辅以跨撒哈拉贸易关税和过路费。伊斯兰天课（扎卡特）开始推行。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was collected mainly as in-kind tribute (grain, livestock, slaves), supplemented by trans-Saharan trade customs and transit tolls. Islamic alms tax (zakat) was beginning to be implemented.

religion.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教（苏莱曼·索隆时期确立为国教，融合非洲传统信仰）
religion.en: Sunni Islam (established as state religion under Sulayman Solong, blended with African traditional beliefs)

culturalAchievements.zh: 苏莱曼·索隆是达尔富尔从非洲传统王权向伊斯兰苏丹国转型的关键人物。此时期兴建清真寺、引入古兰经教育、建立伊斯兰法庭，开创了达尔富尔伊斯兰文化传统。达尔富尔本地的铁器锻造、纺织和土建建筑技术亦有特色。
culturalAchievements.en: Sulayman Solong was the pivotal figure in Darfur's transformation from an African traditional kingdom to an Islamic sultanate. This period saw mosque construction, introduction of Quranic education, establishment of Islamic courts, and the founding of Darfur's Islamic cultural tradition. Local Darfurian ironworking, textile, and earthen architecture also had distinctive character.

languageFamily.zh: 尼罗—撒哈拉语系富尔语
languageFamily.en: Nilo-Saharan, Fur language

philosophy.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教义与达尔富尔本土宇宙观、祖先崇拜传统并存
philosophy.en: Sunni Islamic teaching coexisting with indigenous Darfurian cosmology and ancestor veneration

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯文（宗教与官方文书），富尔语以口述为主
writingSystem.en: Arabic script (religious and official documents), with the Fur language primarily oral

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 达尔富尔经济以粟类种植和畜牧为基础，辅以跨撒哈拉贸易。奴隶、象牙和鸵鸟羽毛是主要出口品。杰贝尔·马拉山区的灌溉农业为核心产粮区。经济整体处于自给自足水平，商品经济有限。
gdpDescription.en: The Darfur economy was based on millet cultivation and livestock herding, supplemented by trans-Saharan trade. Slaves, ivory, and ostrich feathers were major exports. Irrigated agriculture in the Jebel Marra highlands was the core grain-producing area. The economy was largely subsistence-level with limited commodity trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟类种植、畜牧（牛、骆驼、山羊）、跨撒哈拉贸易、铁器锻造、棉布纺织
mainIndustries.en: Millet cultivation, herding (cattle, camels, goats), trans-Saharan trade, ironwork, cotton-cloth weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 奴隶、象牙、鸵鸟羽毛、阿拉伯胶、皮革、牲畜、棉布
tradeGoods.en: Slaves, ivory, ostrich feathers, gum arabic, leather, livestock, cotton cloth

name.zh: 以棉布条和牲畜为主要交换媒介，部分使用奥斯曼铜币
name.en: Cotton strips and livestock as primary media of exchange, with some Ottoman copper coins

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以牲畜（牛、骆驼）数量、谷仓储量和奴隶人数衡量。酋长与贵族拥有大量牲畜和土地，普通农民以自给自足为主。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured by livestock counts (cattle, camels), granary reserves, and number of slaves. Chiefs and nobles held large herds and lands, while ordinary farmers were largely subsistence-level.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部族贡纳和自给农牧为基础、辅以跨撒哈拉长途贸易的前近代苏丹国经济。
economicSystem.en: A pre-modern sultanate economy based on tribal tribute and subsistence agro-pastoralism, supplemented by trans-Saharan long-distance trade.

source.zh: 部族贡纳（谷物、牲畜、奴隶）

treasuryDescription.zh: 苏丹国储备以牲畜群、奴隶、象牙与棉布为主要形式，集中于苏丹宫廷。苏莱曼·索隆时期国库规模有限但在增长中。
treasuryDescription.en: Sultanate reserves were held mainly in livestock herds, slaves, ivory, and cotton cloth, concentrated at the sultan's court. Treasury was limited but growing under Sulayman Solong.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以支撑军事扩张和伊斯兰国家建设为优先，通过扩大贡纳范围与控制贸易路线增加收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military expansion and Islamic state-building, increasing revenue by expanding the tribute base and controlling trade routes.

description.zh: 苏丹直属的骑兵精锐，以骆驼和马匹为主要坐骑，装备矛、刀和皮甲。
description.en: The sultan's elite cavalry, mounted primarily on camels and horses, armed with spears, swords, and leather armor.

description.zh: 各部族战时提供的步兵，装备矛、刀、弓箭和盾牌，战斗力参差不齐。
description.en: Infantry provided by various tribes during wartime, armed with spears, swords, bows, and shields, with uneven fighting quality.

name.zh: 骆驼骑兵（巴加拉游牧战士）

description.zh: 以巴加拉阿拉伯游牧民为核心的骆驼骑兵，擅长沙漠机动和突袭。
description.en: Camel cavalry centered on Baggara Arab nomads, skilled in desert mobility and raiding.

description.zh: 以战俘和购入奴隶组成的苏丹私属武装，忠诚度较高。
description.en: The sultan's private armed force composed of war captives and purchased slaves, with relatively high loyalty.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏莱曼·索隆苏丹亲自统军

technology.zh: 军事技术以冷兵器为主，火器使用极为有限。骆驼骑兵在沙漠与萨赫勒环境中具有较强机动性。防御工事以土石墙和木栅为主。
technology.en: Military technology relied mainly on cold weapons, with very limited firearm use. Camel cavalry had strong mobility in desert and Sahel environments. Defensive works consisted mainly of earthen walls and timber palisades.

keyWeapons.zh: 长矛、阔刀、弓箭、盾牌、皮甲、骆驼
keyWeapons.en: Spears, broadswords, bows, shields, leather armor, camels

defensiveStructures.zh: 法希尔土墙城寨，各部族聚落的木栅与土围
defensiveStructures.en: Al-Fashir earthen-walled compound, timber palisades and earthen enclosures at tribal settlements

threats.zh: 东方的丰吉苏丹国（森纳尔）是主要竞争对手，南方非伊斯兰部族可能反抗，北方是的利比亚沙漠游牧势力。
threats.en: The Funj Sultanate (Sennar) to the east was the main rival, southern non-Islamic tribes might resist, and Libyan desert nomad forces lay to the north.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以富尔族为核心，兼有巴加拉阿拉伯游牧民、马萨利特人、扎加瓦人等多个族群。杰贝尔·马拉山区人口最为密集。苏莱曼·索隆时期伊斯兰化进程加速了人口与文化的整合。
populationDescription.en: The population was centered on the Fur people, alongside Baggara Arab nomads, Masalit, Zaghawa, and other groups. The Jebel Marra highland area was the most densely populated. Islamization under Sulayman Solong accelerated population and cultural integration.

ethnicGroups.zh: 富尔族为核心统治民族，巴加拉阿拉伯游牧民、马萨利特人、扎加瓦人、通加尔人等多族群并存
ethnicGroups.en: Fur as the core ruling ethnic group, with Baggara Arabs, Masalit, Zaghawa, Tungur, and other groups coexisting

socialClasses.zh: 苏丹王族与凯拉贵族、伊斯兰学者与法官、部族酋长、自由农牧民、工匠与商人、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Sultan's royal family and Keira nobility, Islamic scholars and judges, tribal chiefs, free agro-pastoralists, artisans and merchants, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与北非（利比亚、埃及）穆斯林商人保持贸易关系，与部分萨赫勒地区伊斯兰政权有宗教联系。
allies.en: Maintained trade relations with North African (Libyan, Egyptian) Muslim merchants, and had religious ties with certain Sahelian Islamic polities.

enemies.zh: 丰吉苏丹国（森纳尔）是东方主要竞争对手，南方非伊斯兰部族时有冲突。
enemies.en: The Funj Sultanate (Sennar) was the main eastern rival, with periodic conflicts with non-Islamic tribes to the south.

foreignPolicy.zh: 苏莱曼·索隆推行伊斯兰化与领土扩张并举的政策，通过宗教传播巩固统治、通过军事征服扩大疆域。
foreignPolicy.en: Sulayman Solong pursued a dual policy of Islamization and territorial expansion, using religious spread to consolidate rule and military conquest to expand territory.

era.zh: 17世纪中叶撒哈拉以南非洲苏丹国技术水平

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造、棉布纺织、灌溉农业（杰贝尔·马拉梯田）、骆驼饲养与沙漠运输、阿拉伯文书写引入。
keyInnovations.en: Ironworking, cotton-cloth weaving, irrigated agriculture (Jebel Marra terraces), camel husbandry and desert transport, and introduction of Arabic literacy.

strengths.zh: 苏莱曼·索隆的伊斯兰化改革为国家提供了新的意识形态凝聚力，跨撒哈拉贸易带来收入，骆驼骑兵适应沙漠环境，杰贝尔·马拉山区提供农业基础。
strengths.en: Sulayman Solong's Islamic reforms provided new ideological cohesion, trans-Saharan trade generated revenue, camel cavalry adapted well to the desert environment, and Jebel Marra highlands provided an agricultural base.

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础薄弱且高度依赖奴隶贸易，识字率极低，军事技术落后，部族整合尚未完成。
weaknesses.en: Weak economic base heavily dependent on the slave trade, extremely low literacy, backward military technology, and incomplete tribal integration.

outlook.zh: 苏莱曼·索隆正在奠定达尔富尔苏丹国三百年统治的基础。伊斯兰化与国家建设将继续推进，但经济发展受制于地理环境与技术水平。
outlook.en: Sulayman Solong was laying the foundations for three centuries of Darfur Sultanate rule. Islamization and state-building would continue, but economic development was constrained by geography and technology.

description.zh: 1648年的达尔富尔苏丹国在苏莱曼·索隆苏丹领导下正从非洲传统王权向伊斯兰苏丹国转型，推行伊斯兰化并积极向周边扩张。
description.en: In 1648, the Darfur Sultanate under Sultan Sulayman Solong was transforming from an African traditional kingdom into an Islamic sultanate, implementing Islamization and actively expanding into surrounding territories.

name.zh: 毛利人（奥特亚罗瓦/新西兰）

ruler.zh: 各部落联盟阿里基（最高酋长）

dynasty.zh: 各部落（伊维）独立的酋长世系
dynasty.en: Independent chiefly lineages within each iwi (tribe)

capital.zh: 无统一首都（各伊维有各自的主要帕堡垒）
capital.en: No unified capital (each iwi had its own main pā fortification)

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭酋长制为核心的部落联盟社会，各伊维和哈普（亚部落）由阿里基和朗加蒂拉（次级酋长）统治。
governmentForm.en: A tribal confederation society centered on hereditary chieftainship, with each iwi and hapū (subtribe) ruled by ariki and rangatira (subordinate chiefs).

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为阿里基（最高酋长）、朗加蒂拉（酋长/贵族）、图图阿（自由民）和塔乌雷卡雷卡（奴隶/战俘）。血统、马纳（威望）和战功决定社会地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into ariki (paramount chiefs), rangatira (chiefs/nobility), tutua (commoners), and taurēkareka (slaves/war captives). Ancestry, mana (prestige), and martial accomplishment determined social rank.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭酋长阶层，权威来自祖先血统、马纳和战功。朗加蒂拉在哈普层面行使实际管理权。
rulingClass.en: Hereditary chiefly class, with authority derived from ancestral lineage, mana, and martial accomplishment. Rangatira exercised practical governance at the hapū level.

succession.zh: 以长子世袭为主，但能力和马纳也影响继承。部落会议（惠）在重大事务上进行集体讨论。
succession.en: Primarily primogeniture, but ability and mana also influenced succession. Tribal assemblies (hui) held collective discussions on major matters.

structure.zh: 各伊维和哈普独立治理，阿里基通过马纳和塔布（禁忌）权威管理社区。帕堡垒是军事和行政中心。
structure.en: Each iwi and hapū governed independently, with ariki managing communities through mana and tapu (taboo) authority. Pā fortifications served as military and administrative centers.

function.zh: 负责宗教仪式、医疗、航海、建筑和纹身等专业领域。
function.en: Responsible for religious ceremonies, medicine, navigation, construction, and tattooing.

function.zh: 在惠（部落会议）上讨论战争与和平事务，协调各哈普军事行动。
function.en: Discussed war and peace at hui (tribal assemblies) and coordinated military actions among hapū.

localAdmin.zh: 哈普层面由朗加蒂拉管理日常事务，包括土地分配、资源使用和纠纷调解。
localAdmin.en: At the hapū level, rangatira managed daily affairs including land allocation, resource use, and dispute resolution.

legalSystem.zh: 以蒂卡纳（习俗法）为核心，强调乌图（互惠/报复）原则和塔布制度。违反塔布可能导致严厉惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Centered on tikanga (customary law), emphasizing the principle of utu (reciprocity/retribution) and the tapu system. Violations of tapu could lead to severe punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 以贡品和劳役为主，哈普成员向朗加蒂拉提供食物、工艺品和战争服务。
taxationSystem.en: Based on tribute and labor, with hapū members providing food, crafts, and military service to rangatira.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教（毛利传统信仰）
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism (Māori traditional beliefs)

culturalAchievements.zh: 发展出独特的帕堡垒防御建筑、精美的木雕（瓦卡鲁努）和骨雕传统、塔莫科（面部纹身）艺术、哈卡战舞、瓦卡独木舟建造技术。库马拉（甘薯）栽培和蕨根采集支撑了稳定的食物供给。
culturalAchievements.en: Developed distinctive pā fortification architecture, exquisite wood-carving (whakairo) and bone-carving traditions, tā moko (facial tattooing) art, haka war dance, and waka canoe construction. Kūmara (sweet potato) cultivation and fernroot gathering provided stable food supply.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系，波利尼西亚语支（毛利语/蒂雷奥毛利）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch (Te reo Māori)

philosophy.zh: 强调万加努伊坦加（血缘联系）、马纳、塔布和乌图原则。人与自然的关系通过凯蒂亚基坦加（守护/管理）概念维护。
philosophy.en: Emphasized whanaungatanga (kinship connections), mana, tapu, and the principle of utu. The relationship between humans and nature was maintained through the concept of kaitiakitanga (guardianship/stewardship).

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，通过口述传统、吟唱（卡拉基亚）和谱系记忆保存知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge preserved through oral tradition, chanting (karakia), and genealogical memory.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以库马拉（甘薯）栽培、蕨根采集、渔业（尤其是鳗鱼和贝类）和猎鸟（尤其是莫阿鸟残余和基维鸟）为基础。翡翠（绿石）是极为珍贵的贸易和仪式材料。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on kūmara (sweet potato) cultivation, fernroot gathering, fishing (especially eels and shellfish), and bird-hunting. Pounamu (greenstone/jade) was an extremely valuable trade and ceremonial material.

mainIndustries.zh: 库马拉栽培、渔业、翡翠加工、木雕、独木舟建造、亚麻编织
mainIndustries.en: Kūmara cultivation, fishing, pounamu (greenstone) working, wood carving, canoe building, flax weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 翡翠（绿石）、黑曜石、渔产、亚麻织品、木雕制品、鸟羽
tradeGoods.en: Pounamu (greenstone), obsidian, fish products, flax textiles, wood carvings, bird feathers

householdWealth.zh: 以翡翠制品、武器、独木舟、储存食物和与酋长的关系衡量。
householdWealth.en: Measured by pounamu artifacts, weapons, canoes, stored food, and ties to chiefs.

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配和哈普集体生产为核心的部落园圃-渔猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal horticultural-fishing-hunting economy centered on chiefly redistribution and hapū collective production.

treasuryDescription.zh: 酋长储备以翡翠制品、武器、食物储存和帕堡垒中的仪式器物为主。
treasuryDescription.en: Chiefly reserves consisted mainly of pounamu artifacts, weapons, food stores, and ceremonial objects kept in pā.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 通过贡赋再分配巩固酋长权威，支持帕堡垒建设和战争准备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Reinforced chiefly authority through tributary redistribution, supporting pā construction and war preparation.

description.zh: 各哈普征召的成年男性战士，装备泰哈（长矛）、梅雷（短棍）和塔伊哈（长柄武器），善于帕堡垒攻防战和奇袭。
description.en: Adult male warriors levied from each hapū, armed with taiaha (long spears), mere (short clubs), and taiaha (long-handled weapons), skilled in pā siege warfare and surprise attacks.

description.zh: 利用大型战争独木舟（瓦卡陶阿）进行沿海机动和河流突袭。
description.en: Used large war canoes (waka taua) for coastal mobility and river raids.

commanderInChief.zh: 各伊维的阿里基或战争领袖分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: Each iwi's ariki or war leader commanded independently

technology.zh: 泰哈（长矛/长柄武器）、梅雷（短棍/翡翠棍）、帕堡垒防御工事、瓦卡战争独木舟。帕堡垒采用复杂的壕沟、木栅和平台防御体系。
technology.en: Taiaha (long spears/polearms), mere (short clubs/pounamu clubs), pā fortification defenses, waka war canoes. Pā employed complex systems of ditches, palisades, and fighting platforms.

threats.zh: 部落间的领土争端和乌图报复性战争是持续性威胁。1642年塔斯曼曾短暂接触但未再返回。
threats.en: Inter-tribal territorial disputes and utu retaliatory warfare were constant threats. Tasman had made brief contact in 1642 but did not return.

populationDescription.zh: 约10万毛利人分布于新西兰南北两岛，北岛人口较为集中，南岛以翡翠产区为主要聚居区。
populationDescription.en: About 100,000 Māori distributed across New Zealand's North and South Islands, with the North Island more densely populated and the South Island's main settlements near pounamu (greenstone) sources.

name.en: Major pā complexes of the North Island

socialClasses.zh: 阿里基（最高酋长）、朗加蒂拉（酋长/贵族）、图图阿（自由民）、塔乌雷卡雷卡（奴隶/战俘）
socialClasses.en: Ariki (paramount chiefs), rangatira (chiefs/nobility), tutua (commoners), taurēkareka (slaves/war captives)

allies.zh: 伊维间通过联姻和贸易（尤其是翡翠贸易）维持关系。
allies.en: Iwi maintained relations through intermarriage and trade (especially pounamu trade).

enemies.zh: 部落间因土地、资源和乌图（报复）引发频繁战争。
enemies.en: Tribes frequently warred over land, resources, and utu (retribution).

foreignPolicy.zh: 无统一外交政策，各伊维独立处理与其他部落的关系。1642年塔斯曼到达后欧洲接触尚未对毛利社会产生实质影响。
foreignPolicy.en: No unified foreign policy; each iwi independently managed relations with other tribes. European contact since Tasman's arrival in 1642 had not yet substantially affected Māori society.

era.zh: 毛利古典时期

keyInnovations.zh: 帕堡垒防御工事、翡翠加工技术、瓦卡独木舟建造、库马拉储存地窖、亚麻编织技术
keyInnovations.en: Pā fortification engineering, pounamu (greenstone) working, waka canoe construction, kūmara storage pits, flax weaving technology

strengths.zh: 精密的帕堡垒防御体系、高度发达的木雕和翡翠加工艺术、强大的战士传统、成功适应温带环境的波利尼西亚文化。
strengths.en: Sophisticated pā fortification systems, highly developed wood-carving and pounamu art, strong warrior tradition, and Polynesian culture successfully adapted to temperate environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散、无文字和金属冶炼技术、部落间持续战争消耗资源。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, no writing or metal smelting, and continuous inter-tribal warfare draining resources.

outlook.zh: 毛利文化正处于古典时期的繁荣阶段，帕堡垒战争和社会组织日趋复杂。但18世纪末欧洲人的大规模到来将深刻改变毛利社会。
outlook.en: Māori culture was in a flourishing classical period, with pā warfare and social organization growing more complex. However, the large-scale arrival of Europeans in the late 18th century would profoundly transform Māori society.

description.zh: 1648年的毛利人正处于古典时期的繁荣阶段，各伊维和哈普以帕堡垒为中心建立起复杂的部落社会，木雕、翡翠加工和战舞等文化传统高度发达。
description.en: In 1648, the Māori were in a flourishing classical period, with iwi and hapū building complex tribal societies centered on pā fortifications, and highly developed cultural traditions including wood carving, pounamu working, and war dance.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎营地。
governmentForm.en: Mobile camps.

socialStructure.zh: 平等。
socialStructure.en: Egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 毛皮贸易开始影响传统文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Fur trade beginning to influence traditional culture.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡/阿尔冈昆
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan/Algonquian

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 驯鹿狩猎，毛皮贸易渐增。
gdpDescription.en: Caribou hunting, growing fur trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、毛皮贸易
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fur trade

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮（已有欧洲需求）
tradeGoods.en: Furs (European demand growing)

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟和毛皮。
householdWealth.en: Canoes and furs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 欧洲毛皮商的影响、疫病传入。
threats.en: European fur trader influence, disease introduction.

populationDescription.zh: 加拿大亚寒带森林。
populationDescription.en: Canadian boreal forest.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 开始获得铁器和火器。
keyInnovations.en: Beginning to obtain iron tools and firearms.

strengths.zh: 森林生存能力强。
strengths.en: Strong forest survival skills.

weaknesses.zh: 日益依赖欧洲贸易品。
weaknesses.en: Growing dependence on European trade goods.

outlook.zh: 毛皮贸易将深刻改变生活方式。
outlook.en: Fur trade would profoundly change way of life.

description.zh: 1648年亚北极猎人，毛皮贸易开始影响传统社会。
description.en: Subarctic hunters in 1648, fur trade beginning to reshape traditional society.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者
北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.zh: 世袭首领社会。
governmentForm.en: Hereditary leader societies.

socialStructure.zh: 等级分明。
socialStructure.en: Stratified.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 夸富宴和图腾柱延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Potlatch and totem poles continued.

languageFamily.zh: 萨利希/瓦卡什
languageFamily.en: Salishan/Wakashan

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

tradeGoods.zh: 鲑鱼干、雪松

weaknesses.zh: 人口因疫病和奴役大减。
weaknesses.en: Population greatly reduced by disease and enslavement.

outlook.zh: 传教区将在18世纪被驱逐。
outlook.en: Missions would be expelled in the 18th century.

description.zh: 1648年瓜拉尼-图皮诸部，许多生活在耶稣会传教区中。
description.en: Guaraní-Tupí peoples in 1648, many living in Jesuit missions.

governmentForm.zh: 部族联盟和分散村社。
governmentForm.en: Tribal confederacies and scattered villages.

socialStructure.zh: 氏族和议事会，但殖民压力增大。
socialStructure.en: Clans and councils, but colonial pressure growing.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 易洛魁联盟成熟。
culturalAchievements.en: Mature Iroquois Confederacy.

languageFamily.zh: 阿尔冈昆/易洛魁/苏
languageFamily.en: Algonquian/Iroquoian/Siouan

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 玉米农业和毛皮贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Maize agriculture and fur trade.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、毛皮贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, fur trade

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、玉米、烟草
tradeGoods.en: Furs, maize, tobacco

householdWealth.zh: 贝壳串珠和毛皮。
householdWealth.en: Wampum and furs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 殖民扩张、疫病、部族间代理战争。
threats.en: Colonial expansion, disease, proxy intertribal wars.

populationDescription.zh: 北美东部（人口因疫病减少）。
populationDescription.en: Eastern North America (population reduced by disease).

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 火器获取、毛皮贸易适应。
keyInnovations.en: Firearm acquisition, fur trade adaptation.

strengths.zh: 联盟外交成熟。
strengths.en: Mature alliance diplomacy.

weaknesses.zh: 人口锐减，依赖欧洲物资。
weaknesses.en: Population collapsed, dependent on European goods.

outlook.zh: 殖民压力持续增大。
outlook.en: Colonial pressure would continue growing.

description.zh: 1648年北美东部林地民族，面临殖民时代全面压力。
description.en: Eastern woodland peoples in 1648, facing full colonial-era pressures.

governmentForm.zh: 海猎营地。
governmentForm.en: Marine hunting camps.

socialStructure.zh: 平等。
socialStructure.en: Egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 因纽特文化适应小冰期。
culturalAchievements.en: Inuit culture adapted to Little Ice Age.

languageFamily.zh: 爱斯基摩-阿留申
languageFamily.en: Eskimo-Aleut

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 海猎和捕鱼。
gdpDescription.en: Marine hunting and fishing.

mainIndustries.zh: 海猎、捕鱼
mainIndustries.en: Marine hunting, fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 海象牙、毛皮
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, furs

householdWealth.zh: 猎具和储备。
householdWealth.en: Gear and stores.

threats.en: Little Ice Age climate stress.

populationDescription.zh: 北极海岸。
populationDescription.en: Arctic coast.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 寒期适应技术。
keyInnovations.en: Cold-period adaptations.

strengths.zh: 适应力。
strengths.en: Adaptive.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少。
weaknesses.en: Small.

outlook.zh: 持续适应。
outlook.en: Ongoing adaptation.

description.zh: 1648年北极因纽特猎人。
description.en: Arctic Inuit hunters in 1648.

governmentForm.zh: 以英国为盟友的中美洲海岸王国，统治米斯基托海岸。
governmentForm.en: A Central American coastal kingdom allied with Britain, ruling the Miskito Coast.

socialStructure.zh: 王室、战士、渔民和附属部族组成的等级社会。
socialStructure.en: Hierarchical society of royalty, warriors, fishers, and tributary tribes.

rulingClass.zh: 米斯基托王室与战士首领
rulingClass.en: Miskito royalty and warrior leaders

succession.zh: 王位世袭，但受英国影响。
succession.en: Hereditary throne, influenced by Britain.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰和莫拉维亚传教影响
religion.en: Animism with Moravian missionary influence

culturalAchievements.zh: 独特的混合文化，融合土著、非裔和英国元素。
culturalAchievements.en: Unique hybrid culture blending indigenous, African, and British elements.

languageFamily.zh: 米斯基托语（混合语）
languageFamily.en: Miskito (mixed language)

philosophy.zh: 战士荣誉和英国盟友关系。
philosophy.en: Warrior honor and British alliance.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以渔业、海龟捕捞和与英国的贸易为基础。
gdpDescription.en: Based on fishing, turtle hunting, and trade with Britain.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、海龟捕捞、贸易
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, turtle hunting, trade

tradeGoods.zh: 海龟壳、木材、染料
tradeGoods.en: Turtle shell, timber, dyes

householdWealth.zh: 英国贸易品和渔获。
householdWealth.en: British trade goods and catch.

grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.
